Name: The Vinum Xchange
Review_Text:
First time visiting, it's hard to imagine coming to Kuala Terengganu for western food but for dinner we decided to come by this shop due to the rave reviews. It's not a surprise given how once we walked through the door we felt a chill bar restaurant vibes with an impressive wall shelf display of various alcohols.

Aside from the ambience, the place is well ventilated and clean with typical yellow light bar lighting with the staff being able to clearly communicate in English which I find rare in the outer skirts of Malaysia.

I have not even talked about the food yet so here we go. For the price, the cheese baked Bolognese spaghetti and lamb shoulder are the best I have tried yet, fantastic!

The spaghetti balancing of heavy and light has always been a hard spot to find, eluding even the most decent restaurants I have tried in the past where it is too heavy or too extreme with the heavy cheese and light tomato sauce vice versa. This restaurant on its first bite blew it away, there's nothing more I can say about this dish other than try it asap if you are in the area!

Moving on to the Lamb Shoulder, it's definitely not the best but for the price of rm30 it's the best lamb shoulder I have tried that is well cooked and seasoned. We dont even need the sauce to enjoy the taste of it which is rare to find. However the sauces for this dish definitely needs improvement as they were pretty unsatisfactory.The other dishes are pretty meh tbh but this restaurant has definitely hit the park with the two dishes I mentioned.

Oh right, the drinks were all great as well. We ordered watermelon mojito and peach frenzy which were a great sweet treat to balance the heavy flavours from the other dishes.

Overall, definitely a must visit if you are in Kuala Terengganu but definitely be ready for a higher than average price.

Thank you for reading my review! 😊

Name: Warung Pok Nong (Celup Tepung)
Review_Text:
Warung Pok Nong (Celup Tepung) – Total Disaster

With over 4,000 Google reviews averaging 4.3 stars, I thought this place would be a must-try. Boy, was I wrong.

The System (5/5): To be fair, the queuing system is impressive. You first queue for a number. An old chap sizes up your group and allocates a suitable table. When your number is called, you queue for drinks. Once you’ve got those in hand, you queue again for food—only when your number comes up. Sounds tedious, but it’s actually orderly.

The Food (2/5):
•	Fish ICT (ikan celup tepung, RM6 each): Zero seasoning. Just gutted fish dipped in flour and deep-fried. Crispy, yes. Flavor? Nonexistent.
•	Cuttlefish (RM17): I came expecting the glorious whole squid from Mark Wiens’ YouTube video. Instead, got sad, chopped-up pieces.
•	Keropok Lekor (RM2 for 3 pcs): Supposedly a KT specialty. Here, it’s fried in the same bland flour batter. Forgettable.
•	Cuttlefish Eggs (RM8): The only saving grace. Clean, creamy, no fishy taste. Decent portion too.

The Rest:
•	Chili sauce: Watery and bland. Calling it a “dip” is generous.
•	Environment: Hot, no AC. Located by the seaside, which sounds nice until you’re sweating while eating. View is good though.
•	Parking: A nightmare. Brace yourself.

Verdict: Portions are okay, prices are reasonable, seafood is fresh—but freshness alone can’t save tasteless cooking. Service? What service? You’ll likely spend 1-1.5 hours here. Try it yourself if you must, but don’t say I didn’t warn you.

I ordered the Chicken Chop + Fried Rice combo, and it was tasty! The gravy was perfect—not too sweet, not too thick. However, the rice was a bit sticky and clumpy, so there's room for improvement there. Overall, it's a great experience and definitely a 5 out of 5!

Name: Nasi Dagang Atas Tol - Kg Atas Tol
Review_Text: Enjoyed a hearty meal at Nasi Dagang Atas Tol! The food was full of authentic East Coast flavors the nasi dagang, ayam goreng berempah, beef rendang, and gulai ikan tongkol were all tasty and well prepared. The portions were generous, and the acar added a nice refreshing touch. The green frothy drink was also delicious and complemented the meal perfectly. The gulai ikan tongkol would be even better if it were a bit less sweet, especially for visitors from other states who might prefer a less sweet version. Overall, a clean place with fast service definitely worth a visit for anyone craving traditional nasi dagang!

Staff/Service: The system here is self-service—you queue, place your order, and pay before receiving a table number. Service was fast and efficient.

Overall: A good spot for a straightforward breakfast experience with tasty food and quick service.

Review_Text:
The restaurant is spacious, clean, and comfortable. Parking is easy to find. The food is quite tasty and decent. The service? Super fast!! I was surprised the food arrived in less than 15 minutes! But also maybe sebab tak ramai mana orang masa ni, but hope dapat kekalkan servis laju macam tu.

However, there is one thing – the siakap 3 rasa was way too spicy 😩✋🏻 It felt like i ordered siakap pedas rasa, instead of siakap 3 rasa??! Maybe i should ask about the spice level sebelum order kut. The tomyam nam khon was delicious tho!!

The total for everything in the picture, including drinks for three and nasi goreng udang = RM157. Not bad, considering we also ordered sotong, and the price matches the quality of the food and the fast service. Definitely worth paying for! They also have a small booth selling coffee/soda, which looked interesting, but we did not get to try it because we were way too full!

I might come again, and harap servis kekal sama and makanan pun still sedap.

Name: D' Tunggal Seafood
Review_Text:
One of the recommended seafood places to try for dinner. Went at 6pm, plenty of spaces available. Parking is just outside the restaurant in an empty lot.
Service was fast and efficient.
Ordered ikan tiga rasa, sotong telur masin and tom yam pekat.
Asked for a small fish but was given a large one. Fish was very fresh, fried to crispiness. The sauce was very spicy, do take note. Prepare for a fiery toilet the next day.
Sotong portion was huge, basically the whole sotong cut and given to us. But not tasty, no taste of salted egg, avoid ordering this. Very plain, basicaly eating plain sotong.
Tom yam pekat was super delicious, creamy and thick, not super spicy, just the right sour taste to it with plenty of seafood inside.
Price of rm 116 for 3 dishes, but from the huge portions given it was a rather fair price.

Name: The Syrup Vs Soda
Review_Text:
Crowded but quickly attended, with friendly service throughout. Prices may look on the higher side, but the portions are really hearty.

The chicken chop was average, decent but nothing outstanding.
The real highlight was the Nasi Goreng Kambing Special – although meant for 2 pax, it can easily feed 3 (you may ask for packaging container to tapao the unfinished portion in case you fail the challenge). The lamb was meaty with a slightly chewy bite, carrying a sweet undertone. The rice itself was nicely balanced, not overseasoned and not too dry. Be warned – it’s so satisfying you might overeat even when you’re full!

The kuey teow goreng was alright, again served in a very big portion. Do ask to omit the chili if you prefer it less spicy.

Overall, hearty food with generous servings that make it worth a try.

Parking could be a challenge but doable if park further. Do observe their business hours.

Definitely recommended to visit. Portions are generous and the price is worthwhile. 150rm including sodas.

Name: Suerasa Nasi Kerabu
Review_Text:
⸻

Sedap, sedap, sedap!

I really enjoyed the nasi kerabu here — so many lauk options to choose from! I had mine with daging bakar and daging goreng, while my husband and kids went for the lamb and chicken. We also ordered teh buih, which was nice and sweet — a little too sweet for me, but hey, it’s cheat day, so no complaints!

The price was very reasonable — all our nasi kerabu meals came to just RM69. Totally worth it.

Parking can be a bit tricky though. Roadside parking is limited, but you can park across the street. Just remember to pay the parking fee using the Flexi Parking app.

Seating inside the restaurant is also quite limited, so be prepared to wait a little, especially during peak hours. But trust me, it’s worth the patience!

Plus to make it even worth it. We Met UAI. Alhamdulillah.

Name: Suerasa Nasi Kerabu
Review_Text:
I tried the Nasi Kerabu with Kambing Bakar at this shop recently, and honestly, it was one of the best I’ve had in a long time. The moment the food was served, I could already smell the nice smoky aroma from the grilled lamb super appetising!

The Nasi Kerabu itself was fresh and flavourful. The rice had that beautiful blue colour and a nice fragrance, and the mix of herbs and vegetables gave it a refreshing crunch. What I really liked was that everything tasted freshly prepared from the ulam to the sambal and the budu sauce on the side. You can tell they put real effort into keeping the traditional Kelantanese taste.

The star of the meal, of course, was the Kambing Bakar. It was well-marinated, juicy, and grilled perfectly — slightly charred on the outside but still tender inside. The portion was generous too, and it paired so well with the rice and sambal. I also appreciated that the meat wasn’t too fatty or chewy, which shows good quality and proper cooking.

The service here was friendly and fast, and the staff made sure the food came warm and fresh. The shop’s atmosphere was clean and comfortable, suitable for both quick lunch or dinner with friends.

Overall, this place really deserves a shoutout. The Nasi Kerabu with Kambing Bakar is flavorful, authentic, and worth every ringgit. Definitely one of those spots I’ll come back to again and recommend to others who enjoy good Malaysian food done right. The only drawback is the shop is hot.

Name: Restoran Asia Signature
Review_Text:
Sincere feedback from me as a chef—your tom yum tastes bitter and lacks depth. Let me share a few tips.

The issue likely comes from how you treat the tom yum paste. It’s already been sautéed during production, so boiling it for too long will make it bitter.

Here’s what you can do:

• Use a direct cooking method.
• Don’t mix the paste into your clear fish stock from the start.
• Instead, bring your stock to a boil first, then add the paste and simmer briefly.

This way, your tom yum will taste fresher and more balanced.

Ala carte include meat, sauce and side dishes while set comes with all the ala carte mentioned, rice and refillable green tea. Their kimchi was superb! Sadly, side dishes can't be refilled. I love their chicken set and black angus cube! The chicken was juicy and tender! Their black angus was flavorful and juicy! However their services was quite slow since i ordered at 6.40pm and there was no other customer waiting for foods before me. I got my full orders around 7.15pm. Hope they can improve the service! Nevertheless i love the foods here and it worth the money

Name: Seoul Garden Buffet
Review_Text:
I recently visited Seoul Garden Buffet, and it was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The restaurant offers an impressive variety of meats, seafood, vegetables, and sauces—perfect for both Korean BBQ and hotpot lovers. The ingredients were fresh, and the buffet was well-stocked and clean throughout my visit.

What really stood out was the staff's excellent service. They were attentive, friendly, and efficient. Plates were cleared quickly, the grill was changed often without having to ask, and they checked in regularly to make sure we had everything we needed. Their professionalism and warm attitude made the meal even more enjoyable.

Overall, Seoul Garden Buffet delivers great value, delicious food, and outstanding service. I’d highly recommend it for anyone looking for a satisfying and fun dining experience.

The buffet spread is really impressive with lots of fresh meats, seafood, vegetables, and sauces for both BBQ and hotpot. Everything tasted fresh and delicious, and there was always something new to try.

Review_Text:
B Beteng riverside cafe is fantastic. It is located in the Taman tamadun islam park Kuala terengganu.
Situated by the beautiful serene Terengganu river. Its an outdoor cafe.
The food was tasty. The service was good. The waiter was smilling all the way.
I ordered mee tom yum. Oh my god...it was super delicious. I guess mee tom yum always tasted better in the east coast state esp kota bharu and kuala terengganu. So it is a MUST try.
While my wife ordered nasi goreng seafood. It was delicious as well.
The delicious food, the amazinh view and the soft river breeze just add so much to the already fantastic value for our memorable lunch here. The place was quiet at that time so we had all of the best element just to ourselves.
You have to order your meal at the cashier counter n then pick ur drinks. And then they will deliver ur food. The price is reasonable too.
Alhamdulillah. I highly reccomend this riverside cafe to u. 5 star.👍🤗

Name: Coffee Garden (Paya Bunga Square Utc)
Review_Text:
- The friendly waitress made the experience even better—all smiles, and the food arrived quickly in less than 10 minutes!
- You must try their signature nasi lemak with fried chicken for just RM 9. The chicken is perfectly crispy on the outside and juicy inside, paired with their unique pandan green rice. The portion size is just right, leaving you satisfied without feeling bloated.
- The classic design plate adds a nostalgic touch, and I'd rate both the taste and presentation a solid 10/10.
- Don't miss their premium JAFTEA Earl Grey black tea for RM 7—it’s perfectly balanced, neither too sweet nor too bitter.
- The shop itself has a cozy atmosphere that’s perfect for a relaxing meal. You can choose to sit inside for more privacy or outside to enjoy people-watching as they pass by.
- Next on my list is their crispy chicken chop rice—I can’t wait to try it!
✨ The restaurant offers a nice perk: write a review, and you'll receive a complimentary iced lemon tea. 🍋🍵

Name: Mei Fong Curry Noodles
Review_Text:
The shop's operating hours listed on Google maps were not exactly accurate as it was closed during the day when we initially visited. However, to our surprise, when we happened to pass by at night, it was open for business. The owner explained that they typically serve Thai food at night, except on Sundays when they take a rest. We had initially wanted to try their curry noodle during the day, but they were tired that day, so they didn't open the shop. Interestingly, we noticed that many restaurants in the area don't adhere to the timings displayed on Google maps, which was puzzling.

Nonetheless, we decided to give it a try and ordered the curry noodle, which turned out to be quite average and quite salty, possibly due to being reheated multiple times. We also tried some of their Thai dishes like the salt-baked fish, BBQ pork jowl meat, and pad khaprao, but unfortunately, they were just average in terms of taste and quality.

Despite the disappointments with the dishes, the owners’ warm and accommodating demeanor added a positive touch to the overall experience.

Review_Text: As an American visiting Chinatown in T town. I wanted something to eat on Raya since only Chinese people work today. It was a nice curry noodle experience. Very reasonable price for the amount of food. 6rm per bowl. They don’t have A/C and just a few fans so it can get hot. The menu is only in Chinese so u will have to look at the pictures but essentially just let the boss know you either want curry noodle soup with meat or vegetarian and or dry noodles with meat or vegetarian. Thankfully cuz ur on Google u can also show her the pictures. It’s pay as you go. So the uncle will come around to order ur drinks and u pay him when he brings them. Same with food. Order in the front and pay when they arrive at ur table. Hope that’s as helpful. Ask me questions on @ninjaxfang if you have questions. Happy Raya!

Name: Auntie Dayana'S
Review_Text:
Auntie Dayana’s never disappoints! The food is not just beautiful to look at — it’s bursting with fresh, authentic flavours that reflect both traditional and modern influences.

•	The Som Tam Jagung (corn papaya salad) was a refreshing burst of sweet, sour, and spicy. The crunchy corn, juicy tomatoes, roasted peanuts, and fresh lime made every bite addictive.
•	The Creamy Spaghetti with Smoked Beef was rich and indulgent, yet well-balanced. The sauce was silky, and the beef added a lovely smoky depth.
•	The Tom Yum Bee Hoon with seafood was hands-down a favourite — light yet flavour-packed, with fresh prawns and squid, and just the right kick of spice and tang from the lime and chillies.
•     The Pad Thai at Auntie Dayana’s and it was amazing! Light, fresh, and full of flavour — the seafood was super fresh, and the balance of sweet, sour, and spicy was spot on. A must-try if you love Thai flavours with a local twist!

The presentation was beautiful — every dish looked like it was plated with care. The use of banana leaves and traditional ceramic bowls added a local, homely touch.

The service was warm and attentive, and the ambiance made it a perfect spot for both casual dining and special occasions.

If you’re in Kuala Terengganu and looking for flavour, freshness, and a touch of heart in every dish, Auntie Dayana’s is a must-visit.

Red ruby dessert and coconut shake is a must have! They’ve green detox juice too if you’re looking for something healthy.

The only food that could be better is the glass noodle seafood salad. Seafood is generous but the sauce ran a little too sweet for my liking.

Name: Yes Corner Japanese Restaurant
Review_Text:
Was having some pregnancy-induced craving for takoyaki. Came and I got four takoyaki which weren't smooth surfaced - I instantly knew they weren't right. At first bite, yes the crispy envelope was there, but that's where the joy ended. The takoyaki batter itself was too thick, it felt like eating spherical mashed potato. The brown sauce felt like that zing that usually comes from Worcestershire sauce was scarce. Good takoyaki should be gooey on the inside and rather light on the palate but here it's really heavy

We had chawanmushi too. The egg custard itself was alright, not overcooked, not too eggy which is good, but I came to wonder why were the shredded kani sprinkled on top rather than mostly sunken in the chawanmushi itself. And that sliced cucumber untouched by the custard... Really didn't make sense both taste and aesthetic-wise

Decided to try unagi nigirizushi to really appreciate the taste of the rice. Sushi rice was not packed tightly enough. The unagi was missing its trademark smoky fishy taste

Finally, we had ebiten uramakizushi. I think this final plate was the one that saved the rest of the dishes. It was just nice - not too much of rice. Tempura batter light and crispy, prawn itself was so-so but I'm not going to be fussy about it because personally for someone from Klang Valley, I find that it's quite cheap

I'm unsure if the dishes are made in such a way to suit the local palate but for people who are used to eating Japanese food, we'd be rather flat to have them. The deco was too dark and depressing. The restaurant sign was really washed out. The acoustic and ventilation were a nightmare for me since I am claustrophobic, but in Terengganu, I didn't have much choice. Nonetheless, service was prompt, courteous, and friendly

All in all I think the restaurant has some potential. Maybe putting someone with a more vibrant mind in charge to make some changes while this sector is still rather unchallenged in Terengganu

Name: Yes Corner Japanese Restaurant
Review_Text:
When I thought I found a nice Japanese restaurant in Kuala Terengganu, am sorry to say the quality is not comparable to most other Japanese restaurant in the Klang Valley. They have a wide array of selection of sushi, sashimi and other selected menus available.

The place is cosy but a bit small and a bit depressing unfortunately with its dim light, quiet ambiance and small space. The staff are nice and friendly and ever helpful which is a plus.

The food, its Japanese food but the quality won't be on par with other Japanese restaurants. Not sure if its halal but the lack of mirin or seasoning in the sushi rice can be felt as its a little bland and the rice doesn't seem to be the high quality Japanese rice as its clumps and not soft to the mouthfeel. The sashimi were cut in chunks which to me is too big to enjoy, and some of the fish were clearly refrigerated and not really fresh as biting it can felt it was hard and tough with a ice texture, had to chew thoroughly. Not really the typical melt in your mouth sushi. Fresher ingredients, better preparation and maybe better cuts would help improve this.

The main meal of ten zaru soba was ok, the soba noodles were well made, though likely not made on the spot as it felt also frozen. the dipping sauce was a disappointment as it was basically soy sauce with ice in it, without any other condiments such as grated daikon, wasabi and greens as well as quails egg normally coming with such. Dipping it in the ice broth wasn't really doing it for me as it was more cold to the mouth than chilled. Putting the grilled saba on the same plate where you have ice also didn't make sense as it sapped the grilled saba of its warmth and made it soggy over time and cold. Again the saba didn't come with the usual accompaniment of grated daikon and lemon slice to adjust the taste. The taste as it is was ok, but due to the arrangement of plating lessen the quality overall.

In the end its a nice little Japanese restaurant if you really need a fix but if you are used to the more normal ones in the west coast, you may want to give this a miss. As am not sure its made to cater to local taste and dietary requirement but its not authentic Japanese cuisine.

Name: Yes Corner Japanese Restaurant
Review_Text:
Cute and classic ambience. Owner and staffs are friendly. Went there around 3pm during weekday, not crowded at all, so we ate peacefully. Waiting is a bit long & the bills are honestly quite pricey. For me, once in a blue moon is okay lol.

We ordered:
1. Variety of sushies - all good, the one with pink sauces a lil bit sweet to my liking, but still considered tasty. Salmons are my favourite, so good, no fishy taste at all. I usually can't eat raw food, so yeah.

2. Chicken fillet with rice - delicious! I love its gravy, so thick, addictive, and not spicy at all, i bet children would love it too.

3. Signature spicy noodle (chicken) - broth's very light in taste, but still got a nice kickin' spicy. I really love the seaweed inside, and please mind it's served super hot, so be careful not to burn your tongue.

Food was top notch 😍 We ordered a bunch of dishes, and every single one of them was yummy 😋 Drinks were pretty good too! My personal favourite were the fish dishes 🐟. The service was late, to be fair, we gave our orders just before the evening prayers. Do note that they close their kitchen during prayer time.

If you have a large group of people wanting to try some seafood at Kuala Terengganu, I’d highly recommend you this place! ✨

Name: Zie'S Corner
Review_Text:
went for breakfast at a spot with ample parking and a riverside view. I ordered the honey pancake and nasi lemak ayam.

The prices were slightly on the higher side considering the portion sizes—RM12 for the pancake and RM10 for the nasi lemak ayam.

The nasi lemak was satisfying, with fragrant rice, crispy chicken, and egg that complemented each other well. However, the sambal could use some improvement as it tasted a bit plain. Same goes with fries anchovies a bit too salty.

The pancake had a decent presentation but lacked flavor.

Overall, it was an acceptable experience with quick service at the counter. That said, the pricing could be better aligned with the portion sizes.

They did not use batter but they used breadcrumbs which is also weird because it dries the already naturally dried fish. The rm 20 dish was 2 very thin fish and chips. I only tasted the breadcrumbs. They should change the fish to dory or salmon and use proper batter so that at least it pleases people's eyes  Wrong moves guys. Nasi lemak was delicious. The chicken chop price went up whilst the portion shrinks. Servers were also lazy and unresponsive to questions. This used to be a good place for people to go to spend their hard earned money once a while now its just a n over hyped hipster cafe. Did not have a good experience. I dont recommend people to come here and spend their money. Okay view tho

Food came very fast and service was friendly and efficient. Kudos to the service team who looked like all locals.

Review_Text:

We arrived at 9.45pm when it wasn’t so busy but had to clean our own table.

Dishes started to arrive at 10.15pm but no rice or drinks were served despite reminding the other workers more than twice, including the one who took our order at the table. I even walked to the place where they were serving the rice to ask for ours.

Food was expensive, small portioned and mediocre in taste.

Name: Kst Ikan Bakar Kuala Ibai
Review_Text:
I recently visited KST Ikan Bakar and was thoroughly impressed. Even their basic plate of couple set RM 70 was more than enough, offering a generous portion that could easily satisfy your hunger. The Set Dulang Bakar was a highlight, well worth its price, delivering a rich and flavourful seafood experience.

While waiting for our food, they thoughtfully provided snacks, which was a nice touch. I also appreciated that they were still serving food around 11 PM, even though the *Celup Tepung* had already run out.

1. Nasi Putih (White Rice) - RM 2.00
2. Set Dulang Bakar (S) - RM 70.00
3. Carrot Susu (Carrot Milk) - RM 5.00
4. Teh O Panas (Hot Tea without Milk) - RM 1.50

Subtotal: RM 78.50
Tax: RM 4.71
Total: RM 83.21

What made the evening truly memorable was the live busking. The slim male singer had wonderful voices that added a delightful and unique touch to the dining atmosphere. HIS VOICE IS SO SOULFUL! 😍😱 It’s not just a meal; it’s an experience worth having! Please ask them to reprint their worn-out QR code as we'd like to contribute! 🥰

Name: Rhopi.Trg
Review_Text:
A hidden gem in KT! Dined at Rhopi Cafe and was genuinely impressed with the authentic Malay dishes and cozy kampung-style vibes. The presentation was clean, and the flavours hit just right!

📌 What we ordered:
•	🐟 Ikan goreng garing dengan bawang goreng – Crispy and full of flavor! A must-try.
•	🍳 Telur dadar – Fluffy and golden, perfect with rice.
•	🥒 Sayur lemak – Creamy, aromatic and comforting.
•	🥗 Ulam-ulaman with budu – Fresh, crunchy veggies served with spicy budu full of kick!
•	🍚 Steamed white rice to complete the set.

The budu was power! Pedas-pedas syok, especially with the ulam. Portion-wise, everything was cukup puas hati for sharing.

Ambience: Clean and simple. Feels like home, with friendly service.
Price: Reasonable for the portion and quality.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (5/5) – Highly recommended if you’re craving authentic Malay comfort food in Kuala Terengganu!

- Parking: Plenty of parking space, making it convenient for visitors.

Highly recommended for a family-friendly dining experience!

Name: Kd Fried Chicken ( Kdfc )
Review_Text:
I had a very disappointing experience at this restaurant. While the place looks spacious and well-ventilated, there are some serious hygiene issues that need immediate attention.

First and foremost, I found a strand of hair in my mashed potatoes, which is completely unacceptable. It clearly indicates poor food handling practices and a lack of cleanliness in the kitchen. This ruined the entire meal for me.

Additionally, the trash bin located near the seating area was overflowing and emitting a very unpleasant smell. Sitting close to it was extremely uncomfortable, and it’s shocking that it wasn't cleaned or managed regularly. No one wants to enjoy their meal next to the smell of garbage.

The food itself was average — the burger and fried chicken were okay, but nothing special. However, even a decent meal can't make up for such poor hygiene standards.

I sincerely hope the management takes these issues seriously and improves both kitchen cleanliness and overall sanitation.

Name: Sejati Chinese Mix Rice
Review_Text: This home-cooked style mixed rice is quite hideaway in a residential area. Parking is tricky during lunch hours; just park any place on the road as long as it would not block people's homes or traffic. This place could be quite packed up, and you may need to join others at the same table during busy hours. Not many selections of dishes but sufficient,  taste wise, i found that most of the dishes are quite okay, vegetarians friendly too. The owner was very friendly and attentive. The price was very reasonable, if not the cheapest, in Kuala Terengganu. Soup (usually gone by 1pm) and water are FOC. Even the herbal tea was only RM 1. My plate of rice with 2 types of meat (big portion) + one type of mixed veggies  + herbal tea was only RM 7. It opens from 1130 until finish around 2ish pm.

Name: Straits Restaurant
Review_Text:
Ordered steamed ginger fish, fried pork meat stuffed yam basket, sweet potato leaves, 2 big rice, 2 ice kosong.
We were then still hungry after visiting the daily held Sha bandar Ramadan bazaar, which is 2 mins walking distance. We googled to find this Chinese restaurant that has a high rating. Once seated, we talked to the lady in charge, asking the steamed fish a little more details like what fish, how much, what cooking etc , also checked the yam basket, the vegetables.
She told us rm7.5 per kilo and rm 35~40. So we would expect around 500 grams by math.
It was not usual the Song fish, different fish, which had a much less flesh unfortunately. And the most important thing is the size, wasn't around 500 grams, maybe less. The ginger flavor and a little lemony tangy flavor is nice.
And the yam basket wasn't cook inside the yam paste, just shaping and fried in way too quick. So its totally cold inside the yam ring paste. So my English husband spitted it out and we called the lady to replace a well properly cook as piping or at least hot to enjoy the yam ring.
The sauce is just nice. Not much pork meats though.
The sweet potato leaves is delicious as it's simple dish, lightly fried vegetables so.
The most surprising thing is the fish price turned out rm 46, not below rm 40(around 500grams)! By math, the fish should have been served over 600 gram at least.  But we highly doubt it was over 500 grams even.
So the total we paid reached to rm 89 including rm30 for the yam basket, vege, rice, water.
Since we were there for calming our yet hungry stomach down, it was achieved, but TBH, the quality for the price, we weren't happy to come back.

Name: Restoran Chuan Kee
Review_Text:
Passed down from father to son, this humble chicken rice shop carries a legacy of flavor through generations. With just rice and tender chicken, the simplicity of the dish speaks volumes—the aroma alone is enough to make your mouth water.

Whether in the father's era or now under the son's care, the taste remains timeless. You can count on getting one of the best plates around.

A true must-try for anyone who appreciates tradition served deliciously.

Name: Restoran Chuan Kee
Review_Text: It's my first chicken rice shop to go to when i am in KT. The shop is clean and i want to praise the attitude of the boss and staffs. They are very polite and friendly. I am already hungry for their chicken rice when i am writing this review 🤣🤣. Only parking is not that easy to find if you looking for council parking. You may need to park at the pay private parking.  I would anytime return to eat if i can find parking there 😅

Name: Restoran Chuan Kee
Review_Text: Nice location. Clean and tidy. Friendly staffs. No foreign workers. The chicken rice is one of the nicest in town. The rice is not really that full chicken fragrant which was a slight let down. The roast chicken is not bad. But the highlight is the steam chicken, which is super tender and delicious. The soy sauce that comes with the serving for the chicken isn’t that great, but you may the light sweet soy that is available to every table to give the excellent balance for the dish. Fyi. No QR payment here. Cash only 😅

Name: T-Homemade Cafe
Review_Text:
The Pros: No wait. Quick food serving. Overall food is ok, Not bad, you can't go wrong with Hokkien lang area.

The Cons: Don't expect too much on the kelopor (it is mainly starch + a little fish meat) not much taste from it, so most of the taste will come from the very spicy sauce. Price for the kelopor is around RM15(where next door selling RM20). Bun is ok (bun taste similar as next door, but Lee Hwa's Kaya is better, and more butter). Drink is average (next door better). Also it is quite hot here on sunny day.

Verdict: I won't order kelopor , I think it is overated. The environment is hot, so I will go with next door new shop with aircond and more variety of food if I visit china town next time.

Name: T-Homemade Cafe
Review_Text:
This eatery serves a delightful variety of foods, offering a mix of sweet and savoury treats. Customers can enjoy traditional local kuih, which are soft, fragrant, and rich in coconut and pandan flavours.

The freshly steamed bao and buns come with various fillings, from succulent meats to sweet pastes, ensuring a satisfying bite. For noodle lovers, the eatery serves flavorful and aromatic bowls of noodles, prepared with rich broths and fresh ingredients. The selection of bread is equally enticing, featuring soft, fluffy textures and delicious spreads.

As the earliest eatery to open at 6 a.m., it provides a perfect start to the day with warm, freshly made delicacies.

Name: Restoran Hafeez
Review_Text:
Restoran Hafeez in Kuala Terengganu is the place where you can savour the best “roti tempayan”. The bread is similar to nan bread but not the same. Hafeez’s roti tempayan is so soft yet crispy. It is served with either lamb curry, chicken tandoori, beef curry, or just gravy.  Once you taste it, you will be asking for more.
The owner, Hafeez, and his brother are very friendly. The restaurant is only open from 7.30 to 11.30 am every day except on Friday.

Name: Amz Seafood
Review_Text:
Ample parking and a spacious venue with both indoor and outdoor seating. Offers a mix of local and Western cuisine.

I tried the grilled chicken, which was delicious but the portion was small (see picture for reference). A larger portion would be ideal. The latte was well-presented and rich.

Other dishes were decent in taste. Waiting time for food was 10-20 minutes due to our large group. Staff were friendly and attentive.

Overall, a pleasant experience with some areas for improvement. Would likely return.

Name: Amz Seafood
Review_Text:
This is just my honest review on that particular day.
I went there on Merdeka Day for dinner
We ordered the set- include steamed fish.
We waited more than 1 hour - nobody told us that we were to expect long waiting hour; we might not order if that was the case.

A jug of orange drink arrived first.
Then after 1 hour, the rice and other dishes ( except fish). Only the rice was hot. All dishes were cold. 🫠

The fish. We waited for another 15min.
Overall, not a happy experience for us.
If there were not enough chef, just let the customers know.
That is simple logic.

Not sure if the owner will read this. As I am not the only one complained about the very long waiting hour.

Kudos to all the waiters and waitresses for their patience , as all other customers had been asking about the food many times too.

The owner should go to the restaurant to see the situation. It is a waste as the restaurant is comfortable and nice.
Cleaned_Text: honest review particular day went merdeka day dinner ordered set include steamed fish waited hour nobody told expect long waiting hour might order case jug orange drink arrived first hour rice dish except fish rice hot dish cold fish waited another min overall happy experience enough chef let customer know simple logic sure owner read one complained long waiting hour kudos waiter waitress patience customer asking many time owner see situation waste comfortable

Review_Text:

Place was supernice and clean, good ambience, but toilets were out of order. Tastewise was just so-so and everything was on salty side.

We ordered 6 dishes, but only 4 came. Then we saw customers that came later than us got their orders first, while we had to ask twice for the remaining 2 dishes. When food finally came, they were a bit cold. So frustrating. Pricewise ok, on the average and not too pricey. But if we were to return, only for the coffee.

Name: Amz Seafood
Review_Text: I came here unplanned and it got my attention. It was eased to park here because they have thier own parking spot. And i was surprised the have the whole prayer room for muslim customers. The surrounding is great, felt luxury and neat. But i did expect it to have AC inside the dining space and there was none when i went there but it's okay. As for the food i ordered their special fried rice and some thai based food. it was tasty for us. And the beverages is regular drink u can find in other restaurant but there's some addition to the beverage menu which i ordered original mojito,  they serve it very well and up to my taste. And the best thing is the price is really affordable considering the restaurant decor. The service here is awesome since they serve our order really quick eventhough the food was hot cooked food. The stafs clean other table right after customers left and i think it was a good work ethics. Plus here they have access for disabled people. The toilets ate bit bad though because the door lock was missing and i cant seem to flush the toilet afterwards. All in all, i recommend this place for dinner and I'd go here soon again.

Name: Amz Seafood
Review_Text:
Hey Owner! your restaurant is one of the worst dining experiences I’ve ever had. Service is painfully slow, food takes forever to come out, and it’s obvious you don’t bother monitoring your own business. Customers already complained about “lambat dapat makanan” in past reviews, but you clearly don’t care enough to improve. You just sit there trying to make money while your staff struggle and your customers get frustrated.

Stop being kedekut and hire more kitchen staff. If you can’t even manage basic service speed, don’t call yourself a restaurant. This is pure embarrassment. Your staff have zero training, no sense of professionalism, and you obviously haven’t taught them anything about hospitality.

To make things worse, you handed me a menu with Western food, but when I ordered, suddenly “tak ada.” Your staff didn’t even bother to tell us beforehand. What kind of nonsense is this? You completely wasted our time, ruined our meal, and spoiled the whole mood.

If you can’t run a restaurant properly, better close shop. Don’t fool customers with fake menus and lousy service. Right now, your place is nothing more than a shameful money-grab with zero standards.

Name: Restoran Mat Kedai Binjai
Review_Text:
Mat Binjai in Kuala Terengganu is an absolute gem for authentic Terengganu cuisine. The food here is truly top-notch, with dishes that reflect the rich heritage of traditional Malay cooking. The fried chicken was perfectly crispy and flavorful, the curries were beautifully spiced, and the grilled fish was incredibly fresh. The watermelon slices were a refreshing touch to round off the meal.

The ambiance is simple yet welcoming, making it a comfortable spot to enjoy a hearty meal. The friendly staff adds to the overall experience, ensuring you feel right at home. The display of dishes is vibrant and inviting, giving you plenty of mouthwatering options to choose from. It’s also great to see they accept modern payment methods like GrabPay, making it convenient for diners.

Mat Binjai deserves a solid 5-star rating for their excellent food, warm hospitality, and authentic vibe. A must-visit if you’re in Kuala Terengganu!

Review_Text: I made a reservation prior to arrival. At first glance it’s easy to spot the restaurant, located right by the beach in between trees. Despite the restaurant looking a bit rundown, the place is fairly clean and the staffs are very attentive. Came here for lunch and there are plenty of table available. According to the server, the place is usually busy during this hour. I had kung pao chicken with rich and chicken chop. Portion wise, they’re enough to fill me up, the chicken for the kung pao is tender and spicy with a hint of sweetness to it. The chicken chop has a crispy exterior with juicy meat inside, enough to make you mouthwatering at first cut. It is served with a light spicy and sweet brown sauce, a side of garden salad and julienned fries. To me the portion for the chicken chop is quite large enough to fulfill your cravings. Not pictured but also part of our orders were Mongolian beef stew, wonton noodles, and fried rice. All of which were well seasoned. Of i had a chance to come by again, i am sure that this will be a spot that i look forward too, and by that account I think you should too.

Name: Warung Dalam Kenangan
Review_Text:
⭐☆☆☆☆
Extremely disappointing experience. Not recommended, especially for tourists.

We visited Warung Dalam Kenangan at Pantai Batu Burok expecting a pleasant seafood meal by the beach, but it turned out to be one of the worst dining experiences we’ve had.
•	Parking is a nightmare. It’s extremely difficult to find a spot, even during non-peak hours.
•	Once seated, we had to wait over 1.5 hours just to receive one meal — the food was served in batches, so others at our table had to wait even longer.
•	The taste was bland and overpriced, definitely not worth the long wait or the money.
•	We later found out that local residents no longer support this place, and most customers are unaware tourists misled by social media marketing.
•	There are no waitstaff to assist; you must select your seafood, order your drinks, and collect everything yourself — which is highly inconvenient given the crowd.
•	Worst of all, there is no apology or explanation from the staff or management. The manager was unresponsive and ignored customer concerns completely.
•	Every table around us had unhappy and frustrated customers. It seems like the same complaints happen daily, yet nothing is being done to improve.

Sadly, this place relies on false hype rather than genuine service or food quality. Would not recommend to any tourist or visitor. Save your time and money
Cleaned_Text: extremely disappointing experience recommended especially tourist visited warung dalam kenangan pantai batu burok expecting pleasant seafood meal beach turned one worst dining experience weve parking nightmare extremely difficult find spot even nonpeak hour seated wait hour receive one meal served batch others table wait even longer taste bland overpriced definitely worth long wait money later found local resident longer support customer unaware tourist misled social medium marketing waitstaff assist must select seafood order drink collect everything highly inconvenient given crowd worst apology explanation staff management manager unresponsive ignored customer concern completely every table around unhappy frustrated customer seems like complaint happen daily yet nothing done improve sadly relies false hype rather genuine service quality would recommend tourist visitor save time money

Name: Kedai Makang Belagoangin
Review_Text:
Browsed from google. Its recommended for lunch time. So I decided to hop on and go here.

I have to say, I enjoyed every bite of the meal here. Considering the deliciousness the price is so reasonable. There were plenty sitting for bigger groups.

BELAGOANGIN follow the concept of their name. Since no FAN was offered at all to customer. Its kinda sweaty yeah during the hot and humid weather.

Lets talk about hygiene. For me, this place is not so hygiene. There is alot of leftover food on the tables and floor. They will clean right up whenever you wanna sit there.

Here is some change that you could do in future:
1) Install some fan
2) DO NOT pour water into your Hand Soap Liquid (it make no bubbles at all)
3) Parking expansion (PLEASE)
4) Always clean the tables after customer left the table

Overall, I would recommend this restaurant. Go and experience yourself here by enjoying the atmosphere.

Name: Beranda Restaurant, Duyong Marina & Resort
Review_Text:
Me and my family booked 2 deluxe rooms via AGODA for RM 175 × 2 per night

+ve Points

1. Cosy
2. Rustic feeling. Nice environment
3. Clean
4. Receptionist were friendly
5. Buggy staff were timely, helpful and friendly
6. The shower head and tub knob were broken. Maintenance repaired it by the time we came back to the room after dinner
7. WIFI acceptable speed. More towards the slower side. But not too bad

-ve points

1. Movie channels. Only 2! The list in the room states almost 10 channels. HBO even. But only RTM 1 and TV3 were available.
2. We understood the fact that it's Ramadhan. So the breakfast buffet was not available for just the 6 of us. Fair enough. But the staff that was dealing with us was so 'blurr' when she was explaining to us on what was available. She pointed us towards the spread which had only few cereals and coffee/tea. Then when we asked her is that all, she further explained we need to order ala-carte. Then when we asked her what was available, she became "miss blurr" again. This was kind of annoying. But in the end the food was acceptable. They just need to educate their staff

Overall, good value for money with the discounted price that we got

Each dish was well-prepared, with balanced flavours and fresh ingredients. The dry noodles had a rich, savoury taste complemented by the minced meat, while the curry noodles had a fragrant, mildly spicy broth. The fried koay teow was flavourful with a good wok hei aroma. Tomyam campur was perfectly balanced, and the siakap masak pedas was crazy good, like a 100% must-try. Bihun kungfu was just right and tasty. Don't miss out on the char kueh teow it’s worth trying. Maggi tomyam brought back memories of flavors from Hatyai. Kailan ikan masin was good, and kerabu sotong had a cool twist. Oh, and the prices? Totally reasonable. Street parking is available.

Shoutout to Haziq from Kedai Kunci Mydin Gong Badak for recommending this place. Thanks to him, my family had an awesome meal!

Name: Taman Tamadun Islam ( Tti Management Sdn Bhd )
Review_Text:
Beautiful Concept, Poorly Maintained

Visited Taman Tamadun Islam with high hopes as the concept is truly unique and educational. The replicas of iconic Islamic architecture from around the world are impressive and offer great potential for learning and cultural appreciation. However, it's disappointing to see that the place is not well maintained. Some areas look rundown, with fading paint, broken structures, and overgrown landscaping. With proper upkeep and attention, this could be a must-visit attraction in Terengganu. Hopefully, it will be restored to its full glory in the future.

Name: Taman Tamadun Islam ( Tti Management Sdn Bhd )
Review_Text:
Taman Tamadun Islam is an absolute gem in Kuala Terengganu, and a must-visit for anyone interested in Islamic heritage and architecture. The Monument Park is breathtaking, featuring meticulously crafted replicas of iconic Islamic landmarks from around the world. Walking through the park feels like a journey across history, with each monument beautifully presented and accompanied by detailed information.

The Crystal Mosque, with its stunning glass and steel design, is the crown jewel of the park. It's not only a visual masterpiece but also a serene place to reflect and admire the blend of modern and traditional architecture.

The park is well-maintained, and the staff are incredibly friendly and knowledgeable, always ready to guide and provide insights. The Islamic Civilization Gallery is another highlight, offering fascinating exhibits about Islamic contributions to science, art, and culture.

The river cruise was a delightful experience, providing picturesque views of the park and the surrounding Terengganu River. To top it off, the souvenir shops and restaurants offer a great selection of items and delicious food, adding to the overall experience.

TTI is a perfect blend of education, culture, and leisure. Whether you’re a history enthusiast, a family on vacation, or someone seeking tranquility, this place has something special for everyone. Highly recommended!

Name: Ben Fishball Noodle
Review_Text: Totally satisfied 吃的过瘾! There could be hardly a few good Chinese noodle specialists to be found in Kuala Terengganu but this is the one you wouldn't want to miss. Fresh hand-made YTFs are the best in town (as in Kuala Terengganu if it’s not the whole Terengganu). The YTFs come in various options like fried fucuk, stuffed bean curds, stuffed vegetables (chilies, eggplants, bitter gourds, etc), or simply fish meatballs. Their fish meat paste is freshly made daily without a fishy smell, is bouncy, and is packed with umami flavor. You can go with either soup, dry or curry noodles. Their curry is rich in coconut and spices (especially lemongrass) flavor, the soup is clear but savory, and dry dark soy sauce is well balanced between seasoning oil and tastiness, not to mention their yellow noodles are free from lye, fresh and chewy. Minimum trace of MSG detected (as I have a very low tolerance for MSG). Very friendly staff especially the aunty who prepares the food behind the front counter, she always greets guests with smiles and patience, willing to accommodate different customer preferences on how to prepare their food like more dark soy sauce,  soaked up the fried fucuk in a curry broth, etc). Very reasonably priced, you will leave this place with a full stomach but not a hole in the wallet. Washroom was clean, but parking could be a challenge and yet manageable during breakfast or lunch hours. Usually sold out by 2 pm, off on Tuesday  (IIRC)

Name: Restoran Lempeng
Review_Text: One of the most delicious traditional Terengganu cuisine I’ve had in my life. It makes me feels like the foods my grandmother cooked for me when I’m home afterschool. The lunch meals and the herbs are various. The chicken is cooked well done, the sambal is not too spicy, suitable for anti-spicy person. The omellete and the white rice are fluffy. This restaurant serves one of the best keropok lekor in Terengganu. The lempeng here is coconuty, the curry is nice and not spicy. The sirap bandung icecream here is very good, perfect complement for the lunch meal. Deep-fried fish is fresh, and suitable to eat during the tea time. One rare traditional Terengganu cuisine; which is “ketupat sotong” is being served here. You can view the South China Sea beside the restaurant, with Kapas Island on the right side of the horizon. The environment is windy especially during evening, and feels very tropical. Overall, five stars for traditional Terengganu cuisine, together with the tropical environment.

Review_Text:
Our homestay is nearby, we just got back from Pulau Kapas. Was really hungry and this restaurant happened to still open at 3PM. However, since it’s already 3PM, the choices of foods is limited. We take away our food. My husband and I took Nasi and Ayam Goreng along with Kuah. I took Kuah from Daging dish and add on Salad. Despite not many options, the salad still looking fresh.

While the cashier pack our Nasi, before giving it to us, she took our order for drink. We both ordered Limau Ais. After done, we go to the cashier counter for our food to be packed securely. The cashier sum up our order was RM21. Price wise, reasonable since my husband took 2 piece of Ayam Goreng.

The cashier straight show the place to pickup the drink, we went there, there are 3 teen guys at the drink station. They haven’t make our order yet, bcs they asked us again to confirm our order. After few minutes standing, I go to our car while my husband still waiting. My husband informed me that one of the guy said that there are no Limau, so can they change to Lemon instead. My husband reask again “Limau habis?”. The guy mockingly said “Habis” in KL accent and brushed it off with small laugh. I don’t think the laugh is necessary. Then we change our order to Teh O Ais. Still, took very long for th to prepare.

Food was great! Rempahan Ayam Goreng was very good, crunchy still. Even I only took the kuah from the daging, it tastes very well-flavoured! Salad is indeed fresh! Our drink is just nicely taste, the level of sugar is okay.

Review_Text: We came across this place on our way back from the islands and were so so thrilled with our meal. They had other lunch dishes (squid, fish, chicken), quite a good variety. But the main reason for this review is that the food was AMAZING. The rice was lemak enough, the gulai was bursting with flavour, and the sambal was a fiery experience. Service was decent. You take your own food, dine first and pay later. Am saving this place for the next time we're in the area.

Name: Restoran Anjung Seri
Review_Text:
Decided to stop here for lunch on our way back home from Pulau Redang and I must say their food is so delicious and authentic!!

It's a self service restaurant where you choose your own food, pick a table, order your drink and pay later. They have a huge selection of food, you'll be spoilt for choice! If I'm not mistaken there's 4 - 5 types of rice to choose from - nasi dagang, pulut kuning (yellow glutinous rice), nasi lemak etc

We ordered the nasi dagang with fish curry & gulai lemak. The rice of the nasi dagang was cooked to perfection; it had the right texture and mixture of white and glutinous rice. The dishes we ordered were also so tasty!

The teh ice (not pictured) was also so yummy 😋 Will definitely come back here again when we are around the area.

The nasi ulam budu with ikan goreng is really really good. The star of the day would be their singgang ikan selayang. OMG!!! They are delicious that a plate of white rice would not be enough I guarantee you! Please try the food here. Dirt cheap, very delicious and the family who owns the place is really humble and always ready to help.

Name: Nasi Dagang Atas Tol - Kelulut
Review_Text:
ATMOSPHERE: LOCAL RESTAURANTS, OPEN DINING, SEAVIEW
FACILITIES: LIMITED PARKING
PRICE: REASONABLE
SERVE: EXCELLENT
MENU: NASI DAGANG, KUEH, KEPOK LEKOR, KETUPAT GORENG ETC

This Nasi Dagang restaurant by the beach offers a refreshing open-air dining experience. The atmosphere is lively, especially during peak hours, as the spot is always packed with customers. The open concept seating make every bite more enjoyable even the taste just OK. The beachside view and sound of the waves create a relaxing backdrop despite the crowd. The taste and price take a back seat of experience.
Cleaned_Text: atmosphere local restaurants open dining seaview facilities limited parking price reasonable serve excellent menu nasi dagang kueh kepok lekor ketupat goreng etc nasi dagang restaurant beach offers refreshing openair dining experience atmosphere lively especially peak hours spot always packed customers open concept seating make every bite enjoyable even taste ok beachside view sound waves create relaxing backdrop despite crowd taste price take back seat experience

Name: Nasi Dagang Atas Tol - Kelulut
Review_Text:
Sedap. Sangat sedap. Boleh repeat.
Servis tiptop, portion besar, view tepi laut best.
Conveniently located on our route back to KL.

Honestly I was initially surprised that Kak Mira (JT Cafe, Kapas) suggested this establishment. Heard that the ‘nasi dagang atas tol’ is overrated and only liked by ‘orang luar’. These rumours in my opinion are well and truly malicious, untrue, and empirically incorrect based on my own experience.

Pukul 10:30 🕥 dia tutup. Nasi pun habis 😂
Cleaned_Text: sedap sangat sedap repeat servis tiptop portion besar view tepi laut best conveniently located route back kl honestly initially surprised kak mira jt cafe kapas suggested establishment heard nasi dagang atas tol overrated liked orang luar rumours opinion well truly malicious untrue empirically incorrect based experience pukul tutup nasi habis

Pok Shah Ikan Bakar, Kuala Berang
★★★★★ 4.5/5 Bintang

Rekomen sangat untuk bawa kawan-kawan atau rombongan makan tengah hari di sini.

Name: Bayou Beach Cafe
Review_Text:
I came around 7.30pm, but all the staff were on their break, so we had to wait outside until 8pm before being seated. It wasn’t too much of an issue since the place has a nice ambience, but just something to be aware of if you plan to come around that time. There were also many cats around, which some people may find cute, though I noticed a few had peed or pooped in the sand near the café. It can feel a little unhygienic if you accidentally step too close, but it’s part of the open, beachside environment.

On the positive side, the food was really delicious and they offered a wide variety of cakes that were worth trying. The view was absolutely superb, creating such a relaxing and cozy atmosphere that’s perfect for hanging out with friends or family. At night, it does get a bit mosquito-heavy, so bringing some repellent might make the experience more comfortable. Overall, despite a few small downsides, it was still a lovely spot to enjoy good food and an amazing view.

Name: Penarik Beach
Review_Text:
This is possibly my favourite beach in all of mainland Terengganu. Beautiful, a little wild and quiet in some places. The sand is soft and golden. The sea can get rough. It is not a good place to swim due to the strong rip currents.

I first encountered Penarik in 1999, when I drove from Kuala Terengganu to Kota Bharu on a business trip for my company. Since that time, I have made a point to return every year. There aren't any fancy resorts or hotels here. It's a place where I go to get away. Over the years, I have seen that the beach is shrinking. Erosion is taking its toll.

Name: Chicken Dance ( Kuala Berang )
Review_Text:
I tried this fried chicken spot for the first time, and it definitely hit the spot! The chicken was juicy— exactly how fried chicken should be. I ordered the spicy version, and it had just the right amount of heat without overpowering the flavor. The sides were solid too — the coleslaw was fresh and the fries were crispy.

The service was friendly, and the place was clean with a good vibe. Prices were reasonable for the portion size. I’d definitely come back — the tender is crispy and tasted good too

Overall, a great spot if you're craving some quality fried chicken

Name: Restoran Aroma Kampung
Review_Text: Limited options of food, but the one they offered taste really good. This is a nice "kampung" restaurant which served traditional malay food. You can find 2-3 types of Kerabu, grilled prawns, grilled fish (small fish), grilled chicken, Baung Masak Tempoyak, Spicy prawn with chilies and also sayur pucuk paku. I went there few times, usually took the same dished. White rice with two pieces of ikan baung, one small piece of spicy squid, a scoop of sayur pucuk paku and iced coffee, and that will cost me RM15. So, not really cheap but reasonable.

Review_Text: Great restaurant! The food is really good and always fresh. Staff are friendly and helpful, making the whole experience pleasant. Prices are affordable and worth it for the quality you get. Their takeaway procedure is also smooth and well-organized, which makes it very convenient. Highly recommend!
Cleaned_Text: great restaurant food really good always fresh staff friendly helpful making whole experience pleasant prices affordable worth quality get takeaway procedure also smooth wellorganized makes convenient highly recommend

Name: Combi Grill
Review_Text:
Overrated, overpriced, bad service and food taste not backing up the high price tag on the menu

First thing first, the other guy is great at their service.. there’s just this one guy who serves our food to us that slammed our plates on the table while deliveour food. I don’t know about you guys but i consider that as rude. Even if you have a bad day, work is work and you need to be professional while working.
F&B is a service career and if you can’t give good service then you shouldn’t be even in this genre of work.

Now let’s talk about the foods

Spaghetti carbonara- fail. It’s actually mushroom sauce and not even a slight hint of carbonara taste. The taste is okay but i wanted to eat carbonara not mushroom..

Spaghetti bolognese- kuah cair, tawar, rasa macam packaged sauce.

Chicken chop & beef steak- the black pepper sauce taste too sweet and only have a slight hint of black pepper taste. Baked beans cold even when they just arrived.. it’s in the name, “baked” beans you know.. maybe some people enjoy cold baked beans but i don’t.. especially for the price, i had high expectations for the foods here. The coleslaw have flavour but it doesn’t have enough kick to it.. the flavour is only like a hint on your tongue. The fries are too crispy, i don’t get me wrong i love crispy fries but i would expect the fries to be crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside. But the marination for the beef is good. That’s the only +1 that i can give

Overall
The food needs a lot of improvements especially the pastas and please2 check ur staff’s attitude at work.
Cleaned_Text: overrated overpriced bad service food taste backing high price tag menu first thing first guy great service theres one guy serves food us slammed plates table deliveour food dont know guys consider rude even bad day work work need professional working fb service career cant give good service shouldnt even genre work lets talk foods spaghetti carbonara fail actually mushroom sauce even slight hint carbonara taste taste okay wanted eat carbonara mushroom spaghetti bolognese kuah cair tawar rasa macam packaged sauce chicken chop beef steak black pepper sauce taste sweet slight hint black pepper taste baked beans cold even arrived name baked beans know maybe people enjoy cold baked beans dont especially price high expectations foods coleslaw flavour doesnt enough kick flavour like hint tongue fries crispy dont get wrong love crispy fries would expect fries crispy outside fluffy inside marination beef good thats give overall food needs lot improvements especially pastas please check ur staffs attitude work

Name: Combi Grill
Review_Text: A smorgasbord of tantalizing dishes and quite frankly the only place in Kuala Berang which offers unique dishes such as Nasi Goreng Lobster or Spaghetti with a side of lobster. Besides, other dishes are delicious in their own way especially their Chicken Chop and Lamb Chop, alongside Spaghetti Bolognese and Spaghetti Carbonara. However, as good as it seems, the establishment has a bit of a problem with bird droppings as pigeons are making the rooftops their home and usually defecate from atop the electrical wires onto the entrance of the eatery below. Side note: My friend got bird poop on his arm after exiting the door. Hopefully this problem will be solved in the near future.

Name: D'Perahu Seafood
Review_Text:
We’ve been craving a proper seafood shellout and this place absolutely nailed it! From the incredibly fresh seafood to the rich, finger-licking sauce, every bite was pure satisfaction. The crabs were meaty, the prawns juicy, and the clams cooked just right - all bursting with the flavours!

What made it even better was the warm, lively atmosphere and the excellent service. The staff was attentive and made sure we had everything we needed for the ultimate messy-but-worth-it feast.

Thank you for the amazing food and wonderful experience, you’ve won us over, and we’ll definitely be back for more! Highly, highly recommend!

Pro tip: Bring wet wipes, wear comfy clothes, and come hungry!

Review_Text: On the second day in Kuala Terengganu, we decided to take dinner here, despite low rating of 3.9. We came early at 650 pm, so that we can have a nice table on the upper floor. We ordered fried ikan kerapu in thai sauce, udang goreng telor masin, and tomyam as the main meal. The foods were very delicious. I truly enjoyed dinner here. Recommended.

Review_Text:
Food: Local food with lots of ulam. Famous for Ikan Baung Tempoyak. I love my masak tempoyak spicy & sour but theirs is not really. Taste wise ok la…

Price: Affordable.

Staff: They have a lot of staff to cater for your orders. Just call them and they will come. Expect some customer service delays during peak hours.

Ambience: The stall is near the main road so it depends on the weather.

Cleanliness: Do come during non-peak hours if you can’t stand some messiness.

Parking: Available parking space but not enough to cater for the customers during peak hours.

Name: Katsetiu Villas
Review_Text:
Our stay at Katsetiu Villas was absolutely amazing! We spent most of our time relaxing in the villa, it truly feels like a home away from home. The main villa comfortably fit our group of three couples, and every moment there was pure enjoyment.

The villa is just steps away from a beautiful beachfront view, perfect for watching both sunrise and sunset. The swimming pool is well-maintained, and the breakfast provided each morning was delicious.

We also loved the nearby fruit milkshakes and authentic local meals, simple yet so flavourful!

Overall, it’s a peaceful and private getaway surrounded by nature and comfort. Highly recommended for couples, families, or anyone looking for a relaxing beachside escape. Beachside 🌊⛱️🏖️☀️🐚🎊…

Name: Katsetiu Villas
Review_Text:
Amazing 5 nights stay at Katsetiu villas and here is my honest review.

Friendly service by the staff, clean rooms with fresh towels with no smell and clean bedsheet. I have travelled and stayed in most run-down homestays or chalets in Terengganu, and Katsetiu is by far the best, most comfortable, luxurious and best value for money.

I paid RM 2,000 for the main villa that can fit 10 people, and it worked out to be only RM 200 per person per night. Unbeatable value, super affordable, and what can I say, the best part is the outstanding beach view with only 50 meters walk from the beach.

Yes, the villas are all beach facing and you can park your car directly next to the entrance. The walls are unpainted, bare concrete and expect an unusual finish that was filled with 2-3 large comfy sofas, large dining table that can fit 10 -12 people, and huge kitchen, and to mention huge fridge too.

Looking at the amenities, it provides more than basic homestay with creature comfort in a well-thought out design. I have no complaints whatsoever in my recent trip, although the food is adequate, their nasi dagang tastes authentic, and a must try for first timers. But since it is free as the rooms came with free breakfast, my family and I had no complaints!

Water is clean, and had no issues drinking it (of course you had to boil it) - and upon checking other villas, I found they installed water filter in each villa (located outside of the villa). I had worst experience in Redang Island resort hotels where the water is less clean than the coastal areas of Terengganu.

One thing I am looking forward to is their pool though - currently being constructed next to the garden villa. During our stay in August, the swimming pool construction is in the final stages, and I believe the area will turn out to be a nice relaxing area for open pit BBQ in the evening and may be used for small events.

Overall, it is worth a repeat, can’t wait to visit this place again in my future trip. Highly recommended for a comfy homestay villa (and not to be confused with 5-star resort or hotel) if you seek for a private luxury vacation home rental with no fuss, hassle-free experience. Definitely made for beach lovers, divers and families who enjoy the premium of staying in a quiet location, away from touristy hyped up places, or those overrated 5 star hotels.

Review_Text:
Honest Review of My Stay at the Villa

We paid RM2,000+ per night for a 3-room villa, but the experience was far from the “luxury” standard it claims to be.

1. Cleanliness & Hygiene

For the price point, cleanliness was unacceptable. Towels provided were smelly, even after requesting replacements, they remained the same. Many amenities were poorly maintained: the bidet, soap dispenser, and kettle were oxidized, while the bed sheets had stains. The soap provided was of such poor quality that I’d suggest bringing your own. Overall, the villa did not feel fresh or hygienic.

2. Food & Breakfast

Breakfast was another disappointment. Each guest is only entitled to one meal, and if you order more, it’s RM15 per plate. The problem is, there was no signage and the staff never informed us about this “rule” until after we ordered. We skipped breakfast entirely on the second day as the options are very limited: two, to be exact.

3. Facilities & Activities

For a villa that charges RM2,000 a night, activities were almost non-existent. The only option was the bicycles provided. The villa is located about an hour’s drive from town, and with nothing around the area, one would at least expect a pool, recreation area, or some form of entertainment. Unfortunately, the reception was poor, and WiFi was weak throughout the stay.

4. Service & Management

When we shared our feedback with the owner, we weren’t looking for compensation just acknowledgment. Instead, the response was defensive, even suggesting they didn’t like guests who complain. At this price point, basic hospitality and professionalism should be a given.

The Only Positive:
The villa’s exterior looks nice at first glance, and it is located opposite a beach. Sadly, that’s the only upside, the interior and experience do not match the image.

Final Thoughts

At RM2,000 per night, this villa is simply not up to standard. With poor hygiene, weak amenities, disappointing food, lack of activities, and dismissive management, it does not deliver the luxury experience it promises. I honestly cannot understand how it has so many positive reviews.

If you’re considering staying here, set your expectations very low or look elsewhere for far better value.

Masalah utama di sini adalah kawasan parkirnya yang kurang dan terpaksa parkiran di bahu jalan, waspada dikala sibuk dan juga letaklah kenderaan sebaik mungkin agar tidak menghalang lalulintas.

Premisnya nampak gah dengan papan tanda perniagaan, cantik, warnanya mencelak serta jelas.

Kebersihan luaran adalah dibawah MDHT, dalamannya biasa saja, tiada tarikan mata hanya tertumpu pada menu semasa. Tata cahaya agak sederhana, tiada pencahayaan khusus atau romantik.

Susunatur meja, agak baik, kemas, meski alatan asas atas meja memang kurang. Meja tidak berlapik hanya akan disapu setelah digunakan.

Kebersihan bahagian kakitangan tidak jelas mahupun penjelasan, kebersihan kakitangan ada pertikaian. Perlu keseragaman agar lebih ceria dan meriah seperti gahnya papan tanda premis.

Keseluruhan penarafan pada kadar 3.5 saja.

Name: Hr Mee Champa 3 - Kuala Berang
Review_Text:
Visiting a restaurant that serves a signature dish called Mee Champa, which offers a mix of chicken and meat. At first taste, the dish had a strong, distinct meat aroma that was a bit overwhelming. However, the experience transformed after adding some chili paste, sour chiles, soy sauce, and other salty condiments. These additions brought the flavors to life, elevating the dish and giving it a balanced kick that engaged all the senses.

For the best experience, I’d recommend letting the bowl sit for about five minutes after adding the condiments to mellow out the heat and allow the flavors to blend. Once you dig in, it’s worth the wait.

The restaurant itself is air-conditioned, providing a cool, comfortable escape from a hot day. The quiet, spacious setting makes it a perfect spot to relax and recharge after working in the heat.

Overall, Mee Champa is worth trying if you enjoy bold flavors and a cozy dining atmosphere. Recommended!

Name: Suria Link Boat Service
Review_Text:
We were 2 women travellers and took snorkelling package to pulau kapas with them. Our skipper aka guide, Alang, was very knowledgable on Pulau Kapas history, attractions and wildlife (which is a total plus point if you want more than just sightseeing).

He was also friendly and very professional, reminding everyone to put on their life jacket and giving safety brief before moving towards pulau kapas. He went out of his way to guide us on how/ where to snorkel and made my friend felt safe in the water (my friend has fear in water).

Totally recommend Suria Link & Alang because the prices of snorkelling packages offered  by the companies at the jetty are more or less the same, however, Alang’s personal touch on the trip definitely made it worth more than the price we paid for. Definitely coming back!

Name: Pizza Hut Delivery Kuala Berang
Review_Text:
The food is the same you’d expect from PizzaHut. So, it’s okay.

As for the service, it delivers, though you have to acknowledge that this place is not really a place to dine in and more to take away your food.

The atmosphere is okay. The place is a little dim and it’s pretty small since it focuses more on delivery and takeouts, not dine in or sit in. So, it’s understandable. I think, the best way to order pizza here is by calling them first in advance and later come to pick it up at the outlet. It’s much more convenient and time saving.

Name: M U N O H
Review_Text:
Kedai ni beroperasi pada waktu pagi (makanan untuk sarapan pagi) dan waktu malam (masakan panas).

Makanan pagi dijual oleh Puan Munoh sendiri. Puan Munoh masak nasi minyak je. Makanan lain semua orang hantar. Ada beraneka jenis kuih-muih. Nasi lemak yang dah siap bungkus pun ada. Ada nasi lemak ikan dan bilis. Nasi dagang pun ada. Makanan pagi semua okay, cuma kuih lipat je tak sedap. Roti canai pun ada dijual di sini.

Masakan panas waktu malam diambil alih oleh anak saudara Puan Munoh. Ada bermacam jenis masakan panas termasuk set ikan tilapia dan sebagainya. Bagi saya, makanan dia semua sedap. Portion pun banyak. Tak kedekut ayam. Cuma harga tu bagi saya sedikit mahal. Tapi kalau ikutkan tahun 2026 ni, harga barang pun semua dah mahal jadi logik je harga yang dijual. Mi goreng mamak RM7.50, telur mata RM1.50, kuetiau ladna seafood RM15.00. Standard je harga begini sebab mi goreng mamak tu penuh ayam. Kuetiau ladna pun banyak seafood. Boleh makan dua orang untuk perut yang tak besar mana.

So far okay je kedai ni. Nak makan dengan tenang, sini la tempatnya. Customer pun tak ramai sangat.

Name: Chobby Seafood
Review_Text: This is a small, old-style chu char restaurant in Kuala Besut, very close to the jetty to Pulau Perhentian. The food is good. We had bitter gourd with egg, fried rice, fried bee hoon, stir-fried vegetables, and fried sotong. The sotong was fresh, although it was a bit tough, possibly because it was slightly overcooked or due to the size of the sotong. Even though it is in a tourist area, the restaurant still keeps its quality, and the prices are reasonable. The service is a bit slow because it isa family-run place with not many staff. But it is worth the wait, especially since there are not many Chinese restaurants in Kuala Besut. Most importantly, the food tastes nice.

Name: Mateema Ikan Bakar Dan Seafood,Kampung Raja Besut,Terengganu (The Best Ikan Bakar Dapur Arang In Besut)
Review_Text:
Rating: ★★★★☆ (4.4/5)
Had a great lunch at Mateema Ikan Bakar & Seafood in Besut! The fish was incredibly fresh and perfectly grilled — you can really taste the sea in every bite. The daging bakar was flavorful with rich, tasty seasoning, though I have to say it was a bit too tough and not as chewy as I hoped, which is why I’m giving 4.4 stars instead of a full 5.

What I absolutely love is the ambience — the stall’s wooden plank design (papan kayu) brought me right back to my childhood memories in Kampung Laut. Nostalgic, cozy, and very much like eating in a kampung kitchen. A beautiful blend of taste and tradition. Highly recommended if you’re in Besut and want an authentic seafood experience!

Name: Magari Station (Western Besut)
Review_Text:
The waitress was pleasant and attentive, and the café appeared quite busy during my visit. However, I was rather disappointed with the food quality this time.

I ordered the Korean Spicy Chicken, which turned out to be overly sweet and tasted more like Honey Garlic Chicken than the expected Korean Spicy Garlic flavor. The Honey Garlic Chicken itself had a strong buttery taste, while the New York Smash Burger was also quite sweet with an overpowering garlic flavor that masked the taste of the beef.

I only managed to take photos of the chicken dishes — the one on the left is the Honey Garlic Chicken, and the one on the right is the Spicy Garlic Chicken. Despite my disappointment, I still finished all the food as I had paid for it, and as a Muslim, I believe in avoiding food waste, especially when many in Palestine are struggling with food scarcity.

I remember dining here in June 2025, when the food was delicious, but unfortunately, this time the experience did not meet my expectations.

Name: 88 Lucky Seafood Restaurant
Review_Text:
Lucky Seafood Restaurant, situated at 88 Jalan Pantai near the Perhentian Island jetty in Kuala Besut, is a popular choice for travelers and locals alike.  The restaurant offers a variety of seafood dishes, with patrons noting both positive and negative experiences.

Positive Aspects:

Fresh and Flavorful Dishes: Many customers have praised the freshness and taste of the seafood. For instance, one reviewer mentioned enjoying prawns, deep-fried squid, fried rice, and vegetables, stating that the food was good and reasonably priced, especially the large prawns.

Generous Portions: Some diners have highlighted the ample serving sizes, which provide good value for money.

Areas of Concern:

Service Quality: Several reviews have pointed out issues with service, including slow delivery of food and perceived unfriendliness from staff. One patron noted that the service was slow and the food uninspiring, while another mentioned being shortchanged and receiving a dismissive response when addressing the issue.

Ambiance and Cleanliness: Some visitors have commented on the restaurant's basic setup and cleanliness. Observations include the presence of personal items like mattresses in common areas and children moving around the premises, which may affect the dining experience for some.

Conclusion:

Lucky Seafood Restaurant offers fresh and flavorful seafood dishes with generous portions, making it a worthwhile visit for seafood enthusiasts. However, potential diners should be prepared for possible service delays and a modest dining environment. It's advisable to manage expectations accordingly and consider these factors when planning a visit.
