Name: 88 Lucky Seafood Restaurant
Review_Text:
I often come here since I work nearby. Today I was very disappointed with the price and food. Come here many times and usually the restaurant aren’t that crowded, price is reasonable, the portion is generous and seafood is fresh.

Today, the restaurant was very crowded with many foreign tourists. I order the same dish, seafood kueytiow. The food quality was poor, the kueytiow smell really bad due to the seafood is no longer fresh and the amount of seafood given was very small and little.

But it’s okay, today has many customers perhaps she has to ration it wisely, it was still bearable and I finished it. When it’s time to make payment, the Kueytiow has become 17myr , 5 dollar more than usual since the seafood Kueytiow price was only 12 myr two months ago. The milo drink is 3.5myr (normal). Total is 20myr.

I asked why the sudden increment, she answered, you’ve ordered seafood (she has definitely forgotten me since I’m lived nearby, her regular customer). She seemed ashamed after I questioned, then said 20myr will do. Hence I paid 20myr.

I am really not happy with the inconsistency when it comes to the food quality n pricing, especially when there are many tourists around. Indeed disappointing.

Following photos:
1. Today (seafood kueytiow with iced milo)

2. Two month ago (seafood kueytiow with hot milk)

They’re full house on the time we arrived (CNY holiday ) and the owner politely inform us there will be an extra waiting time for our order.

We ordered ikan merah tanda (red snapped)bakar berempah, Ikan cermin bakar tawar and sotong celup tepung, a plate of mix ulam and & pantai timur best condiments (budu).

Name: Cherofee Coffee And Sandwich Smoke (Coffee Besut)
Review_Text: Great parking area and a pleasant environment , cozy and feels like home. Unfortunately, the food and service were a bit off during my visit. I came at night, and some of the ulam such as pegaga looked wilted and were not edible. The petola also tasted slightly sour. The daging salai was a bit stiff. There were a few menu items that didn’t arrive, and I had to ask at the counter , hopefully in the future, the staff can check with customers whether all food has been served. The creamy tomyam might be the café’s signature dish; the taste was unique, although at first I couldn’t really tell it was tomyam. The staff were also a bit loud chatting at the front. Overall, I would still come again to support and hope for improvement. Thank you for the food and service provided.

Name: Cherofee Coffee And Sandwich Smoke (Coffee Besut)
Review_Text:
accidentally came here after three cafes i wanted to go to closed without prior notice (it was agong holiday)

let me tell you — IM SHOOK. the food??? all solid chefs kiss.

the first food to arrive was carbonara spaghetti. i was skeptical at first since the presentation was so-so. but i was mind blown by the taste. creamy, salty and cheese-y. 8.5/10 for this pasta. just slightly sweet to my liking.

then ikan keli salai masak lemak cili api with ulam & nasi putih. so so good too. when i first entered the cafe, everyone was eating their masakan melayu dish. so i bet it’s good and it really is good. a solid 9.

third one was chicken chop. thought it’s just a silly chicken chop with crumbs but they proved me wrong again. haha so good yeee. the crumb was crispy and airy bit, and meat was juicy. hua how come is everything so good??? 8/10.

last dish was nasi goreng daging salai. SOOO SMOKEYYYY. makan tu memang dapat rasa smokey dia and meat was tender as well. sambal pun sedap. 9/10.

drinks however, the ice lemon tea particularly — very disappointing huhu. instead of real lemon juice, they used the bottled lime (the famous one from thailand) sebab rasa pedih dekat tekak and rasa similar macam air limau kedai kedai makan siam guna.

but overall, im satisfied. i wanted to try their beef sandwich tapi sold out on the day i went there. will definitely visit again if ada rezeki kesana.
Cleaned_Text: accidentally came three cafes wanted go closed without prior notice agong holiday let tell — im shook food solid chefs kiss first food arrive carbonara spaghetti skeptical first since presentation soso mind blown taste creamy salty cheesey pasta slightly sweet liking ikan keli salai masak lemak cili api ulam nasi putih good first entered cafe everyone eating masakan melayu dish bet it’s good really good solid third one chicken chop thought it’s silly chicken chop crumbs proved wrong haha good yeee crumb crispy airy bit meat juicy hua come everything good last dish nasi goreng daging salai sooo smokeyyyy makan tu memang dapat rasa smokey meat tender well sambal sedap drinks however ice lemon tea particularly — disappointing huhu instead real lemon juice used bottled lime famous one thailand sebab rasa pedih dekat tekak rasa similar macam air limau kedai kedai makan siam guna overall im satisfied wanted try beef sandwich sold day went definitely visit rezeki kesana

Name: Monkey Hut Cafe & Bed
Review_Text:
What to expect from this hostel?

Don't expect to get an early check-in, it's 2pm not before.
You need to give 100MYR deposit in cash for the towel and bed linen.

Import info: THERE IS NO LOCKER AT ALL IN THE HOSTEL, THE DOOR OF THE DORM DOES NOT LOCK AND AT NIGHT MANY EXTERNAL PEOPLE COME TO EAT AND PARTY AT THE BAR.

The mixed dorm is Spartan:
- 20 beds in 15m2
- the floor has been through WW2 and is as sandy as the beach of the D-Day (or Long Beach if you don't know much history)
- the room is mined: empty bottles of water or sunscreens, abandoned clothes, dirty tissues... maybe soon we will find Private Ryan. 🤷
- in order to preserve this UNESCO historical place, nothing gets cleaned or removed, who knows the trauma of the war that all those generations of empty bottles hold.
-The beds don't have curtains or lights, so get ready to be woken up by your roommates who use their mobile torch like a lighthouse.
- there is no shelf at all, your mattress will become your shelf/wardrobe... If you don't care just put everything underneath the bed so your stuff can keep company to the ancient trash
-There are 4 sockets close to the door to share with 20 people....
- the AC is decent, but with 20 people it's not enough.
- at night the manslaughter starts: mosquitoes bombard you without pity.

- The showers are on the ground floor, like in trenches, don't expect comfort: each of the 3 showers is inside the toilet, a foot away from the toilet (common in South East Asia) but there is no hot water nor toilet paper.
since this place is a restaurant and a late bar, you get also all the external clients to use them... Be strategic about the time of your shower if you want the floor to be clean.

It's noisy, extremely noisy, the bar blares music until 2 am and then you get the drunken loud clients who don't want to leave the place and still shouts until 3am.

---- on a positive note-----
- you can't be closer to the beach
- the free breakfast is good
- lovely staff
- good atmosphere

Forgive my sarcasm in this comment, it was my best comrade during my stay .

Review_Text:

Those of you guys who happen to be in the area, go try them out. You won't regret it. 😋👍

Name: Rosemarry Cafe
Review_Text:
We came here once for lunch and once for dinner. We were staying at Marriott and this restaurant is within walking distance from Marriott’s gate. It’s the first and only restaurant you see if you are on Marriott’s jetty.

During lunch, it was a lot less busier than dinner so we got our food quickly. Service is friendly but we did wait longer than we would have liked for dinner. Nevertheless, the food is delicious. Their juices are not sweetened but served cold. Portion size is generous. We were surprised that we got 1/2 a crab in the mixed seafood fried noodles which also came with shrimp and calamari. Everything was yummy. For dinner, you can pick your own fish to be grilled by them. They had a few variety.

I wish their menu was a bit more descriptive. I would’ve ordered more! Their rosemary fried rice is a popular choice so I’m told by them and did not expect it to come with fried chicken breasts and a fried egg with soup. It was a nice surprise.

Watch out for your feet! Some of the tables had nails sticking out under the table where a middle bar was supposed to be. A bit dangerous so please be careful.

If you are lucky you could spot black tip sharks right outside.

My first time there, I ordered a grilled salmon and waited 35 minutes before telling them I would like to take it away. After that, the lazy waitress came to me and said they were out of salmon. Wasted my time.

My second time there, I waited 45 minutes before getting my order of honey lemon and fried mantou buns.

My third time there, the sole male staff attended to us while 3-4 female staff were at the counter looking at their phones. We specifically asked for something quick, and the waiter recommended a rice dish. I ordered half-boiled eggs. 10 minutes later, the female waitstaff came to the table saying they were out of rice. We asked to cancel whatever items weren't prepared. Even the half-boiled egg was not started.

Avoid this place and don't fall for the lighting from outside, its interior has poor ambiance from the poorly-curated furnishing and cramped layout, plus the service staff are dreadful.

Name: Keropok Lekor Zaki
Review_Text:
When I went to Pulau Perhentian, I was really hoping to find the best keropok lekor in Kuala Besut, since I couldn’t make a stop in Kuala Terengganu. After reading several reviews, I decided to get it from Keropok Lekor Zaki.

The shop was closed on Tuesday, so I wasn’t sure if I could still buy any. I decided to call the number listed, and the seller—who was a very kind lady—answered. She confirmed that the shop was closed, but kindly offered to deliver the keropok lekor to me at the Kuala Besut Jetty, without any extra delivery charges. I was so grateful for her kindness!

Since the shop was closed, only the frozen (keropok lekor gelap) version was available, not the fresh ones. I bought it around 4 PM, and after a long overnight journey, I reached home the next morning at 7 AM. I was a bit worried about the condition, but once I fried it, I was so impressed—it was absolutely delicious.

I’ve tried keropok lekor from other shops before, and many of them use too much flour. But this one was clearly different. It was packed with real fish, with just the right amount of chewiness, and the taste was so authentic. I also bought the dipping sauce, which was tasty and very affordable.

The seller was incredibly friendly. If you’re ever in Kuala Besut, I highly recommend buying from Keropok Lekor Zaki. I’ll definitely return next time. Thank you for the wonderful service and amazing keropok lekor!

Note: The picture was taken during our teatime

Name: Mee Champa Rokandis
Review_Text:
Driving along the scenic coastal road from Kuala Terengganu to Besut is an experience in itself, but what truly elevates this journey is the promise of indulging in a steaming bowl of Mee Champa. As someone who appreciates authentic flavors, this stop turned out to be a hidden gem worth every kilometer of the drive.
What sets this dish apart is its topping. Generous portions of tender beef slices, fresh herbs, and crunchy bean sprouts create a harmony of textures. For those who like a little kick, the sambal served on the side adds an irresistible heat, making the dish even more satisfying.
This was more than just a meal—it was a journey into the rich culinary heritage of the region. If you're ever in Terengganu, make it a point to take this drive. The Mee Champa isn't just food; it's a celebration of flavor and culture that you’ll remember long after the last spoonful. Highly recommended!
Cleaned_Text: driving along scenic coastal road kuala terengganu besut experience itself truly elevates journey promise indulging steaming bowl mee champa someone appreciates authentic flavors stop turned hidden gem worth every kilometer drive sets dish apart topping generous portions tender beef slices fresh herbs crunchy bean sprouts create harmony textures like little kick sambal served side adds irresistible heat making dish even satisfying meal—it journey rich culinary heritage region youre ever terengganu make point take drive mee champa isnt food celebration flavor culture you’ll remember long last spoonful highly recommended

Name: Perhentian Idaman Cafe
Review_Text: The taste of the food and its portion is quite decent, not too horrible. The setting of the place is like any regular warung on the side of the road in Malaysia, so its okay since we are used to it. However, as a tourist which are a fellow local people like them, i feel a bit sad and insulted  as they treat the foreigner customers better than us. For example, they serve a foreigner's table in the middle of taking our order, and 1 person got their food way earlier than us even though we have ordered first and have waited for almost 40 minutes at that point. The waiting time was too long we almost cancelled our order. I hope they will treat all of the customers equally next time. Kita support kita.

Name: Tuna Bay Restaurant
Review_Text:
I had the 4 days 3 nights package (see photo of itinerary).

Staff smoke nearby in the non-smoking zones and it makes sitting near the exterior very uncomfortable.

Avoid all the western food haha!

They accommodate allergens (can remove eggs from fried dishes). Sometimes you must ask twice (such is normal and understandable).

I would avoid the package deal that gives food since they tend to serve an imitation of a western meal called chicken chop for lunch that is not good. People having it looked sad and it has no flavour lol.

Good asian dishes: The mee goreng and char kuay teow are good! So are the sweet and sour fish, Malay curry, roti canai, satay, bbq.

Juices; The passionate and the juice with pineapple+apple are both good. Sometimes they are lukewarm and not cold though.

Name: Tuna Bay Restaurant
Review_Text: Their kampung fried rice which was supposed to be kampung style tasted like the typical Chinese fried rice. But then I played it safe and went for their spaghetti seafood aglio olio, which was also a mistake. The overwhelming black pepper flavor and spiciness made me lose my appetite and upset my stomach. Overall, I left feeling disappointed and would not recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for good value for their money.

This humble warung offers incredibly budget-friendly options, with drinks starting from just RM1 and local dishes available for as low as RM3. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy delicious local food on a budget while visiting Kuala Besut.

Name: Nasi Kukus Alom
Review_Text:
Nasi Kukus Alom has really nice food – the nasi kukus itself is tasty, the gravy is good, and overall it feels satisfying, especially when eaten hot. It’s the kind of place you’d happily come back to for the food.

The barista team is super friendly – they keep checking on us, asking if everything is okay, making sure our drinks are fine. That part really made us feel welcomed and taken care of.

However, I really hope the other servers and counter staff can improve their attitude. The way some of them looked at us felt a bit uncomfortable, like we were “weird” just because we’re not local Terengganu people. Being a “foreigner” to the area doesn’t mean we should be stared at like that. A simple smile or neutral face would already feel much better.

So for me:
•	✅ Nice nasi kukus, tasty food
•	✅ Barista very friendly and attentive
•	❌ Other servers & counter staff need to be more friendly and open, especially towards non-locals

If they can make the whole team as warm as the barista, Nasi Kukus Alom would be a place I’d confidently recommend to everyone, not just for the food but for the overall experience.

Name: The Barat Restoran
Review_Text:
The Barat Restoran is one of the better places to eat on Perhentian, though you’ll notice the prices are a bit higher than most restaurants around. That said, the food does taste better, so you kind of get what you pay for.

If you’re here, definitely go for the BBQ – they’ve got a wider selection of seafood than anywhere else, and it’s really fresh. Just make sure you head over early because it’s super popular and tends to run out fast.

The only downside for me was the service. Some staff had trouble with English so our orders got a bit mixed up, and a few of them just hung around instead of greeting guests or handing out menus. Food = great, service = could be better.

Name: The Barat Restoran
Review_Text:
Visited in April 2025:

We had dinner there and tried the BBQ. We had red snapper and tiger gambas bbq’ed. 🍤🐟Particularly the tiger prawns were very tasty. The red snapper was ok, maybe a bit too dry. As side dishes we ordered some veggie and rice. The price of the fish was ok, the tiger prawns a bit pricey, around 50RM each.

For dessert, we shared the Oreo cheesecake, nice and sweet.

Interestingly Barat Tioman and Barat Perenthian have the same concept and also the menu is very similar, also the design of the accommodation.

Excellent service and attentive staff!

Also, we tried the Singapore breakfast noodles, very yummy  🍜

Name: The Barat Restoran
Review_Text:
Food:
1.Aioli Olio was decent but flavor was simple and not garlic as described.
2.Chicken Burger: the burger came out quite late and felt like a sponge, meaning squishy and wet. Perhaps was a frozen burger that was undercooked but not edible.

Beverage:
1.Latte are quite sweet and come from a Keurig dispenser type machine. Not a latte with frothed milk fyi.

Service:
After pointing out the burger taste and texture issue, I ordered Fried Barat with chicken and was edible if nothing special. However, the attitude of needing to fix it, not clearing the table and leaving a large number of plates and cups was unprofessional.
The worst part was when I went to check out, they didn’t initially refund the burger and I found an unrelated item added to the bill.

I came to Malaysia to enjoy my time, and the scuba diving staff nearby have been great. But the food staff and general lack of professionalism is bothersome.

Tips to improve: use better quality food, train to have responsive staff who aren’t so busy playing with the local squirrels as they are the customers and take feedback from reviewers more seriously.

F&B post COVID must be a tough business, but this one feels stuck in the past. Doubtful I’d ever return.

Review_Text:

The soup is ok, a bit salty and you can taste the MSG..the firt you sip it. For those who dislike salty soup..I dont recommend this.
Their famous Cendol..I ordered the ordinary cendol means the cheapest one..honestly, I'm a bit dissapointed..with RM4 for a bowl..just not worth it..sorry.
Location is easy, just across the Government Clinic of Kuala Besut. Cleanliness obviously needs to be improved..I didnt see any washroom here too.
Service was okay by the way...pls be reminded..Mee Stambong will takes a little extra time to be prepared so be patient for 15 minutes than you can have all the protein plus carbohydrate deliciously..

Name: Sri Tanjung Restaurant
Review_Text:
For the most part, it's hard to expect culinary excellence from island eateries, regardless of the price on the menu. The cooks at this particular establishment would do well to take a refresher course in cooking, but to be honest having read all the reviews prior, there was little surprise and we knew what we signed up for.

Still a good place to chill, play with the squirrels which roam all over the place, while enjoying the sea breeze and ocean view. The location is also super convenient and strategic, being straddled in between, and thus having easy access to, both sides of the island's beautiful beaches.

Dahlah salah punch in order, kitorg order frappe + whipped cream (add on 1.50), tapi punch in as hot drink+whipped cream, sampai nya hot drink je and no whipped cream. Kalau tak gi kaunter minta tukar, free free je duit kitorg bayar whipped cream masuk poket diorg. Its not the amount that matters, but your honesty in doing business what really matters. I am so frustrated dengan business tak jujur mcm ni.

Masa makan tu, kitorg order lagi air for take away tapi dah lama sgt menunggu and we had somewhere to go, we asked for a refund for that drink sebab air tu tak buat lagi, but they refused to refund and baru nak buat air tu. Name: Ombak Cafe
Review_Text:
I write this review as someone who booked a package with Ombak Resort which included breakfast, lunch and dinner. Breakfast was buffet style and repetitive - same thing every morning. Good vegetarian options but almost no vegan options besides fruit, beans, cereals and sometimes soy milk. Not a great start to the day, especially when you go diving afterwards, for which you need fuel.
For lunch and dinner you can choose from a very limited menu which is the same every day. It includes a drink (also limited choice) and some fruit for dessert (always watermelon). The main course options are good for vegetarians but there are no vegan options. You can ask for vegan food but the staff makes you feel as if this is a hassle. I got the same dish for every meal during my 4 day stay - so I ate the same dish 8x. It was always veggies with tofu. Sometimes with sauce, sometimes without. One night I was served plain white rice with a slice of cucumber. This was very disappointing.

Besides that, the restaurant has a big menu but prices are incredibly high. I read many comments about long waiting times but from what I observed, this isn’t the case. A nice touch is that they play two movies every night. But then again: drinks ans food are so expensive that you don’t want to splurge on that too much. The staff often looks like they hate their job, almost no smiles etc. I recommend Ombak Resort for staying there but I can’t recommend the Café/restaurant at all.

Name: You&Me Thai Street And Western
Review_Text: Dropped by this place last week for dinner and tried quite a few things. I had the maggi tomyam belut — big portion, thick flavourful broth, and they were generous with the eel cuts. The seafood kerabu was nicely tangy, with a good amount of prawns and squid, though it would've been nicer with more veggies instead of so much sliced onion. Also got the beef burger and fries set to go — taste-wise, the patty could be improved a bit, kind of reminded me of the typical roadside burger stall. As for the ABC, the flavour was good and packed with toppings, but the shaved ice was a little coarse, so it wasn’t as satisfying to eat. Overall, the portion sizes are decent and the price is still reasonable.

Name: Ohlala Divers
Review_Text:
Amazing diving center for first time divers. We planned to stay 3 days and ended up staying 2 weeks.

The diving school is located at one of the best free snorkeling spots, where you can see a lot of baby sharks and hundreds of fish. You literally walk down into the water from the school’s entrance. And if you dive here you can borrow the snorkel for free.

We did open water and advanced open water at Ohlala divers. The instructors were great (thanks Mica!)

Is still hard to believe that this is the cheapest place to get your diving license in Asia, while it’s providing so much value: some of the best diving spots in the world, with sharks and turtles, amazing staff and instructors, accommodation with great views. and free accommodation if you get your license here or if you do 2 fun dives. For only 20 dollars per fun dive.

You can also spot some cool wildlife in the evening: geckos, monitor lizards, owls. And very cute cats.

Infrastructure is what I expected on an island dive school : outside showers without hot water and bungalow’s without ac. But it still feels comfortable. And no WiFi, which means people actually interact with each other.

Extra advantage : It’s located next to the pier on Long Beach, which means you don’t have to carry your luggage on the beach.

Name: Ohlala Divers
Review_Text: Oh La La is about beautiful dives done while staying in a scenic, jungle setting. But most of all it's about the people. I felt encouraged, taken care of, and learned so much about the ocean's inhabitants. All the instructors are skilled professionals who take the time to help each student progress further. Come to the island, let go of time perception, and join this family full of real characters. Thank you to everyone for your kindness. Especially Aris, Idan, Mira, Alex, Amir, Mica, Iwan, Didi, Marwan, Adi, Lee and Lucy. You are all complete gems 💎

Name: Ohlala Divers
Review_Text:
I recently spent a week at Ohlala Divers on the Perhentian Islands, and I can honestly say it’s one of the best dive center I ever experienced.

From the moment I arrived, the warm, personalized attention made me feel right at home. The team is incredibly professional, always ready to help. Dive groups are just 4 divers per instructor.
Simple and clean accommodation, mosquito proof windows, fan. Good vienes.
drinking water is available for free.
A generous breakfast: egg and toast, coffee and tea all day available.

Good equipment. high-quality fins, and Instructors emphasize safety above all, and you really feel in expert hands. Even better, courses and fun dives are surprisingly affordable excellent value for money compared to anywhere else in the region.
If you’re heading to the Perhentian Islands and want exceptional diving, friendly, expert staff, and clean, comfortable lodging at a budget price, Ohlala Divers should top your list. Highly recommended

Name: Ohlala Divers
Review_Text:
I stayed here for 3 nights with my friend and would not recommend.

This is mainly due to the poor organisation of the hostel. The workers were very friendly and the guys in the kitchen were especially friendly and kind. However, we weren’t able to book all of our dives (that the hostel had confirmed on watsapp before we arrived) because of the hostel’s poor organisation and communication.

This hostel is very reasonably priced (especially for the island and great location) and was very simple. However, the rooms were not clean and the bathrooms were a walk away up a hill, with manual toilet flushes and were not clean. The scuba equipment was old and worn out, with rips/holes in the BCDs.

The dive sites were good but I didn’t feel safe on my second dive, as our guide rarely checked in on us and swam ahead of us, so we had to work hard to keep up. (Me and my friend are both qualified at an advanced level, so we are capable divers but didn’t enjoy having to chase after the guide for the whole dive). He also swam through lots of very enclosed swim throughs (which I had never done before) sometimes without looking back or checking in on us. I felt very uneasy and uncomfortable.

The worst part about Oh La La is the caged monkey sat right in the middle of the hostel (presumably belonging to the owner), who roams the hostel on a leash at night.

I would not recommend this hostel. You should pay more to stay somewhere nicer.

Name: Ohlala Divers
Review_Text:
Couldn't have picked a better dive school to do my Advance course on the Perhentians. You get your board free and breakfast every morning. If you pay and extra 40 ringgit you get your own private as pictured in the screenshot. (Worth it!)

Location was great as you could see the pier that you get dropped off at from Kuala Besut as soon as you reach the island. It also has access to a really unique marine diverse reef right at the foot of the academy where you can hire snorkels for free with the diveschool.

To finish off the service was fantastic! Aris the owner of the dive school who is a very nice and funny guy who made me and everyone feel very welcome and will smash you at Chess given the oppertunity. Then there was Felix who helped me complete my Course. He is really patient, fun and incredibly talented at diving! If you have the right attitude he will teach you a thing or two about bouyancy!

Thank you for having me. I will be referring you to everyone who even mentions coming to the Perhentians!

Craig

Name: Temu Primula Hotel
Review_Text: The best place to stay at when in Kuala Besut. A huge property with big rooms, some of them having bathtubs too. Very close to beach as well as jetty. There is a cosy in-house cafe, a big restaurant and nice garden cafe in the premises. Had dinner and breakfast, and the food was prepared and served with lot of care and love. Had a wonderful stopover night stay in the hotel before our journey to Perhentian.|And the best part of this hotel is its Manager, Areef, who is gem of a person, and I have hardly met someone as helpful and friendly as him... He always speaks with a lovely smile on his face, always ready to help with anything one needs, drove us to Kuala Besit market for shopping, again came back to pick us up for return to hotel. Next day, he dropped us at jetty for our onward boat journey to Perhentian. He also helped us in booking boat as well as arranged car to airport on our return from Perhentian at a good discounted rate.|And the most generous gesture by him was calling his friend during our dinner, and they together played live music only for us that included some Bollywood numbers too, making the entire ambience very pleasing.|Rarely in our trips we are touched so closely by someone as Mr. Areef, and his hospitality for that one day will remain unforgettable forever.

Food: 4.5/5

Chicken (can’t remember the exact name of the dish, but it was the one with the coconut spice sauce) 4/5

It was really good but I think the flavours aren’t suited for my palate. I think others who like that type of gravy would really love it!

Chocolate cake dessert: 4/5 - a bit of a rich chocolate cake with a nice crunch!

Service: 5/5 - very friendly and quick to serve!

Ambiance: 5/5 - a well kept isolated spot on the island. It’s super cool and a lovely area. Would have loved to come here for dinner again or even stay on the resort.

Name: Long Beach Cafe Espresso
Review_Text:
The coffee was just average and the staff had a very bad attitude, not friendly at all. On top of that, they charged an extra 3% for card payment, which is against the Bank Negara rules!

Hi, thank you for your explanation. While I appreciate the transparency, the issue is not about disclosure. The MDR is a business cost and should not be passed on to consumers. On the island, card payments are often the most reliable option due to connectivity issues, and customers should not be penalised for using them. Considering that an iced latte is already priced at RM19, which is a premium amount, adding a card fee makes the experience unfair to customers.

Name: Restoran The Dapo
Review_Text:
The lamb chop is marinated in sambal-percik like marination. Not stated in the description of the menu. Was hoping for lamb with a simple black pepper and salt. Mashed potato is a bit gritty from what I assumed is the instant potato flakes. Black pepper sauce a bit runny. Side vegs is goood

Tomyam pasta a bit tasteless on the tomyam. Spicy korean wings are quite small for the price. Had burnt taste at the end.

Mac & Cheese (not in pic) is about small foil size (around 3x6inch?). A bit too strong on the garlic and not so much on cheese. The mac is overcooked.

Some of the workers were playing videos out of their phones, making the atmosphere a bit ‘cheap.’

Review_Text:

The ordering until the payment process was done efficiently. You pay at the cashier counter and submit your order as well. The waiting time for them to fulfill my order is surprisingly fast even though they have many customers. Thumbs up Manis Cafe!
I have ordered Pineapple Asam Boi (RM7.90), well I don't really like it because it is too sweet, Hot Latte (RM8.90), good brew coffee & smoked duck cream sauce (RM24.90), the duck was cooked well and the amount is reasonable big and I can share with my husband (since I'm not a big eater). The taste is superb! Glad that I'm taking my time to try it out. Verdict, I will be a repeat customer for Manis Cafe because the ambience, food, service are good. Furthermore, they don't charge SST, all included in the menu prices. I would say, it's damn cheap compared to my favorite restaurant here in Dungun. Well done! Keep up your good work 👍🏼

2nd visit - I tried chicken parmigiana (24.9) & lamb chop (32.90). Both are very tasty 🫰🏼. I also tried the dessert, well since I don't like something sweet, I would say the biscoff puff is quite nice . My rating remains 5*

Name: Restaurant C.B.Wee
Review_Text:
Restaurant C.B. Wee – Disappointing!

When I travel on business, I often rely on Google reviews. Chinese non halal pork free restaurant. With 300+ reviews averaging 4.3 stars, this place looked promising. Sadly, I was conned again.

We arrived at 11 a.m. to an empty restaurant—just us. Staff were busy prepping in the kitchen. Ordering was simple: the friendly server hands you a menu and you write down your choices. Chice if meat and how you want it prepared. So far, so good.

The Food (or lack thereof):
•	Deep Fried Cuttlefish: Fresh, yes. Flavor, no. Bland and forgettable.
•	Ha Look Prawns: Six medium prawns, fresh but soggy. Honestly, I believe I could’ve cooked them better myself.
•	Lemon Chicken: How anyone can butcher such a simple dish is beyond me. Starchy chicken cutlets drowned in lemon “slime.” Disgusting.
•	Fried Meehoon: The worst dish I’ve had in my life—period. Burnt, starchy, and painful to finish. I never waste food, but this one was a test of willpower. Lesson in life: not all 4.3 stars are created equal.
•	Seafood Fried Rice: First two bites seemed fine, then it fell apart—unevenly cooked, bland, prawns not at their freshest.

The Rest:
•	No AC, but parking is aplenty.
•	Bill for all the above + two iced Chinese teas: RM75. Reasonable price, unreasonable regret.
•	The server was the only saving grace—smiling, polite, and attentive throughout. Kudos to her.

Verdict: One of the biggest letdowns of my food-hunting life. If you value your taste buds (and sanity), skip this restaurant at all costs.

Review_Text:
Pork-free Chinese restaurant near Tanjung Jara resort. As the only restaurant in the vicinity, it surprised us with its well-cooked dishes.

The friendly waitress recommend the prawn cooked in Har Lok style. Unlike the typical Har Lok flavor with minimal visible sauce, their version had a slightly saucier consistency that was irresistibly delicious. It was addictively good, making it a standout dish.

We also tried the lemon chicken, which turned out to be quite tasty. The tomyum and tofu stir-fry with vegetables were decent choices as well that satisfied our palates.

The prices are reasonable, you can enjoy a fulfilling meal without breaking the bank.

Review_Text:
Mak Wan Mee Sup Melayu Champa – Malay noodles served hot!

Passed by this restaurant a few times and noticed it was always packed, so we decided to give it a try. The place was exceptionally busy. Find a table, queue to order, pay on the spot, and quote your table number for the food to be delivered. We ordered mee champa daging, teh O ice and satay.

Mee champa daging came with a flavorful, aromatic broth and generous slices of beef. In case you’re curious about the white powder on the side — it’s actually sugar! Personally, the broth was already perfect as is. No extra seasoning needed, especially not sugar.

The drinks were generic and nothing much to shout about.

Satay was a letdown. Though claimed to be from Kajang, it certainly didn’t taste like it. The meat was too lean, resulting in dry, bland skewers. The peanut sauce was overly sweet and lacked the richness of real peanuts.

The restaurant has no AC, but thanks to its large seating area, high ceiling and plenty of fans, it stays surprisingly comfortable. I also liked how they stack the noodles with a plate separation when bringing it to the table — practical and neat.

- Basically it is a roti john and it taste very nice... Big portion and comes with fries too ..

- The chicken taste wonderful and juicy... The black pepper sauce is okayh...
5. Taxes BBQ Lamb (Rm 29.90)
- Very juicy and tender... It is nice but the sauce bit sweet for me, but who cares I finished it 😜

- The lamb is juicy and tender, the sauce is nice too...

- Bit dry but the taste is okayh...
8. Mushroom Soup (Rm 6.90)
- This one is superb...
9. French fries (Rm 6.90)
- A standard fries
10. Lamb Fried Rice (Rm 26.90)
- Very nice 👍🏻
11. Mixed Grilled (Rm 39.90)
- You'll get a piece of lamb, chicken and beef... The mint sauce is very nice!

The portion for each food is okayh.. For the spaghetti, the portion is enough for 1 person... The rest can up to 2 person depends on appetite 😬 For big eater like me, I can eat one menu alone...

Ohh the drinks is good...  For price wise it is okayh.... For fruit juice you can get it at Rm 6.90...

Name: Kakilima.My
Review_Text:
We’ve had the pleasure of dining at Kakilima three times now, and each visit has been an absolute delight. As French visitors and passionate food lovers — my husband being a chef himself — we are always looking for authenticity, quality, and heart in a meal. Kakilima offers all of that and more.

A special mention to the chef, whose mastery of meat and sauces is truly exceptional. The flavors are bold, perfectly balanced, and deeply satisfying. My husband was so impressed, he already plans to adapt one of their sauces with a French twist back home — the mark of true inspiration!

We would also like to sincerely thank the wonderful service team, especially Dira, for their warmth, professionalism, and hospitality. Each visit felt like coming home — a rare feeling when dining abroad.

Congratulations to the entire team for their hard work, their passion, and for offering such high-quality products. Kakilima is more than just a restaurant — it’s an experience we’ll remember fondly.

We highly recommend this place to anyone looking to truly enjoy themselves during their travels in Malaysia. You won’t be disappointed.

Merci et bravo!

Name: Kakilima.My
Review_Text:
I arrived at the eatery slightly past the noonday mark, roughly two hours after noon, expecting a bustling lunch crowd. To my pleasant surprise, the ambiance was serene, almost contemplative, with just enough quiet to savour the moment with disruption.

I opted for the nachos and pasta buttermilk and I must confess, the nachos caught me off guard. What I had presumed would be a casual snack & go turned out to be an exquisitely curated dish. Visually enticing and abundantly portioned, it was evident from presentation alone that this was no mere appetiser. A solid 10/10.

The pasta buttermilk, requested non-spicy, and the kitchen honoured my preference. The accompanying crispy chicken was tender to the point effortlessly cuttable with a spoon. The buttermilk sauce was subtle, mellow, and notably unsweetened.

What impressed me most, however, was the evolution. I have dined here before, and candidly, once vowed not to return due to a string of underwhelming, tasteless menu. But this visit marks a noticeable and praiseworthy transformation. Whoever is behind this shift, consider this review as a quiet ovation.

Name: Aroma Kitchen
Review_Text:
Arriving around 8 pm, I wasn't particularly hungry but fancied a quick bite and some relaxation with a view and the beach breeze. The place seemed cozy from my perspective, with few customers at this hour. It was clean overall; I used the restroom within the restaurant, which was well-maintained, and as I passed through the kitchen area, it appeared tidy as well.

Now, let's talk about the food. The Nasi Goreng Cina was rather plain, lacking substantial ingredients like chicken or assorted veggies. It consisted mainly of peas and egg, though we were fortunate to have ordered a sunny-side-up egg on the side. Next, I ordered a grilled chicken salad. The chicken didn't seem freshly grilled, likely due to being cold. It would have been preferable if it had been heated before serving. As for the sauce, it seemed to be a blend of barbecue sauce and possibly some Thousand Island dressing, judging by the taste.

If you're prioritizing taste, I'd advise thinking twice before choosing this place. However, if you're on a tight budget, it might be worth a try. My meal, including plain water, came to around MYR 25.

Name: Nelayan Restoran Pulau Serai (Misi Azlina)
Review_Text:
We were invited to have dinner at Nelayan Restaurant by Mr. Terrence and Mr. Ramesh on our last night in Tanjong Jara. We arrived around 8.30 pm, and most of the other guests had already left after having dinner early. The restaurant is open-air, so it was a bit warm at night, but they provided stand fans to help cool us down.

We ordered the Angus steak and seafood pasta, and they were incredibly delicious. The only problem was that they didn't have a menu for kids, so I asked if they could make a simple carbonara pasta for my son. Unfortunately, it turned out a bit dry. So, if you want something not on the menu, be careful when ordering.

The special smoothies we had, the Bumbung, and Anjung smoothies, were really good. They were made with unique ingredients like roselle syrup, banana, and cucumber, which gave them a tangy and refreshing taste.

The waiters were very attentive and took good care of us. At night, we could also enjoy watching a bonfire from a distance, which added to the pleasant atmosphere.

The only thing that could be better is if they had a wider variety of food on the menu, like standard pastas and burgers, to give more choices to customers.

Overall, our experience at Nelayan Restaurant was enjoyable, especially with the delicious Angus steak, tasty seafood pasta, and delightful special smoothies. With a few improvements to their menu options, the restaurant could become even more popular and appealing to different tastes.

Name: Singgang Ulam Budu
Review_Text:

I opted for a regular seafood selection with a drink, which cost less than RM20 per person—quite reasonable, especially with a beachfront view.

Name: Singgang Ulam Budu
Review_Text:
The food at the restaurant was delicious, and the price was reasonable. However, our experience was slightly affected by a delay in my order due to a mix-up. While they did replace the order, the delay tested our patience a little, as my rice had already finished by the time my turn came.

On a brighter note, the customer service during the ordering process was excellent, and the staff was polite and attentive. Before arriving, we walked from our hotel by the seaside, around 800 meters. The night was windy and airy, and the pleasant weather made the walk to the restaurant very enjoyable. My child also had a great time playing on the swing at the restaurant, which added a fun touch to the visit.

Overall, it was a nice evening, and while the food service could improve, we appreciated the good food and the relaxing atmosphere.

We came here for dinner and the restaurant was quite busy, most of the tables were taken.  We saw an empty table and sat ourselves down as the owner was very busy.  We noticed that most of the customers knew each others as they would greet each other, which is good sign as local people also come here to eat.  The owner then came to take order when he was a little free.  So there was no menu actually, we just told the owner what we wanted to eat, such as fish or prawn, and he recommended what to order.   The waiting time for food to be served was a bit long as there was only one person cooking but the customers all looked very patient.  When the food was finally served, we were happy to know that the food was not very salty like other local restaurants.  We ordered the signature tofu dish but were underwhelmed.  Squid dish was tender and fresh.  The giant prawn was ok.  Loved the steamed grouper, very fresh and delicate.  With the price difference between giant prawn and steamed grouper, one giant prawn is more expensive than one steamed grouper, we would definitely go for the fish next time.
Cleaned_Text: stayed resort nearby wanted fresh seafood many options neighbourhood place looked like good choice came dinner restaurant quite busy tables taken saw empty table sat owner busy noticed customers knew others would greet good sign local people also come eat owner came take order little free menu actually told owner wanted eat fish prawn recommended order waiting time food served bit long one person cooking customers looked patient food finally served happy know food salty like local restaurants ordered signature tofu dish underwhelmed squid dish tender fresh giant prawn ok loved steamed grouper fresh delicate price difference giant prawn steamed grouper one giant prawn expensive one steamed grouper would definitely go fish next time

Name: Di Atas Sungei
Review_Text:
The restaurant setting is magnificent. It's wooden structure, traditional furnishing, strategical layout truly delivers a nice ambience.

But what is all that, when food struggles to impress. Poor tasting, lack of thought, fairly uninteresting, it serves disappointment. Although certain items on their breakfast was acceptable, the rest seems below par. Like spreading salt to an open wound, the hefty toll to pay to crossing this road of low quality food is not cheap either.

Hope that the management can put in extra effort to ensure the quality of items served can be monitored as the place already has what it takes to build an impression.

Name: Keropok Cap Bot Nelayan
Review_Text:
Keropok Cap Nelayan – a plethora of Terengganu favourites!

Asked a few locals which keropok lekor they swear by, and KCP kept coming up. This stall offers a wide spread of local staples — keropok lekor, noodles, rice dishes, vegetable sides, meat dishes and even dried keropok varieties. You can sample almost everything Terengganu-style in one stop.

Visited twice, and each time I tried something different.

Keropok lekor – some versions taste more like flour sticks… but not this one. You can clearly taste the fish. Soft inside, crispy outside, and made fresh daily.

Satar goreng – basically satar wrapped in popiah skin and deep fried. Great when hot, but turns a bit fishy once cold.

Mihun sup daging – a must-order. Broth is flavorful and spicy, just the way I love it. Beef chunks are tasty (though slightly chewy) and they’re generous with the meat. Big portion too.

The place has no AC, but plenty of fans to keep things comfortable. Everything is self-service: pick your food and drinks, queue to pay, then enjoy.

Located conveniently by the roadside with ample parking. Hard to miss, and worth the stop.

Name: Restaurant Lian Fong Yuen
Review_Text:
Lian Fong Yuen – charming seafood restaurant.

Was craving big prawns after my morning pasar shopping and stumbled upon this place. As soon as I walked in, I saw two tables having the same noodle dish topped with giant prawns — that sealed the deal for me. We ordered Sang Har Yee Mee, fried rice with char siew, wat tan hor, and two bottles of sugarcane juice. They also sells mixed rice here but cold though.

The Sang Har Yee Mee came with large, fresh prawns — perfectly cooked, juicy, and full of natural sweetness. Definitely the highlight of the meal. The noodles, however, were slightly bland and could use a touch more seasoning to bring out the prawn gravy’s richness. RM60

The char siew fried rice was solid — full of wok hei and well-balanced in flavor. On the other hand, the wat tan hor suffered the same fate as the Yee Mee — lacking in wok hei and a bit too mild for my taste.

Sugarcane - served in a recycled tiger beer bottle. Tasted like how my mom would’ve made it.

The restaurant has ceiling fans, so it’s reasonably comfortable. Parking was fairly easy. Manned by a friendly Chinese couple who run things efficiently. Food came out quickly and piping hot. I also suspect there’s a swallow house nearby, as the bird call plays repeatedly throughout the meal — a familiar sound in this area. Cleanliness could be improved a little, but overall it’s a charming, no-frills spot worth visiting for the prawns alone.
Cleaned_Text: lian fong yuen – charming seafood restaurant craving big prawns morning pasar shopping stumbled upon place soon walked saw two tables noodle dish topped giant prawns — sealed deal ordered sang har yee mee fried rice char siew wat tan hor two bottles sugarcane juice also sells mixed rice cold though sang har yee mee came large fresh prawns — perfectly cooked juicy full natural sweetness definitely highlight meal noodles however slightly bland could use touch seasoning bring prawn gravy’s richness rm char siew fried rice solid — full wok hei wellbalanced flavor hand wat tan hor suffered fate yee mee — lacking wok hei bit mild taste sugarcane served recycled tiger beer bottle tasted like mom would’ve made restaurant ceiling fans it’s reasonably comfortable parking fairly easy manned friendly chinese couple run things efficiently food came quickly piping hot also suspect there’s swallow house nearby bird call plays repeatedly throughout meal — familiar sound area cleanliness could improved little overall it’s charming nofrills spot worth visiting prawns alone

Name: Medan Ikan Bakar Seri Warisan
Review_Text:
Heading to Tenggol Island tomorrow, but will be spending a night in Dungun first. Tried a local Malay stall for dinner—famous among the locals for its affordable prices and wide variety of dishes. If you’re around the area, it’s worth checking out too. The place stays open till late, making it a convenient spot for those arriving from out of town and looking for a late dinner fix.

Medan Ikan Bakar Seri Warisan Kuala Dungun
Address: 171 Jalan Pasar, Kuala Dungun, Terengganu
Opening hours: Till 12am

We ordered 6 dishes plus 4 glasses of watermelon juice, total damage around RM140 for 4 pax.

Name: Kayu Manis Restaurant
Review_Text: Last weekend was probably the 4th time I stayed here. It's a great place to stay especially for travellers with kids and the elderly since all the villas are single-storey bungalows or semi-Ds. My kids love it there because of the vast area they could run around and play. On our recent trip here, the villa and rooms were nice and clean but the water in the master bedroom took some time to heat up and there was no hot water in the shared bathroom at the back. Other than that, everything else was pretty much good. We have also brought our late grandparents here before and we liked it because it's easy for the elderly to walk around since they don't have to climb up and down the stairs and easy to access for those on wheelchairs.

Name: Kayu Manis Restaurant
Review_Text: Been to Kertih for work and had to stay in Paka as other hotels were full due to peak season. Its a quiet place with basic facilities. Stayed for 2 nights in spacious semi-d unit with daily housekeeping. Need to run the water tap for few minutes to get rid the yellow water. The towel is clean but seems old. No toilettries provided and phone not functioning. Astro channel,kettle and fridge provided. Swimming pool, gym and bicycle rental available. The house and the area is clean and I can see they are working hard to maintained it. They also have 'Little Paka' restaurant which just beside the river with a live band performance every night. Not so much choice of breakfast in 'Kayu Manis' restaurant. You might need a transport to stay here since this place is quite isolated and the taxi fare also quite expensive.

Name: Kayu Manis Restaurant
Review_Text: Stayed in this resort for 4 nights. We took a semi-d with three rooms. The reason of staying here was due to the room rate. There were 3 of us thus three rooms sounded nice for us.||||Each room was equipped with air cond. However these air cond were poorly maintained and small and old. Two of these three air cond couldn't be switched on with remote and we had to press the manual start button on the blower unit. Due to low wind flow and small, it took hours before the rooms could be cooled.||||There's a kitchen. However there's no utensil. There were only 6 ceramic mugs with a pre packed coffee and tea with creamer and sugar. There's a small fridge and a kettle. So basically we can't cook in the kitchen.||||Two toilets in the house. One toilet had a boiler hidden somewhere providing warm shower. However the bathroom in master bedroom had no hot water. The original shower head was long broken and replaced with cheap plastic type. Even that was in poor condition. This was the worst shower head I had ever encountered in the past.||||The living area and rooms were clean. And the bed is OK. These were the only pro I could find. The pillows were hard though.||||There was a swimming pool in this resort. This was another reason we picked this resort as we thought we could relax in the pool after a full day of hard work. However we found that the pool was not cleaned and water in the pool was not overflowing thus not recycled through filter. There were leaves and tree branches in the water. I doubt they put chlorine in the pool.||||There were two restaurants in this resort. One was serving breakfast for 2. Although we had a three room house. Breakfast was not bad though. We had dinner in the other restaurant and the food was okay.||||Pro: Good breakfast. Clean room. Satellite TV.||Con: No kitchen utensils. Very poor bath room (old and not maintained). Not hot shower. Very poor air cond. No wifi. Dirty pool.

Review_Text: Our stay at Tanjung Jara Resort was truly amazing. The resort is beautifully surrounded by lush greenery, and I absolutely loved strolling along the long private beach. Name: Tanjong Jara Resort
Review_Text:
We returned home yesterday after spending 4 nights at TJR and it was truly a beautiful and memorable experience. To be honest, I wasn't entirely sure what to expect, but everything exceeded our expectations. Everything felt PERFECT!

We have visited many beautiful places throughout the world and with excellent facilities, but what truly makes this resort special is the STAFF. Every single person was helpful, attentive and genuinely caring. We visited as a family and my mother requires a wheelchair due to her physical weakness. From start to finish, everyone showed exceptional care and made sure she was comfortable at all times.

I would also like to extend a special thank you for the NY celebration organised by the resort. We definitely were not expecting it. It was beautifully executed and added a very special touch to our stay, making the experience even more memorable.

There are several staff that I need to acknowledge, especially the Resident manager, Mr. Ong who is truly outstanding. He took time to speak with everyone, was warm and approachable. This clearly reflects the culture and leadership throughout the entire team.

A special thanks as well to Chef Ann, MK, Arun, the lovely waiter of ours who was from Myanmar at the Teratai Terrace on NY Eve evening, sorry I can't recall your name) and many others whose names I regret I cannot remember. Each of you has played a part in making TJR a place truly worth visiting for us.

Your are truly keeping the Malaysian hospitality alive and God willing we will be back.

The Ganesan Family

Name: Tanjong Jara Resort
Review_Text:
We had an absolutely wonderful time. The rooms are very spacious, with large bathrooms, generous closet space, and super comfortable beds with plenty of room to relax and unwind. The pool is nice and deep, and the spa experience was amazing (just wish there were more slots available for treatments!).

The games room is HUGE and even has a KTV upstairs, making it a great spot for families. The only thing that could be improved is the food at the hotel, but there’s an excellent Chinese seafood restaurant just three minutes down the road.

Overall, a fantastic, family-friendly resort… we had a fabulous time and would definitely come back!

Name: Tanjong Jara Resort
Review_Text:
Tanjung Jara Resort – Peaceful, Calm and Quiet

What a beauty! The rooms are clean, spacious, and well-maintained, while the staff are friendly and genuinely welcoming — everywhere you go, you’ll be greeted with a smile. I had a wonderful time here with my family.

Breakfast was a real treat, offering a fusion of American classics and local delicacies. The beach is clean and tranquil, perfect for morning walks, and the sunrise view is simply spectacular. There are two swimming pools — one smaller and the other much larger, fondly referred to as the “water park.”

The resort’s lush landscaping and diverse flora and fauna add to its charm, with free-roaming peacocks adding a touch of magic.

All in all, a memorable and rejuvenating stay.

Name: Ayam Gepuk Rempah Jogja
Review_Text:
Ordered Vietnamese Ice Coffee and Bubble Tea to go.
The restaurant is pretty small and they prep the drinks on the roadside so if you are planning to sit at a table you'd better avoid peak hours but that would still be a challenge.
Vietnamese Ice Coffee is not really like the Vietnamese Coffee but it still tastes good so yes I would recommend. Just don't expect it to taste like a Vietnamese Coffee and you will be alright.
The Bubble Tea... is also okay.
You will need to write your name on the cup(as I did in the photo) and they will make bubble tea for you.
They rotate the cup as they pour in the tea and bubbles(like any bubble tea) and fill the cup with lots of Ice.
Then pour in the full cream milk followed by some whipping cream(?) then they will sprinkle some brown sugar on top which is then torched to melt.
Your Ice Bubble Tea is now ready to serve.
The bubble is not soft and gets hardened pretty soon so it is more than chewy.
It is pretty sweet, in fact a bit too sweet so instead of the bubble tea you may be tasting the sugar more then followed by full cream milk taste and lastly the hard bubbles.

Maybe it is because I ordered for take away, maybe it would be tastier and softer if I had it there?

Would I recommend it? Yes, why not?
There isn't much place to get bubble tea anyway.

Name: Kedai Roti Tempayan
Review_Text:
Kedai Roti Tempayan Dungun – Good Food, Lousy Service

“Roti Payang” is the local slang for roti tempayan, or what KL folks would call roti naan. After reading so many rave reviews, I decided to give it a try — and came away with mixed feelings.

You’ll need to queue to place your order, so be sure to secure a table number first. On a hot day, that queue can feel endless. I ordered roti Payang with kambing, nasi lemak, and teh O ais.

Nasi lemak – disappointing. Hardly any anchovies and just a small dab of sambal, though the rice itself was nice, fluffy, and fragrant. I liked how they wrap the cucumber separately though, it’s not in contact with the rice.
Roti Payang with kambing – excellent! The naan was cooked to perfection, and the mutton curry was thick, flavorful, and melt-in-your-mouth tender.
Drinks – tasted like watered-down sirap; not my cup of tea.

Mode of cooling - ceiling fans, located near the market, so it can get quite busy. Service is practically non-existent and the ordering system is a hassle. Cleanliness could also use improvement.

If you’re here, just go straight for the naan with lamb — skip everything else. Not cheap though.

Name: Mok Teh Yang
Review_Text:
Warung Mok Teh Yam – Don’t Miss the Sotong Fried Rice!

Came here with my local Dungun friend who swore by this place — and I’m glad I listened. We ordered the nasi goreng sotong and two plates of udang celup tepung.

The nasi goreng sotong came in an unbelievably huge portion. I initially wanted to add more sotong, but my friend insisted otherwise — and he was right. The plate came loaded with massive, fresh sotong, and the fried rice already had bits of sotong mixed in. Every bite was bursting with freshness and natural sweetness from the seafood. At RM25, it’s well worth it.

The udang celup tepung was another winner. Fresh prawns, crispy batter, simple but satisfying. No fancy seasoning — just clean-tasting seafood done right. Sometimes simplicity is the best seasoning.
6 medium prawns per plate.

The stall is manned by only two people, so service time can vary depending on the crowd. Still, it’s worth the wait. The seaside view is spectacular — waves crashing on rocks, salty breeze in the air — it’s the perfect spot to unwind. Total bill for 3 fried rice with sotong celup tepung, 2 plates of udang celup tepung, 4 drinks came up to be RM136.00.

Parking is free and plenty. Fans? Who needs fans right next to the beach? Cleanliness could use some improvement, but for the taste and the view, I’d come back in a heartbeat. Dungun gem indeed! 😁

Name: Ma Ani Selera Kampung
Review_Text:
Came here for dinner about two weeks ago. We ordered keropok lekor, sotong celup tepung, and nasi putih with siakap 3 rasa.

Service was surprisingly fast despite the crowd, definitely a plus! The keropok lekor was okay, nothing standout. The sotong celup tepung was a bit of a letdown, the squid felt tough and kinda shrunken, so probably not very fresh. The batter was still flavourful though.

The siakap 3 rasa (sadly no photo!) was decent, fresh fish, which we appreciated. But the gravy was more on the oily side instead of that sweet-spicy-sour balance we expected from a proper 3 rasa.

Price-wise, it was on the pricier side. Overall, not a bad experience, but there’s definitely room for improvement especially in terms of quality and value for money.

The nasi lemak stall here is also famous, price is slightly more expensive compared to others in the area, but the sambal is blazing hot.

Name: Sushi King
Review_Text:
Disappointing Experience at Sushi King (During Ramadan)

I usually enjoy dining at Sushi King, but unfortunately, my recent experience was quite disappointing.

I visited during Ramadan and arrived at the restaurant around 7:30 PM. We were told by the staff that we could only start ordering after 8 PM, which we completely understood and respected. So we decided to come back after 8 PM.

We returned restaurant around 8.20PM, the restaurant was empty, so we thought our food would be served quickly. We placed our order immediately using the iPad menu, but to our surprise, many items were marked as “not available.” Still, we were very hungry and tired, so we just selected whatever was left.

A staff member later informed us that rice would take 20 minutes to prepare. Since we were really hungry, we decided to cancel the rice-based meals. However, we ended up waiting more than an hour for our food, and several of the dishes we ordered never even arrived.

What made the experience even more frustrating was that there weren’t many customers in the restaurant, yet we noticed a few staff members sitting down, eating and chatting among themselves.

After waiting so long, we simply didn’t have the energy to complain further. We left feeling very disappointed. I really hope Sushi King can improve its service, especially during peak or sensitive times like Ramadan, when customers are fasting and just want a smooth dining experience.

Name: Nasi Dagang Pok Yusop
Review_Text:
I never loved nasi dagang until I tried it at this place. Nasi Dagang Pak Usop is just a small Malay restaurant with limited seating—perhaps a maximum of 10 tables. When I was there, I considered myself lucky to get a seat.

My husband and I ordered nasi dagang, while my boys had nasi lemak with fish. Both dishes were incredibly delicious—the spiciness and ingredients were just perfect. The service was also fast.

You must try this place! Some of my friends have told me that Nasi Dagang Atas Tol is the best, but I personally prefer this one. Give it a go and let me know what you think!

By the way, I have a question for the people of Terengganu—or anyone who knows: why is nasi dagang only available before 11 am? Can’t they cook more and serve it until lunchtime? It would be great for tourists like us to enjoy it later in the day too. Just my two cents!

Name: Riverbank Dungun Guesthouse & Caf
Review_Text: This time round is my second time staying in this hotel as scuba diver guest. The dive centre is literally a few steps away from the hotel. The jetty too is literally infront of the hotel. If you are a diver, you might appreciate the convenience of having this hotel very close to the dive centre and jetty. Parking is free for hotel guests. The room I got is spacious. For divers this is fantastic because the space can be used for equipment including camera gear. There are plenty of power points too. This is good for charging the cameras and video lights everyday, among other things. Aircon is cold and silent. The bathroom is spacious and nice. I had dinner twice at their restaurant when i was too lazy to drive to Dungun town. The food was delicious and portions were good. The stay comes with fresh breakfast everyday too. Rooms are cleaned everyday and stocks replenished. All the staff are great. The managers, Adrian and Wai, are friendly folks and always hang around the lobby to speak to the guests. For divers, I highly recommend staying here. p.s: complaints about bedbugs are usually confused by people with stings from jelly fish and corals. I spent 10 days in this hotel and not a single bug bit. Only Fabulous experience, great memories. 💖

Name: Riverbank Dungun Guesthouse & Caf
Review_Text: We only stayed a night here for a layover as we have to catch an early boat ride to Tenggol island the next day. The room with windows appear more spacious than we initially thought, with A/C, toiletries and bath towels, and enough power plugs. The bathroom is pretty basic with board walls built around it instead of concrete, but there's hot shower and it works superb. The room package comes with breakfast and since it was served around 7.30am, we could take our time to complete our meal as the guesthouse is located right opposite the jetty. Just nice.||||It's best for you to self-drive, as the guesthouse is not within considerable walking distance to town where all the eateries and convenience shops are located. The owner is friendly and helpful enough to suggest stalls or cafes we could try.

Review_Text: This place is a transit to go Tenggol Island. Very closed to jetty and dive center. It is an old building that require urgent maintenance. Name: Qimily Celup Tepung
Review_Text:
Qimily Celup Tepung Dungun: Mixed Bag

The Good:
Food: The seafood and keropok lekor were enjoyable, hitting the spot for a satisfying meal.
Price: The affordability of the dishes made it a budget-friendly option.

The Bad:
Cleanliness: Unfortunately, the overall cleanliness of the establishment was sorely lacking. From the sink to the toilet, table surfaces, and even the presence of cigarette butts and animal waste, the lack of hygiene was a significant downside to the experience.

Overall:
While the food itself was decent and reasonably priced, the poor cleanliness of the premises overshadowed the positives. If hygiene is a priority for you, you might want to consider other options. If you're solely focused on a quick and affordable meal, and can overlook the cleanliness issues, then Qimily Celup Tepung might still serve its purpose.

Recommendation: It's highly recommended that the management address the hygiene concerns promptly to provide a more pleasant dining experience for customers.

Name: Siakap Garing Cili Songkhla
Review_Text: From all the foods i ordered, i only enjoy their sotong celup tepung (rm25) 🫠🫠. All of my foods were received like they were not fresh from the stove. Not. Even. Warm. When we asked their waitresses, they claimed that the foods were all cooked freshly once ordered. Trust me, it was all lie ☺️. I dont know what's going on in their kitchen.... kailan ikan masin that we ordered... the sayur kailan was not even cooked. As if they just throw the kailan leaves in kuah ikan masin.... even the vegetables in our ayam goreng kunyit were also undercooked.... dont get me started with their chicken fried rice. The fried chicken was like a chicken that has been fried multiple times... but it's still red inside... and i can still taste the minyak kotor they used to fried the chicken. The fried rice, it's tasteless, cold. Ikan stim limau was a 👎👎 they didn't steam the fish together with its kuah. But i knew that the fish was already steamed a long time before we ordered (we received it cold). Their omelet and seafood tomyum was ok but still, it's not even warm...Our siakap 3 rasa, the fish was undercooked. But luckily, they agreed to cancel the fish from our order. They offered to replace a new one but we rejected since it was not that good after all.... with the price. The musics played was so loud. But i can't deny that their online marketing is nice. We were fooled... not gonna try this restaurant again. Maybe its not a masakan panas restaurant 🤷‍♀️🤷‍♀️ for the foods, a lotttttt need to be improved.

Name: Kedai Makan Sup Tulang Dan Sup Belut,Amz Mudahkire Ent.
Review_Text:
Restoran ini diusahakan oleh seorang anak muda bernama En Din yang berwawasan jauh dan kreatif. Inilah kali pertama saya jumpa menu Sup Belut.

Saya jangkakan sup belut (ikan) mesti ada bau atau rasa hanyir dan ternyata telahan saya itu salah. Sup belut yang disediakan memang enak dan penuh aroma rempah. Semangkuk dijual dengan harga RM8.

Kedai yang berada di tepi jalan di kawasan Bukit Pak Sabah mudah dilihat. Parkir juga agak mudah.

Dari segi kebersihan, ianya tidak diambil mudah oleh pemilik. Tandas yang bersih juga disediakan untuk pengunjung restoran yang dibuka pada setiap hari kecuali hari Jumaat dari jam 5 petang hingga 12 malam.

Saya syorkan anda cuba dan nilaikan sendiri sup belut ini. Terbaik dari periuk En Din👍

Name: Hotel Uitm
Review_Text:
Clean rooms and decent housekeeping. The rooms are tidy and well-maintained.

Friendly and responsive staff.

Good value for money — competitive rates for the area, especially for short stays.

Convenient location for local attractions and easy access to nearby beaches and eateries.

Wifi is inconsistent, with occasional connectivity issues. Internet can be slow or unreliable at times.

Basic facilities with limited amenities and older furnishings.

Sometimes the outside is very noisy, possibly due to thin walls.

Breakfast variety is limited, but the taste is good and enjoyable.

Overall, a solid option for a short stay, with friendly staff and good value, but with room for improvement in wifi, facilities, and noise insulation.
Cleaned_Text: clean rooms decent housekeeping rooms tidy wellmaintained friendly responsive staff good value money — competitive rates area especially short stays convenient location local attractions easy access nearby beaches eateries wifi inconsistent occasional connectivity issues internet slow unreliable times basic facilities limited amenities older furnishings sometimes outside noisy possibly due thin walls breakfast variety limited taste good enjoyable overall solid option short stay friendly staff good value room improvement wifi facilities noise insulation

Name: Hotel Uitm
Review_Text: Stayed one night to attend a family wedding nearby. A decent place. Classic interior. Room was spacious enough but very small bathroom. Some furnitures looking a bit old and got chipped. TV can be switched on, but not digital TV, it's analogue? So we didn't get to watch anything, no channel available. Bed and pillows were comfy enough. Breakfast buffet was alright, tasty and not bad, got a few choices to go for, main was nasi dagang and fried kuetiau, got bread, salad, congee, cereals, kuih. Coffee was very diluted, not the freshly brewed kind I think, taste like the instant powder kind. Front desk staff was friendly and nice. Nice view from my room on 2nd floor, can see the beach. No swimming pool for my kids but the staff told us that at the opposite venue, the golf club, has swimming pool so we crossed the road to get to the pools. Had to pay rm10 per entry for my kids to swim there. Overall it's alright.
Cleaned_Text: stayed one night attend family wedding nearby decent place classic interior room spacious enough small bathroom furnitures looking bit old got chipped tv switched digital tv analogue didnt get watch anything channel available bed pillows comfy enough breakfast buffet alright tasty bad got choices go main nasi dagang fried kuetiau got bread salad congee cereals kuih coffee diluted freshly brewed kind think taste like instant powder kind front desk staff friendly nice nice view room nd floor see beach swimming pool kids staff told us opposite venue golf club swimming pool crossed road get pools pay rm per entry kids swim overall alright

Name: Hotel Uitm
Review_Text: I stayed in this hotel for about 2 weeks. Here are the details:||||Location:||||No doubt the location is the USP for this property. In front you have golf club and on left hand side you have the beach. Its at a walking distance from the UiTM campus.||||Ambience:||||The ambience is just fine not the greatest but keeping in view this is a very small town hotel is providing decent place to stay. The rooms are quite big and furnishing is fresh. However not all rooms have LCD tv but rather old fashioned TVs. However not much channels on the offering.||||Restaurant:||||Though the hotel offers buffet breakfast but frankly if you are not a local resident you won't enjoy the food. Very few varieties on offer, no omelettes, sometimes half boiled eggs, few local dishes and white bread apart from 1-2 fruits and orange squash. Frankly you do not have much choice as well as nearby you won't find decent place to eat your food. Same is true for lunch and dinner.||||Staff:||||Staff are nice and friendly. The check-in process is quick and hassle free.||||Wi-Fi:||||Here is the catch, they do not offer Wi-Fi service in rooms. Only at hotel lobby they have Wi-Fi connectivity and that too very poor speed. Not good enough to run skype or whatsapp video call. So I would recommend you to have local SIM with 4G internet else you will have tough time communicating with your loved ones.||||Overall its a decent place to stay again I repeat considering its a very small town and you do not have much options to choose from.

Name: The Retreat Tanjung Jara
Review_Text:
I had a very disappointing experience with The Retreat Tanjung Jara regarding their deposit refund policy. I booked the entire stay and paid a deposit for a reservation scheduled within three months. However, due to unforeseen circumstances, I had to cancel just a day after booking.

Despite canceling early, the management has been holding onto my deposit for over three months, promising to refund it by February. Unfortunately, even after repeated follow ups, a portion of the refund is still outstanding. This has been an extremely frustrating and unprofessional experience.

I urge future customers to be cautious before making any bookings here, especially when it comes to their deposit policy. I hope the management takes responsibility and resolves this matter promptly.
Cleaned_Text: disappointing experience retreat tanjung jara regarding deposit refund policy booked entire stay paid deposit reservation scheduled within three months however due unforeseen circumstances cancel day booking despite canceling early management holding onto deposit three months promising refund february unfortunately even repeated follow ups portion refund still outstanding extremely frustrating unprofessional experience urge future customers cautious making bookings especially comes deposit policy hope management takes responsibility resolves matter promptly

Review_Text:

Sekian terima kasih.

Name: Warung Mr.Baung
Review_Text:
Warung Mr. Baung is a malay style restaurant located in gawi, kuala berang that serves a variety of local cuisine. The atmosphere is casual and welcoming, with a lot of seating available. Located near a river.

The menu features a wide range of dishes, including popular dishs like nasi Campur. The food is prepared fresh and is well-spiced, with plenty of flavor in each dish. Vegetarian options are also available.

The service at Warung Mr. Baung is friendly and attentive, with staff members happy to recommend dishes or make modifications to accommodate dietary restrictions. Prices are reasonable and offer good value for the quality of food and service.

Overall, I would highly recommend Warung Mr. Baung for those looking for an authentic malay dining experience in kuala betang. The restaurant provides a great mix of tasty food, friendly service, and a relaxed atmosphere, making it a must-visit for any food lover in the area.

Name: Warung Mr.Baung
Review_Text:
There’s a rhythm to Warung Mr. Baung that pulls you in, like the steady hum of a river flowing in Kuala Berang. You step in, and the air hums with the chatter of folks gathered, their eyes bright with the promise of good food. The staff greet you with warmth, smiles that come easy, and a sense of hospitality that makes you feel at home, whether you're just passing through or staying a while.

The stars of the show? The freshwater fish—baung, patin, kerai, and sebarau—each one a testament to the river from which they came. But if you’re here for lunch, you’ve got to try the baung tempoyak. Now, don’t just settle for any baung; make sure it’s warm from the counter, where the flavours are alive and kicking.

The self-service style keeps things flowing, and the prices are just right, leaving you satisfied without a hole in your wallet. Motorbikers seem to know this spot well, and you’ll find a free parking lot to rest your ride.

But even paradise has its small storms. Crowds gather often, the noise rising like a summer wind, and the heat of the day can settle heavy on your shoulders. But with the friendly service, and the generous spread of nasi campur calling your name, you’ll find the rewards are worth the wait.

Name: Umira Nasi Kukus Kuala Berang
Review_Text:
At Umira’s - where the ayam is crisp, the sambal is mean, and your diet doesn’t stand a chance.”

Deep in the heat-hugged heart of Kuala Berang, Umira Nasi Kukus serves up a plate that doesn’t whisper, it screams. The ayam berempah arrives like a fried thunderclap, shattering all doubts. Sambal? Unapologetically volcanic, yet addictive enough to consider therapy.

Service is fast, smiles are rare (but who needs them when the food loves you this hard), and the price is so reasonable it feels mildly illegal.

Historical Side Dish: Kuala Berang once launched early Terengganu civilization,  now it launches cholesterol levels. Balance, kan?

Bring tissues. Not for hygiene. For the sambal tears.
Verdict: Worth the drive, the sweat, and the existential spice crisis.
