Name: Murtabak Rusa Warisan Kota Temir
Review_Text:
(notes:- this review is solely focused on the "Warung Murtabak Rusa" which situated adjacent to the attraction spot of Warisan Kota Temir...)

the premises...
very clean & tidy... lots of tables & seats... really have a classic vibe of the "old days"...

the foods...
the signature dish - Murtabak Rusa... very delicious... really got into my tastebuds... RM20/pc... but worth it... big portion... quite enough for 2 person...

the roti telur... just so-so... same like other warung... the "kuah ikan" (dip sauce) was a bit salty... not worth it...

mee udang... also not that good... but still can eat...

the drinks... "air bandung ais"... thick & creamy... marvelous... really like it...

the service...
there was one lady worker as a waitress... i guess she is indonesian... very hardworking... she moved fast & responsive to the customers... kudos to her... i pray all the good doing for her...

the cook...  local malay man... also good... very quick in doing his job... all order was prepared so well... not so much time in waiting...

THEN... COME TO THE WORST PART...!!!
there was 1 lady... very old & slow... i was told maybe she is the wife of the shop owner OR the mother to the shop owner... very grumpy & not customer friendly...

at the material time... we had finished our food & having our own free leisure family time... the suddenly she came asking to clean up the table... our drinks still got half of the glass... we were really annoyed by her conduct...

but then... we decided to leave the table & ask if the payment can be made by QR... came to our shock... the old lady harshly replied...

"...if you can pay by cash is better...!!!"

OH MY GOD...!!! that was really a deal breaker...!!! we definitely wont come to this place again...!!!

(i was told that the owner of the shop is a guy... and my friendly advice to him... please dont put your wife or your mother as a "shop frontline"... and now already year 2025... please upgrade your payment mode option... online payment is a "must have" for every shop nowdays...!!!)

Name: Kfc Kuala Berang
Review_Text: Humble and welcoming, that's what I can say about KFC Kuala Berang and its staffs. Despite being in a rural area, there's nothing rural about the services provided by its employees. The service is top-notch and amazing. If I were to compare it to other branches in a more urban area, KFC Kuala Berang's level of efficiency and management boasts its reputation better than the former. Here you will definitely get your orders on the spot without having to wait longer than needed; no queue numbers are given, and the orders are all ready on the serving stations, freshly prepared from the kitchen. Unlike other branches where Zinger Burgers ala carte may take around 15 minutes to prepare, KFC Kuala Berang has prepared the burgers already. The only downside is the internal design of the establishment which doesn't really cater much to patrons on a wheelchair as the stairs are quite narrow and steep. Kudos to KFC Kuala Berang.

Name: Kak Pah Nasi Dagang
Review_Text:

Review_Text: If you are looking for a tasty & reasonable price of seafood, this is the place. Thumbs up for the food. But every restaurant must also consider the good services towards customers. I had ordered grilled fish (I would say the sambal is very nice), sotong celup tepung ( ordered this after reading the reviews from others and I really love it, super crunchy and tasty) & kerang bakar ( so so). Name: Medan Ikan Bakar Kuala Ibai (Bawah Jambatan)
Review_Text:
Such a dreamy dinner dibawah jambatan restaurant at kuala ibai.
We had the famous sotong celup tepung that was so tender in every bite. We also tried the sotong cili padi goreng with telur masin. We had ikan bakar with bawal fish. The ikan bawal was 'bakar' in banana leaf to perfection with the right amount of chili paste. Siakap 3 rasa was different compared to the ones we have tried in southern part of m'sia whereby  the 3 rasa fish taste was heavy with serai flavor which we loved very much. We had the kangkung belacan, eggs bistik, mix vegetables, and tried the popular satay ayam which was heavily marinated with serai and other spices which turned out mouth watering good.
By 8.30pm the restaurant was swelling with dinner crowd. We left the place happy and satisfied.
Cleaned_Text: dreamy dinner dibawah jambatan restaurant kuala ibai famous sotong celup tepung tender every bite also tried sotong cili padi goreng telur masin ikan bakar bawal fish ikan bawal bakar banana leaf perfection right amount chili paste siakap rasa different compared ones tried southern part msia whereby rasa fish taste heavy serai flavor loved much kangkung belacan eggs bistik mix vegetables tried popular satay ayam heavily marinated serai spices turned mouth watering good pm restaurant swelling dinner crowd left place happy satisfied

Name: Ayam Gepuk Pak Zayn
Review_Text: I highly recommend this place! The chicken (ayam) is so tender and the portion size is very generous. Unlike other places that only give you a slice and small piece of cucumber & cabbage, the amount of ulam-ulaman here is impressive. Even the tauhu goreng comes in a reasonable amount for a single meal pack. The price is very affordable, and the sambal is seriously 'sedap'—it reminds me of the famous sambal lala in Bandung. Definitely worth it!

Name: Hulu Terengganu District Council
Review_Text:
Assalamualaikum Tuan/Puan,

Saya ingin membuat aduan tentang masalah Perpustakaan Cawangan Hulu Terengganu kepada pengunjung dan pelajar yang ingin menjalankan peperiksaan dan tugasan-tugasan penting.

1. Masalah aircond yang tidak berfungsi sejak sebulan yang lalu. Tiada sebarang perubahan atau penambahbaikan untuk membaiki aircond ini. Padahal, majoriti pengguna dan pelajar menggunakan tingkat dua untuk menyelesaikan tugasan kami.

2. Masalah kedua adalah tentang kekurangan plug. Boleh dikatakan bahawa Perpustakaan Hulu Terengganu hanya mempunyai 4 suis yang berfungsi termasuk tingkat atas dan bawah. Hal ini perlu dipandang serius, kerana ia mengganggu tugasan kami. Melalui pengalaman saya, kos penukaran suis/plug baru hanya RM15/RM20. Jadi agak tidak logik jika tiada sebarang penambahbaikan kerana masalah ini sudah terjadi sebelum Covid19.

Jadi saya mewakili pengguna pustaka ini berharap pihak bertanggungjawab dapat memandang serius tentang perihal ini, kerana bermula pada bulan ini pelajar-pelajar universiti perlu menghadapi ujian dan 'oral presentation'. Demikian, kami perlu menggunakan aras atas secepat yang mungkin supaya tidak mengganggu ruang bacaan dibawah.

Keprihatinan Tuan/Puan amatlah kami hargai. Kami berharap pihak tuan/puan dapat menyelesaikan masalah ini dengan secepat mungkin kerana hal ini sudah lebih sebulan dan seminggu.

Sekian ,Terima Kasih.

Ikhlas dari Pengguna dan Pelajar Hulu Terengganu.

Name: The Grand Puteri Hotel
Review_Text:
Staff friendly and attentive. Room was comfortable. Water heater in our room not functioning well. Reported and they fixed it but issue recurring. The technician advised to change room so they assigned us another room.

Breakfast was good with simple options of Malay foods. We love their nasi dagang. Non hotel guest can come to enjoy their very cheap breakfast buffet RM15/pax but they just need to improve breakfast flow for hotel guest and walk in (outside) customer because during the peak hour so hard to find seats and some guests who just came simply wait for their table instead of waiting for the staff to assign table.

Pools were not properly maintained. The water was a bit dirty. They actually separate Men’s and Women’s but during our stay Men’s pool was under maintenance.

Name: The Grand Puteri Hotel
Review_Text:
The Grand PUTERI Hotel – what a letdown!

I booked a night here after Googling and reading plenty of rave reviews about the location, buffet breakfast, and rooms. Sounded like a safe bet—turns out, not quite.

Location (10/10): This is the one thing the hotel absolutely nails. Right smack in the middle of Kuala Terengganu’s busiest area—if you can call it that. Almost everything is within walking distance, so convenience-wise, no complaints.

Buffet Breakfast (2/10): Honestly, the only thing worth mentioning is the quirky door gift—a pack of Gardenia bread, mineral water, and a soy drink. Everything else? Cold. The half-boiled eggs, egg toast, and nasi dagang all sat there lifeless. Since it came with the room, I went through the motions, but if you have options outside, skip it.

Room (3/10): Either the mosque speakers are extra loud, or my corner room was built right under them. The whole room smelled like wet carpet mixed with dirty socks. The TV was smaller than my PC monitor. Bed was just about bearable, but the pillows were thin and weightless. The bathroom was the real dealbreaker—dirty grouting, zero soundproofing, and a shower design that floods the entire toilet floor.

Verdict: Book here only if location is your top priority. Sadly, in Kuala Terengganu, choices are limited—no condos, barely any Airbnb options, and mostly seaside chalets or homestays. Just don’t bother with the breakfast. You’ve been warned.

Name: Restaurant Stuff Crab
Review_Text:
Kemaman stuffed crab (甘马挽酿蟹) is a must-try dish in Chukai, Terengganu. The crab shell is generously stuffed with tender pulled crab meat mixed with chopped onions, carrots, greens, and other ingredients, then coated with a thin layer of egg and flour batter. The stuffed crab is deep-fried until the batter turns lightly golden brown. The dish offers a delightful sweetness from both the crab meat and the softened onions and carrots.

Additionally, we also tried stir-fried belacan kangkong, fried egg with onions, steamed tofu with scallion oil, and sweet and sour chicken.

Name: Restaurant Stuff Crab
Review_Text:
This time around, decided on Restoran Stuff Crab Kemaman among the may restaurant in the row.

- Stuffed crab was ok, inflation maybe so the filling is less, but taste is ok, goes well with their chilli sauce.
- Seafood fried rice, good, normal, nothing much to complain
- Stir fried cabbage with dried shrimps. This one they say is their speciality. Wow, very good actually, even for something simple like cabbage
- Steamed prawns. Good flavour, fresh prawns, reasonable size

RM84.40 total, not the cheapest but still alright. I'd say it is better than their next door neighbour

Stayed at Suite 18 Boutique Hotel and had a great experience overall.

Pros:
•	Excellent location, just a short walk to Pasar Payang and surrounded by plenty of cafes.
•	Definitely value for money.
•	Rooms are pleasantly equipped with Young Living essential oil air diffuser, giving a fresh feel.
•	Convenient facilities with a water dispenser and iron provided on every floor corridor.
•	The receptionist (a Malay guy) was very attentive and friendly, which made the stay even more welcoming.

Plus the free welcome drink at the hidden cafe is much appreciated..the hot chocolate served is yummy😋😋

Cons:
•	Parking can be limited, but it’s quite understandable given the hotel is in a busy, tourist-friendly area.

✨ Overall, a comfortable and worthwhile stay if you’re visiting Kuala Terengganu!

Name: Tong Juan Restaurant
Review_Text:
**Fresh Flavors, Timeless Traditions—Discover Tong Juan's Legendary Stuffed Crab.**

A visit to Tong Juan in Kemaman is a delightful journey into one of Terengganu's culinary treasures. Established in 1967, this beloved restaurant is deeply rooted in the region's food culture, known for its commitment to quality and fresh ingredients. Its founders sought to create dishes that celebrate local flavors, a tradition that continues to draw loyal locals and curious travelers.

The highlight of Tong Juan's menu is undoubtedly the signature stuffed crab, a harmonious blend of sweet, fresh crab meat and carefully balanced herbs and spices. Each bite reflects the passion and care poured into every dish, making it a must-try for seafood enthusiasts. Beyond the stuffed crab, Tong Juan offers a variety of seafood dishes that are sure to please.

What sets Tong Juan apart is not just its food but also its welcoming ambiance. The restaurant takes great pride in its cleanliness, ensuring a pleasant dining experience. Loved by the local community, it has become a gathering place for families and friends. Additionally, Tong Juan is Muslim-friendly, making it a great choice for Muslim diners seeking delicious and fresh halal seafood.

For those exploring Kemaman, a meal at Tong Juan is more than just satisfying your cravings—it's a chance to experience a piece of Terengganu's rich culinary heritage. Let the fresh flavors and cozy atmosphere ease your food anxieties, and don't forget to savor the legendary stuffed crab!

The image photos and video clips are taken via Poco F5 Pro smartphone

A must-visit if you’re feeling carnivorous and don’t mind a short drive into Terengganu.

Name: Mamatin Kitchen
Review_Text:
Assalamualaikum dear friends.

How many time do you crave and long for a combination of well mixed seafood with genuine local taste? Well I'm not gonna say it's Manhattan Fish Market as this is on the other category in my mind.

But here, I would have to say that Mamatin Kitchen at Taman Bandar Putra Mak Lagam is really fantastic choice to dine in / take away a fantastic set of Shellout.

Before you quickly dismiss my opinion as exaggerated, I simply ask that you consider some of the reasons I have selected this Mamatin Kitchen restaurant worth in the list of my favourite spot in Kemaman. I'm gonna go straight to the main reason I'm here. My 10yo daughter requested to have her desired dish called the "Shellout" .

Looking at the name of Shellout give the definition in the sense: to remove from a pod or (figuratively) a purse) is just right on what we are going to have for dinner. A variety of seafood like the Kupang, Lala, Kerang, Siput Buluh @ prawns, crabs, squids. We've ordered the Shellout of buttermilk and curry paste cooked separately and served side by side on the big plastic sheets.

The concept quite the same as Pan Dinner or Tray Dinner but instead a big plastic sheets spread across the table for serving. Some people might not be able to familiarise themselves to this way of having their fills. But within my own family members we love it. We already familiar with the practice as it is the same at our own kitchen too.

By the way Shellout is their quick selling signature dish. I believe it due to the flavorful of the dish itself giving it the status. The mixture of mussels with crabs and squids taste great. The buttermilk gravy is a richly flavored with kinda mildy creamy not too creamy / buttery  and also herbs (curry leave) while the curry paste gravy is spicy thick with a hint of sweetness and sourness and it taste great all together. I taking it wit a cup of rice and there are still lots of shellout on the table to be enjoyed. Yummy.

Other considerations in my opinion is :

1) their setting of tables. The restaurant itself is spacious and the tables arrangement is comfortable to sit in not too close with the other tables making us not embarrassed while we have to work on the shells. The marble tables is clean and sturdy. Important for a good first impression.

2) sink to wash your hand is near the corridor and its is clean, placed with handwash and tissues / hand napkin. The tissues or hand napkins were not on the tables to make easy to clear and clean the tables everytime needed.

3) the waiter os very polite and hospitable.

4) the owner Brother Epul is kind and friendly as always and give discount on the little changes /coins.

5) the building of the restaurant is new and clean environment with calmimg atmosphere with no traffic jams or havoc.

6) the parking is available in front of the restaurant.

7) clean corridor and comfortable walkway  makes it easy for me to carry my little sons and safe for my little princess to walk all by themselves.

Name: Casa Dinda By Nostimo'S
Review_Text: Went to this place way back during its early opening days whereby the menu is kinda limited to its signature chicken rice. This time around they really revamped the place. There were a few hawkers allowed to operate in the compund giving more options to the vastly increased number of foods. They also provide an indoor airconditioned dining area chargeable at 3% of your total order. Service was fast. Not sure if we came during not that peak hour causing it but we are sure glad with it. There are a few more room for improvement but it bears no significance. Managed to try its Pan Fried Bread with mushroom soup, Mee Rebus, Buttermilk Chicken Rice, Crispy Fried Chicken Fried Rice and its regular chicken rice. All of it are delicious. Mee Rebus could be better if they used 100% pure seafood and cleaner vegetable. The chicken rice is quite plain compared to others. Other than this all is ok. The drinks are also nice. Dragonfruit & Choc Oreo Smoothies are very nice.

Name: Steven Coffee House
Review_Text:
There are not many places in Chukai for Chinese food. There are a few famous ones which I see workers or visitor would typically try. I thought of going to ones I haven't been.

This place supposed have been open for more than 10 years. It's one of the few which are authentic and air conditioned.

The food is ok. There are a few choices, both western and local Chinese choices. My expectation was not high cause its a small town. I had the steak sandwich and my friend omellte with carb meat. The taste is reasonable with the beef has its thickness and texture. Those I would like to see more 'ingredients' between the 2 slices of bread. Probably with cheese, tomatoes and mayonnaise.

I may still visit this place to try other dishes.

Name: Banafsha
Review_Text: One of the iconic food place in Kemaman for all those foodies out there. Served mostly grill-styled food, burgers with a home made patties and one of favourite Malaysian dishes, Roti John. A bit pricy  place but the lavish ingredients really made up to it. Recommended to eat in rather than take away to enjoy the freshness of the food itself. Pair the food with its signature Mango Vanilla Smoothies and you'll be amazed with what those combo can do to your taste bud. The place were nicely setup with a strip of yellow bulb light surrounding a cabin kitchen where u can see the food is prepared. In short, a must try for food lovers.

Name: Restoran Tong Cheng
Review_Text:
This shop closes after lunch time. We finished our lunch around 2pm and the shop closed for the afternoon after we were done.

Ordered 3 dishes, Pork Rib King(排骨王), clay pot toufu and baby kailan.

Pork Rib King (排骨王) was a bit tough. Not all pieces were marbled, so depends on how soft you like it. I like it soft, so not really my preference.

Baby kailan with oyster sauce was really nice. Maybe because the oyster sauce tasted nice 😅

Clay pot toufu had pork in it. The pork had a strong taste/smell. I guess they didn't parboil the pork first. Not a fan of strong pork smell/taste.

Overall ok. RM34+ for 3 dishes + 2 rice + 2 drinks

Review_Text:
This kopitiam (coffee shop) is an institution in Kemaman.  It is busy and bustling with customers.
The food it serves is the usual kopitiam fare like soft boiled eggs, roti bakar (toasted bread) with kaya (coconut jam), and good coffee & tea.  There are also other foods available like nasi lemak (coconut rice), a small selection of kueh (pastries), and other items.
The service is efficient & friendly, and prices are reasonable.

Name: Kedai Kopi Hai Peng
Review_Text:
Arriving around 2:30pm, we had to wait for approximately 10 minutes in the queue to get a table. Unfortunately, the kuih that initially attracted me to this place had already sold out, which was a bit disappointing.

Nevertheless, we proceeded to order both the kaya butter toast options: bun-shaped and sliced. Both variations were delicious, but personally, I preferred the sliced toast. The bread had an old-fashioned charm, with a moist interior and a crispy exterior, setting it apart from the plain and dry instant yeasted kopitiam bread commonly found these days. The coffee served was decent and complemented the toast well.

On the other hand, the nasi lemak was just average. It was served with plain white rice, which was a letdown considering the typical expectation of fragrant coconut rice for nasi lemak.

Please note that there are multiple Hai Peng Kopitiam locations when using Waze for navigation. Make sure you select the correct one to avoid any confusion.

Overall, despite missing out on the kuih, the delicious kaya butter toast and satisfying coffee made the visit worthwhile.

All the foods we order were really tasteless (like forgot to put salt). Maybe we were unlucky as we came that time it was really packed. We have to arrange our own table for 10 pax. At the end of the day, they left out 2 dishes.
The seafoods were fresh, just the cooking method and services were not that great.

No tissue provided on the table. U have to find it at the cashier.

Name: Leong Seafood Restaurant
Review_Text:
Stayed at Cherating and went to Kemamam for dinner. This restaurant is only 20mins away. Husband and I ordered fried jury teow for 1 person, salted egg calamari, salted fried prawns (went well with beer), and a spicy fried clam dish (don't remember the exact).

The good ones - kuey teow has very good "wok hei" and not oily. The prawns were great - fresh, big, and juicy flesh. The bad ones - salted egg calamari was wet and not crispy, apart from tasted salted egg, the sotong has no taste at all. The clam dish, I ordered 甘香 but it came out wet curry fried, overpowering the seafood so apart from spicy, I tasted nothing else 😅

The waiting hours are ridiculously long, 1hr plus. Because the kitchen cooked order by order. So when it's our turn, all dishes will come up to the table all at once. I feel this has more bad than good, because the appetite and mood diminishes when time getting longer. Well maybe just me.

Name: Galley
Review_Text:
A MUST-VISIT RESTAURANT IN KEMAMAN. SERIOUSLY.

We picked this restaurant at random on Google Maps for a late lunch on our way to our homestay. Lo and behold, it was everything we could have asked for and then some. Don't be fooled by the unassuming surroundings. Yes, the shoplot where Galley is located might look dodgy, but this restaurant definitely is not.

First, the ambience. I love how they are not trying too hard to achieve that retro vibe. The decorations are tastefully done. The vintage booth is, ugh, so gorgeous.

And then the food. THE FOOD.

We ordered nasi goreng cili kering ikan bekok (fried rice infused with dried chili and semi-dried salted fish), nasi bawal emas bakar (white rice with grilled golden pomfret), and nasi ayam penyet (white rice with smashed fried chicken and sambal chili paste).

The first dish is simply divine. The dried chili tastes so fresh, coating every grain of fried rice. Semi-dried salted fish (ikan bekok) is a hard dish to master, yet they serve it effortlessly, fried to perfection. We later found out that the dried chili paste is homemade and the ikan bekok is sourced directly from a traditional maker in Kuala Krai, one of the districts on the East Coast renowned for such products.

And the grilled golden pomfret? It is charcoal-grilled. It's served plain, with no marinade, so you can enjoy the freshness with a magical mixture of anchovy sauce (budu), fresh bird's eye chili, lime, and julienned shallots. When they say that sometimes the simplest dish is the most delicious, this is one of them.

The nasi ayam penyet is average at best, but there were no complaints from my son.

Next time we are in Kemaman, we will definitely come back to Galley for the comfort, honesty, and deliciousness wrapped in both their dishes and their service.

Name: Warung Aziz Satar
Review_Text:
Very friendly service at Warung Aziz Satar—especially the lady boss, who was genuinely warm and took the time to introduce all the different fish-based dishes they offer. It was my first time trying the East Coast-style otak-otak and satar, and the experience was definitely something new for me.

The satar is sold at RM1 per piece, which can feel a bit pricey unless you’re buying in bulk. Still, it’s a unique taste worth trying at least once, especially if you’re not familiar with this regional style.

We also tried the Terengganu lontong, which was another first. The broth didn’t have an overpowering fishy smell (which I appreciated), but the overall dish felt a bit bland to me. It came with some raw cabbage and sambal, but lacked the depth or variety of condiments I’m used to. That said, it’s clearly a local favourite, so while it didn’t quite hit the mark for me, I can see why others might enjoy it.

Name: Miss Cabin Western Food
Review_Text: If you're looking for real western taste beef steak or lamb chop, this place is not for you. The service is good and fast, presentation of the food is western food like served on a non-ceramic black plate. Looked presentable. Both steak & lamb chop are well seasoned with Malay satay spices. Good to eat on its own. It's served with a mild black pepper sauce, which in fact is not really needed. The meat is not thick like a proper steak. Lamb chop is of low-quality grade with many tendons. However, one can't ask for much since it's only RM18 & RM16 for the chop & steak respectively. You can skip the RM7 mushroom soup with 2 slices of garlic bread. I guess ratio of water to soup is 2:1 (suspect cheap canned soup is used), as it's rather tasteless and served in only half a small bowl size. Bread is the cheap white bread used in kaya toast. Saving grace is the garlic herb butter. We also ordered a nicely made drink with many colourful bits of jelly in it. Alas, the wrong type of straw is used for the drink. The jelly can't even pass through it. The drink should be served with bubble tea straw. We had to remove the ice in order to finish the drink. Good place if you don't mind eating local flavoured "western" food, where the feeling is like eating a big piece of satay meat served on a plate with french fries, coleslaw and a small dish of black pepper sauce.

Name: Kemaman Kitchen
Review_Text:
Kemaman Kitchen's triumphant return unveils the most delectable Char Kuey Teow, an experience that must be savored to be believed. The Western dishes are equally enticing, creating a culinary journey that is nothing short of delightful. Our order, a feast of diverse flavors, included the exquisite Mee Udang, tantalizing Char Kue Teow Special, comforting Mee Rebus, indulgent Buttermilk Prawn Spaghetti, and succulent Chicken Grill. The kids were enamored with the scrumptious Western offerings.

Yet, stealing the spotlight was the Char Kuey Teow Penang Style – an all-time favorite that left me captivated with its undeniable and rich flavors. The perfect blend of ingredients created a symphony of taste that's hard to argue with.

Kemaman Kitchen effortlessly merges traditional and Western cuisines, appealing to diverse palates. The cozy ambiance and attentive service enhance the overall dining experience, making it a haven for gastronomic adventures.

In this culinary paradise, each dish at Kemaman Kitchen tells a flavorful tale, leaving patrons eagerly anticipating an encore. For those seeking a gastronomic adventure that satisfies every craving, Kemaman Kitchen is a culinary haven worth exploring.

Name: Satar & Otak-Otak Che Wan Geliga,Kemaman Trg.
Review_Text: Easily accessible with ample parking although on the road side only. Varieties of local delicacies available although the main attraction is their Otak2 and Sata Terengganu. The sitting arrangement is also ample with a few open area sitting available. The Otak2 came with two version of either ikan tenggiri@spanish mackerel or ikan selayang@sardine.Unlike the Otak2 Johor which is fish paste combined spices wrapped in palm leaves, the Otak2 here is a piece of Tenggiri@Selayang fish meat smothered with spices. More like a miniature pack of barbequed fish. Personally i love the Otak2 Ikan Selayang better than Tenggiri. Their Sata is also exceptional. Do come early as their Otak2 and Sata usually is out of stock before 5pm. Highly recommended if you are looking for the authentic Kemaman Sata and Otak2.

Name: Satar & Otak-Otak Che Wan Geliga,Kemaman Trg.
Review_Text:
This place is very hot. Came around 1PM lunch time on Sunday and the place was quite empty. Alot of people came for takeaway though. We ordered the satar and otak-otak set. There are 2 prices - RM11(normal) and RM14( tenggiri) The waitress didn't ask which fish you prefer and when i paid she charged me RM14 for the set. The otak-otak is good a bit on the sweet side -not spicy at all. The keropok lekor set is RM4 - didnt quite like the taste as the lekor were soft and spongy.
We also ordered coconut drink but it was so sweet - like they added sugar to it- RM3.50 for a glass of coconut is more expensive than KL standard for this kinda no aircon place. She said the whole coconut is not available. Overall, i wouldn't go there to eat as it is way too hot.

Name: De' Office Cafe & Restaurant

🌟 De Office Cafe and Restaurant – Simply the Best! 🌟

If you're searching for the perfect mix of mouthwatering Chinese and Indian food, look no further than De Office Cafe and Restaurant! 🍜🍛

Every dish is packed with incredible flavors — from spicy Indian curries to sizzling Chinese stir-fries. And when you add a chilled beer from their well-stocked bar 🍺... it’s the ultimate dining experience!

What makes it even better? The staff are super friendly, always serving with a smile, and the owner is one of the kindest and most humble people you’ll meet. You feel welcomed like family from the moment you walk in.

This place is not just a meal — it’s a vibe! Highly recommended, 10/10. Will keep coming back! 🔥👑

Name: Keladi Western Hut
Review_Text:
Ordered a grilled beef but more like boiled meat.. LoL..
Ask the front staff, they denied it but as a frequent steak/meat eater I'm definitely judging ;
1. The beef came fully cooked but served warm (not hot, warm!) and in less than 15mins. The beef was thick, this indicate the beef was pre-cooked first before re-heat & served.
2. The beef was kind of melted in mouth, like a boiled/pressured cooked beef kind of texture. It also had stringy texture that I don't normally see in grilled beef. Grilled beef definitely had different textures despite different grilling method.
3. Taste was off, no taste of charred/smoked/grilled taste. How can one call a grilled beef when there's no taste of meat oil also? This one was absent of meat oil. Just like a boiled meat.

Although their coffee was nice.
Would recommend the coffee but none of the food. Their black pepper sauce was nice but really not worth it. Fries tastes of plastic and salad was given with cheap 1000 island dressing.
The food was totally out.
Service was fast tho.

Name: Restaurant Win Thong Zen
Review_Text:
It is THE first time i have ever ever tasted noodles made this way!! 🥹☝️
Followed a friend to kemaman and was told this is how locals eat breakfast of fresh fish!
Caught just off the coast (which is less than a kilometre away), this stall brings in FRESH FRESH fish from the seas - Selar, Kembong, Mata Besar, Ikan barat and Kerapu (Grouper). They dint even need to put in on ice to keep fresh because it goes off his box before he does so!!
We ordered two 'Ikan Barat' ( Leather Jacket Fish) and one Grouper(which doesn't look like the rock groupers we've ever seen in West Malaysia markets!).
The process is simple, it produces the best tasting fish & noodles anyone could ever have. The fish is steamed, and served as it is - no ginger, garlic, oils or sauces. The juices grom the steamed fish are added into a soup broth and ladled over plain noodles. A little sayur Kangkong is added and that's all - you have an extremely tasty, umami-packed broth/soup with noodles! The fish sells out fast and if it's gone, you just have to wait the next morning for the fishermen to bring in their catch from the sea.
I dream of it nightly and count the days where i could make a trip there soon to savor this. I could eat this for weeks literally!

Name: Kedai Kopi Tim Kam Leng
Review_Text:
##Kedai Kopi Tim Kam Leng: Where Tradition Meets Taste##

Nestled in the heart of Chukai, Kemaman, Kedai Kopi Tim Kam Leng is more than just a coffee shop—it's a living testament to the region's rich culinary heritage. Located near the Kemaman bus station in a traditional wooden building, this humble establishment offers a blend of nostalgia and flavor that keeps diners coming back for decades.

The charm of Tim Kam Leng lies not only in its historic setting but also in its legendary fish noodle soup. This signature dish, crafted with fresh ikan kembung (mackerel) and handmade noodles, epitomizes the simplicity and authenticity of Malaysian cuisine. The clear, savory broth is a delicate balance of flavors, enhanced by a side of sambal that provides a fiery kick. Each bowl tells a story of dedication to quality and tradition.

The coffee shop’s old wooden structure, with its high ceilings and airy design, creates a welcoming atmosphere that feels both timeless and comforting. Over the years, it has become a beloved landmark, drawing locals and travelers eager to experience a taste of Kemaman's culinary traditions in a setting that honors its heritage.

To add convenience to your visit, there is a clean public toilet within walking distance from the restaurant. The facility is well-maintained and available for a nominal fee of **50 cents per entry**, ensuring you can enjoy your dining experience worry-free.

Kedai Kopi Tim Kam Leng stands as a cultural and culinary icon, offering not just meals but memories. Whether you're savoring the flavorful fish noodle soup or simply taking in the nostalgic ambiance, a visit to this coffee shop is an experience that lingers long after the last bite. Let its timeless charm and authentic flavors be the highlight of your culinary journey.

Timeless Ambiance, Authentic Flavors—Discover Kedai Kopi Tim Kam Leng.

Name: Kenny Rogers Roasters
Review_Text:
Although the food is good, this place has terrible service. I've had multiple experience where we ordered food and had already paid for the meal but one of the employees came to say that the food is not enough. Sure, opting for other food is no biggie, but this has happen multiple times. Despite the operating hours is until 10.00 p.m, we often come at 6 p.m only to discover that most food is either unavailable, or they will not make more for the day.

One of the joys of eating at KR is their muffins and there has been multiple times where either they dont have enough muffin, or they are in the process of making it. I am well aware that usually people would prepare for closing an hour earlier, but it seems like at 6 p.m the workers is already preparing to close down the shop seeing that many things are unavailable, or they wont make anymore for the day. If you want to come to this branch, I'd suggest come earlier in the day as if you come later you'll be served with disappointment. My family and I are fond of KR, but because of this we've stopped visiting this particular franchise as it is very disappointing.

Name: Warung Roti Tempayan
Review_Text:
A Hidden Gem I Found on Google – Totally Worth the Visit!

I stumbled upon this restaurant while browsing Google and was really curious about what roti tempayan is. When I arrived, there was already a long queue — I thought I’d have to wait forever! But the owner manages the place very efficiently, and all the customers were polite and queued up in an orderly way to place their orders.

I recommend ordering both dine-in and takeaway at the same time, otherwise you’ll need to queue again. They also serve a variety of tasty local snacks — the fried ketupat was especially delicious and crispy.

Highly recommended for anyone looking to try something authentic, affordable, and truly satisfying!

Name: Geliga Bus Terminal Food Court
Review_Text:
this a regular 3-star food court, what gives it an extra star is Kak Noor's Chinese style food at the far right. The mee kari is very satisfying, tastes just right and not too heavy. The nasi kukus is good too, comes with a generous portion of vege. The star attraction, however, is the steam chicken nasi ayam. It will make a believer out of you. One taste and you will be at peace with the world and yourself.

If you fancy some lauk, the popular option is Najwa next to Kak Noor's.

That doesn't mean the others are bad, though.

Edit 26 April 2023
Sadly, Kak Noor's shop has been closed for some time now, for good. I miss her food. I wish her well.

Name: Geliga Bus Terminal Food Court
Review_Text:
This comment refers to one of the few stalls there, called Miera Wafer Cake. It was midnight on the 4th day of Raya. Most of the shops were full due to Perantauan returning to town. We ordered nasi goreng paprik because we knew there would be a long wait, hoping it would be quicker if they could prepare it all at once. Unfortunately, without any information, they changed it to nasi goreng kampung. After a long wait, they came and informed us that there were very few anchovies left. When asked if I wanted to proceed, I reluctantly agreed.

Finally, we received an OILY plain fried rice with a lot of anchovy heads. I could count that there was 3 pieces of chicken, no egg, and only 5 tiny pieces of anchovies were present, along with numerous anchovy heads.

Review_Text:
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)
Warung Bira Perahu – Great Spot with Tasty Celup Tepung

This warung has a superb location right next to the Nelayan Jetty, with a beautiful view where you can even see fishermen unloading their fresh catch. The Celup Tepung is really delicious and worth trying, but the waiting time can be almost an hour during peak hours. In contrast, other fried items (goreng-goreng) come out much faster.

Parking is quite limited, so it can be a challenge if the place is busy. Still, the atmosphere and food make it a nice spot to visit. Overall, I’d definitely come back again, but preferably not during peak times.

Name: Abezi Nasi Kerabu
Review_Text:
A hidden gem you must try!

Abezi Nasi Kerabu is truly a must-visit for anyone craving authentic and heartwarming Malaysian cuisine. We had the pleasure of trying the Ayam Bakar and Ayam Percik, both of which were incredibly flavorful and perfectly cooked. My mother-in-law opted for the Ayam Rendang, and she absolutely loved it — tender, rich, and deeply aromatic.

The hospitality here is just as memorable as the food. The staff were all very kind and welcoming, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere from the moment we walked in. A special shout-out to Miss Nabila, who personally came over to thank us for dining in — a lovely and rare gesture that made us feel truly appreciated.

To our surprise, just as we were about to leave, Miss Nabila came out and personally handed us a box of home-baked red velvet cake as a thank-you gift. It was such a sweet and thoughtful gesture that left a lasting impression.

We left Abezi not just with full bellies, but with full hearts. Thank you for the wonderful experience — we’ll definitely be coming back! ❤️

You select any drink you like (I picked honeydew latte) and can refill the sambal & rice (unlimited). But chicken was small but still taste good. Just go to the counter and say you want to refill. Also, you have to order at the order & pick it up once they call your order number. After you finished,  you have to clean your table. Overall, it was nice experience for me and I'd suggest to dine in because you can eat while the food is still fresh/hot.

Update: I went here again twice with my friends for iftar (during ramadan). You have to walk in for booking if you wanna have iftar there. It's best to come early, around 5-6 pm to book & order there. You can reserve afterwards (subject to number of reservations too). Taste good as usual. I'd repeat this again.

Name: The Best Nasi Dagang Tuan Norlida Viral Sedap
Review_Text:
A laidback kinda stall located in a kampung area. Set up in her house compound. The owner is friendly . Locals eatery joint . Not overly crowded . I liked less viral joints which usually are superb. No frails but quality food !

Their speciality is nasi dagang. They served  two type of nasi dagang ; nasi dagang  mas ( consist of 5 types of rice ) & nasi dagang biasa. The nasi dagang are riched & tasty . Cooked accordingly & I can just eat the rice by otself without the kuah gulai ikan tongkol. The curry aka gulai ikan tongkol gravy are tick & full of spices. Sedap dooh !

Beside nasi dagang they also served nasi kerabu . Side dishes lemak bakar , ayam bakar . Try their Kayu Kamat ( it is a Ganu kinda chakoi) on the sweet side . Tasty. I ate with the gulai ikan tongkol .yummy !

So each time I come to Terengganu , I will make a pitstop here for my nasi dagang fix.

Food : 10/10

The rest area is far from the highway main road, with a very confusing entrance and exit layout.

What should be a quick stop turns into a long detour.
Not friendly at all for first-time visitors or night drivers. Avoid if possible.

Name: Ombok Cherating Surf Cafe
Review_Text:
We went here for an early breakfast and arrived about 20 minutes before opening, since it usually gets really crowded by midday from what I’ve seen.

There was only one breakfast menu available, which was a bit of a letdown. The options were limited to a Big Breakfast, an omelette, and a beef bacon and egg sandwich. We ordered all three dishes, along with a latte, a mango smoothie, and of course, the mango cheesecake.

The Big Breakfast, which is apparently one of their best sellers, was just okay, nothing special. The sandwich was decent and definitely better than the Big Breakfast. The poke omelette was very average as well. The only thing that stood out, and the main reason I’d consider visiting again, was the mango cheesecake.
Cleaned_Text: went early breakfast arrived minutes opening since usually gets really crowded midday i’ve seen one breakfast menu available bit letdown options limited big breakfast omelette beef bacon egg sandwich ordered three dishes along latte mango smoothie course mango cheesecake big breakfast apparently one best sellers okay nothing special sandwich decent definitely better big breakfast poke omelette average well thing stood main reason i’d consider visiting mango cheesecake

Name: Ombok Cherating Surf Cafe
Review_Text:
Ombok is located directly opposite Cafe Marion and definitely bigger and more spacious. We walked over and asked if we could bring over the desserts - and we will order drinks from them. They said OK 😉

A charming hipster cafe run by surfers, serving both local and Western style food.. it feels right at home with its cool and relaxing atmosphere.

◾Mango cheesecake - the famous Mango cheesecake by Kula Cakes was available here and we didn't hesitate to order one to eat.
◾Coffee and matcha while not the best around, brought much satisfaction in that afternoon.
◾Others - kombucha and orange juice

Not much of cafes in Cherating area, and Ombok is one of them. We proceed to Club Med for our vacation once everyone arrive.

Name: Ombok Cherating Surf Cafe
Review_Text:
I really disappointed with this place. None is good.

1) Waited for nearly 30 minutes as it full house but luckily they provide indoor waiting area with aircond.

2) Need to order at the counter. So we have to collect everyone's order and proceed to the counter.

3) Payment method is only QR and cash.

4) Service is quite slow. Waited for nearly 30 minutes for our sandwiches. While the rest has finished their foods.

5) Foods verdict. Bland. We ordered:-
- carbonara chicken: served with minced chicken. The carbonara itself not thick so its nice! But the minced chicken is a no no for carbonara.
- meatball bolognese: the meatball missed the memo. They are absent from the plate.
- Fish n chip. A normal fish n chip with 1 fillet of fish.
- monsoon beef burger: its so basic
- fish sandwich: waited nearly 30 minutes for just a simple sandwich. No side dish. Taste bland.
- chicken sandwich: same a above, but came with fries as sides.
- matcha: so blanddddd no taste of the 'grass' from matcha. Only the milk
- other drinks: bland. Need to add syrup (there is self service counter where they have syrup but it is so watery)

Overall, not recommended unless they improve their foods.

Nasi Dagang was amazingly delicious. Fried Chicken too had a very unique flavour. The sambal on the nasi lemak was sweet and smoky. Overall the food was great and it worth to come back again.

Price was very affordable with a total of RM18.50 for 2 boxes of nasi dagang and 1 box of nasi lemak ayam.

The people in the restaurant were super friendly and nice.

Great lunch spot with a sea view

We had the Mee Rebus Udang – it came with 5 fresh prawns and had a simple, comforting taste. The Nasi Ikan Keli was also enjoyable; the sauce paired well with the fish.
Overall, a good spot for a laid-back meal with a view.

Name: The Glaze By Hebak
Review_Text:
A great place to hang out with friends and family. The grilled chicken chop I had was delicious, the taste was quite unique. The creamy tom yum pasta was great as well. However,  it could've been spicier and had more tom yum taste as the creaminess overwhelmed the pasta. The milky strawberry was more like Dutch Lady strawberry flavoured milk with some strawberry sauce. They served multiple western food and nasi lemak but unfortunately there were no stock of nasi lemak when I was there.

The service was fast as I was one of the first customers who came after it opened. The seatings concept were outdoors with beautiful and dim lightings surrounding the area. For muslims, there was a mosque in front of the shop.

Name: Port Kopi Kaki Bukit Kg Geliga Pantai
Review_Text:
Here's a review for Port Kopi Kaki Bukit, incorporating your feedback:
A Seaside Gem for Coffee and Nasi Goreng Lovers!
Port Kopi Kaki Bukit is an absolute delight! This charming spot offers the perfect combination of delicious food, great coffee, and an unbeatable seaview.

I was incredibly impressed with both the coffee and the fried rice (nasi goreng). The coffee was expertly brewed, providing a fantastic pick-me-up, while the nasi goreng was flavorful and cooked to perfection, topped with a beautifully fried egg.

Dining here while gazing out at the sea was such a relaxing experience. Port Kopi Kaki Bukit is the place to go!
Cleaned_Text: heres review port kopi kaki bukit incorporating feedback seaside gem coffee nasi goreng lovers port kopi kaki bukit absolute delight charming spot offers perfect combination delicious food great coffee unbeatable seaview incredibly impressed coffee fried rice nasi goreng coffee expertly brewed providing fantastic pickmeup nasi goreng flavorful cooked perfection topped beautifully fried egg dining gazing sea relaxing experience port kopi kaki bukit place go

Name: Port Kopi Kaki Bukit Kg Geliga Pantai
Review_Text: The scenery at this place is really lovely, making it a great spot to relax. The Spanish latte was quite nice, so I’d give it an 8/10. However, the food could use a bit of improvement. The chocolate waffle had a chocolate topping that was a bit too runny and oily for my taste. The cheesy meatballs were on the dry side and a bit too salty, and the portion size felt a little small for the price. The service, however, was excellent – kudos to the staff for being attentive and friendly. Overall, it’s a great place to hang out, but I hope they can tweak the food a bit in the future. I’d give it a 5/10 for now.
Cleaned_Text: scenery place really lovely making great spot relax spanish latte quite nice i’d give however food could use bit improvement chocolate waffle chocolate topping bit runny oily taste cheesy meatballs dry side bit salty portion size felt little small price service however excellent – kudos staff attentive friendly overall it’s great place hang hope tweak food bit future i’d give

Name: Club Med Cherating Rembulan Restaurant
Review_Text:
If you’re at Cherating, you MUST visit this restaurant. The food here (both lunch & dinner) is really good! For dinner, you will need to make a reservation in advance (one day before) or else you’ll be put on a waiting list but it’s not guaranteed that you’ll get a table.

We’re lucky enough to bump into their F&B manager and requested for a table and voila! We managed to get it. We were seated at the upper deck and get to enjoy the night view and away from the crowd. (FYI, Just like Mutiara restaurant, you don’t have to pay additional fees to dine here, unless you’re going for their premium dinner menu.)

For starters, we ordered Dukkha Coated Tuna (the Tuna is really good, slightly salty but really chewy and nice) and Seafood Vege Tempura (typical tempura, can’t go wrong).

For mains, we had Australian Beef Tenderloin and Grilled Sea Bass Fillet. Both are equally good but the sea bass was much better. The Tom yum risotto was on point and the portion is just nice!

Lastly, for desserts, we had Pandan Panna Cotta and Earl Grey Indulgence. The Earl Grey one was a bit weird and I wouldn’t recommend it but the Pandan Panna Cotta is really, really good. It’s soft and springy and everything just melts in your mouth. All in all, had a great and filling dinner here!!

Name: Resorts World Kijal
Review_Text:
This is an old resort (over 30 years), but to be fair, it’s still decently well maintained. You can tell there have been efforts to refresh the place — the rooms have been repainted (even the bathrooms), and the furniture and fixtures look refurbished. Not brand new, but not run-down either.

That said, age does show in certain areas. The window areas were quite dirty — you can clearly see dust and dirt stuck in the gaps and crevices, and the curtains also looked overdue for a proper clean. The bathroom water heater was another issue — hot water was inconsistent, and at certain times of the day, there was none at all.

The road leading to the resort and the parking areas need attention too. There are potholes.

Breakfast was fine — no complaints there. Reasonable spread, a mix of local Malaysian dishes with some Western options, and there’s an egg station which is always a plus.

Despite all that, my family and I genuinely enjoyed our stay. The resort itself is huge, and the architecture is beautiful. It has a very traditional Terengganu / Malay-inspired design — high timber roofs, open spaces, lots of natural light, and good airflow. The lobby especially is impressive: very spacious, very high ceilings, and completely open-air. No air-conditioning needed, minimal artificial lighting — just breeze, light, and space. The moment you step in, you instantly feel more relaxed. And at night, the place looks and feels almost magical.

The swimming pool is big and nicely segmented. There’s a proper kids’ area with a mini water park, which is great for families. The overall compound is clean, the grass is well maintained, and the resort grounds are pleasant to walk around.

The gym, however, looks quite old and dated. Also worth noting: this is a Muslim-friendly resort — no alcohol and no live band. That also means nights can feel very quiet, even boring, as there’s really nothing much to do after dinner.

Overall, despite the setbacks, I’d still give this place 3.5 stars. It’s somewhere I would come back to if I just want to unwind, slow down, and relax.

Now the saddest part… the beach.

Everything inside the resort compound is clean and nicely maintained — and then you step onto the beach.

It was heartbreaking 💔

The amount of trash was shocking. Plastic bottles, paper bags, shampoo bottles, cigarette butts — everywhere. It’s not just a bit dirty. It’s sad, angry, and frustrating kind of dirty.

Come on, Malaysia… is this who we are?
Such a beautiful coastline, wasted like this.

It really hurt to see. If I wasn’t on holiday, I would have went on a solo beach cleanup myself. Other developing countries have stunning beaches and they protect them. They take pride in them. Why can’t we?

This isn’t just about the resort — it’s about us. And it’s honestly very, very sad to see this happening to our country

Name: Resorts World Kijal
Review_Text:
Subject: Complaint regarding premature closure of Studio One facilities

Dear Resort World Management,

I am writing to express my disappointment and frustration regarding the premature closure of your facilities at Studio One on 29 Dec 2025 at 10:35pm. Despite the clear signage indicating operating hours of 4pm till 11pm, your staff shut down the facilities while there were still over 10 customers enjoying the karaoke and pool table games.

When I brought this matter to the attention of the staff at Hanan Cafe, who manage Studio One, the manager appeared to be unresponsive and ignored my concerns, prioritizing closing activities instead.

Name: D&G Kijal Celup Tepung
Review_Text:
If you’re looking for an authentic East Coast snack experience, D&G Kijal Celup Tepung at Pantai Penunjuk Kijal is definitely worth a visit.

Their menu truly reflects the local flavours of Terengganu. I tried the Sotong Celup Tepung, and it was excellent — fresh squid coated in a light, crispy batter that wasn’t oily, just nicely golden and satisfying. You can really taste the freshness of the seafood. The Keropok Lekor was equally enjoyable, chewy on the inside with a perfect fried texture, just like the traditional ones you’d expect on the East Coast.

The combination of good food and the relaxing beach atmosphere makes the experience even better. A great spot to enjoy local favourites while taking in the sea breeze.

Highly recommended for anyone craving authentic celup tepung and classic Terengganu snacks!

Name: Intan Seafood Restaurant
Review_Text:
Went to Intan Seafood around 6:45–6:55 PM and got all our food and drinks by 7:15 PM — not bad on timing.

Overall, the food was okay. I’d personally rate it 3.5/5, but my husband gives it a solid 4/5 — mainly because of the Lala, which was hands down the champion of the night!

Here’s a quick breakdown of what we had:

Salted Egg Squid – Wouldn’t recommend this one. It tasted more like telur dadar than salted egg. Didn’t have that rich salted egg flavor at all.

Butter Prawns – Big-sized prawns, definitely worth the price and not tough at all. But the taste was a bit off; seems like they haven’t quite nailed the proper butter prawn flavor.

Fish – Sedap! The fish itself was fresh and cooked well. The gravy was just okay.

Lala (Clams) – The highlight! Full of flavor and perfectly cooked. This dish alone bumped up my husband’s rating.

Vegetable (Baby Kailan) – Pretty standard. Nothing special, but it did the job.

Total bill came to RM173.80 for two people. Not cheap, but fair considering the portions and seafood.

Would still come back to try other dishes — but will skip the salted egg squid next time!

Name: Cafila Cafe
Review_Text:
Let's  start with the positive things about this cafe. It looked clean, tidy, and organised. The seatings were adequate and not too close to other tables. The staff were friendly and quick to respond.

Now food. The presentation, the colours of the food were fantastic. They looked super delicious. But when it's the taste part, it's actually not. The masak lemak cili api on its own was okay. It was creamy and not spicy at all. But when it was cooked with udang galah in it, the kuah tasted weird. The udang galah were soggy and squishy. They were most definitely not fresh.

And we also asked for specific food to be spicy or crispy. Unfortunately, the food was not prepared according to our request.

The tom yam was sweet. The fried chicken was okay, but the fried talapia was soggy. The somtam itself didn't have any crunch to it. It was soggy. We were looking for salted fish taste from the kailan ikan masin, but we didn't find any. The ikan masin taste was too mild. And the cendawan goreng lacked flavours.

The only 3 items that we love from the things that we ordered were the Iced Yam 3L, the daging harimau menangis, and the complimentary budu. The yam drink was good and satisfying. The daging harimau menangis was flavourful and tender but a bit gamey for my liking. The budu was superb.

I don't think my friends and I will return to this establishment in the future.
Cleaned_Text: lets start positive things cafe looked clean tidy organised seatings adequate close tables staff friendly quick respond food presentation colours food fantastic looked super delicious taste part actually masak lemak cili api okay creamy spicy cooked udang galah kuah tasted weird udang galah soggy squishy definitely fresh also asked specific food spicy crispy unfortunately food prepared according request tom yam sweet fried chicken okay fried talapia soggy somtam itself didnt crunch soggy looking salted fish taste kailan ikan masin didnt find ikan masin taste mild cendawan goreng lacked flavours items love things ordered iced yam l daging harimau menangis complimentary budu yam drink good satisfying daging harimau menangis flavourful tender bit gamey liking budu superb dont think friends return establishment future

Name: Cherating Steakhouse Restaurant
Review_Text:
Place: this is sherwood 2.0 with the exact same menu, and cooking style. Layout is slightly more gothic here. Cleanliness is satisfactory.

Service: ok la. Nothing to shout about. But the waiter that served me knew a lot of the menu. Was patient in explaining to me.

Food:
1) Shrimp cocktail (RM18): loved the way they separate the dressing (which was a lot by the way!) And the long vegetables that allows for 1 to hand hold and dip. But, some slices are just too huge. The prawns were fresh despite being cold. The French dressing was PERFECT (sour side) and a great appetiser. Verdict: slightly smaller vege pieces and more prawns = perfection appetiser.

2) Pizza Hai Fungi (RM43): the branch here is more generous with their toppings. Loved it here. Verdict: value for money.

3) Grilled Chicken Chop (RM35): chicken was moist, but skin had a tinge of burnt smell. Sauce (rosemary) was a let down. Again, salad served here were all in huge chuncks. Verdict: needs improvement.

4) Ice Lemon tea (RM4.50): just 1 word for this...TOOSWEET!! verdict: diabetes inducing. Order "kurang manis" please.

Name: Sara Thai Kitchen Balok
Review_Text:

- siakap stim limau
- tomyam campur
- ayam goreng sos ikan
- ayam pad kha prow
- sotong goreng tepung
- kailan ikan masin
- kangkung sos tiram

Kerapu bakar isn't charred at all. They roast it in aluminium foils then put the sambal on top. But sambal was good.

Sotong goreng also. There is a lot of flour but the squid is fresh, not rubbery. Even if there's a lot of flour, it's still flavourful and good.

Both the chickens are really good. Recommended.

Name: Sara Thai Kitchen Balok
Review_Text:
It was 3 pm after our batik painting activity ended at the nearby Natural Batik Village, and we were famished, even though we had a late breakfast. A quick search online pointed us to Sara Thai Kitchen Balok, a Thai restaurant located just a few hundred meters away.

Unsurprisingly, the restaurant was completely empty when we reached it. We were worried that their kitchen might be closed at this hour, but thankfully, they were still serving. As we sat down and studied the menu, another family also walked in for a late lunch. The dining hall was a roofed, open air area, and it was well ventilated. Even though it was a hot afternoon, we felt quite comfortable dining here.

As usual for a Thai restaurant, we ordered Seafood Tom Yum. The soup was thick and savoury, the squid was tasty, but the prawns were a little mushy. Overall, it was adequate, and nothing to shout about.

I enjoy salted egg dishes, and the Salted Egg Squid did not disappoint. The squid was covered in fragrant salted egg yolk sauce and cooked just right. It was firm but tender, and not chewy at all.

To complete the meal, we also ordered two more dishes: Mixed Vegetables and Mixed Mushrooms. Both came with flavourful gravy and large prawns, and these prawns tasted better and not mushy.

Overall, the portion sizes of the dishes were just right, and everything tasted great. We left the place feeling completely satiated. The price was also surprisingly reasonable. This is a place I'd recommend others to try.
Cleaned_Text: pm batik painting activity ended nearby natural batik village famished even though late breakfast quick search online pointed us sara thai kitchen balok thai restaurant located hundred meters away unsurprisingly restaurant completely empty reached worried kitchen might closed hour thankfully still serving sat studied menu another family also walked late lunch dining hall roofed open air area well ventilated even though hot afternoon felt quite comfortable dining usual thai restaurant ordered seafood tom yum soup thick savoury squid tasty prawns little mushy overall adequate nothing shout enjoy salted egg dishes salted egg squid disappoint squid covered fragrant salted egg yolk sauce cooked right firm tender chewy complete meal also ordered two dishes mixed vegetables mixed mushrooms came flavourful gravy large prawns prawns tasted better mushy overall portion sizes dishes right everything tasted great left place feeling completely satiated price also surprisingly reasonable place id recommend others try

Review_Text:
I really love the food and atmosphere of this place! I ordered the fish and chips and my partner ordered the chicken chop, everything tasted so good!
For the fish and chips, the serving portion of the fish was huge and perfectly battered, not overly floured and the tartare sauce didn’t taste of cheap mayonnaise! The sauce itself had pickles chopped up inside and it tasted like something you’d get from a 5 star restaurant in KL 👍👍
The grilled chicken chop was amazing too! I’m a black pepper sauce hater and their sauce was so well made so it says something about the chef! The chicken was thick and juicy as well as the mashed potatoes actually tasting of butter 🤩🤩

11/10, I wish I could rate 6 stars here but 5 is the maximum 😔

Bonus points, the cats here are super manja and fluffy, especially the black one. However the orange cat does get jealous if you pet other cats haha

The breeze is lovely and refreshing.angin sepoi-sepoi gitew and the prices are very reasonable for such a stunning location. They sometimes even have live music/band performances on weekends or public holidays, which is a great bonus. Parking is also easy! A must-visit spot in Kuantan.

Name: Seaba Seafood
Review_Text:
Strategic location, outside the city, facing the sloping beach and ample parking space.

Spacious dining area, there are many dining table sets, either under the roof or open. The decoration of the dining area is attractive and suitable for OOTD.

The purchase section based on flour dip, drinks, rice and others separately is very good because it can avoid congestion in one place.

In terms of food and drinks, it is very tasty, varied and appetizing.
The fish, squid and flour dip shrimp are very tasty and sweet. However, the prices are quite expensive and sometimes a bit illogical.

Overall, I give it 5 stars.

Name: Seaba Seafood
Review_Text:
On December 14, we ordered takeaway seafood from this restaurant while visiting Kuantan. The order included two fish, one squid, several prawns, and two crabs.

Before leaving, we confirmed with the staff whether all items had been packed, and we were clearly told that they were.

Unfortunately, when we returned to our accommodation, we found that the two fish were missing from the takeaway order.

This experience was extremely disappointing and left us with serious concerns about the restaurant’s honesty and service standards.

Name: Seaba Seafood
Review_Text:
Operation hours are stated as 12:00 PM, but at that time they were only finishing a briefing with the kitchen crew, and there was no food available at the counter. Customers had to wait another 30 minutes for the food to be cooked, and the queue was already long. On top of that, the waiting time at the drink counter was more than 45 minutes.

Price-wise, it is quite expensive compared to the Terengganu side. The celup tepung batter was okay, but the sauce was not that good, and they only gave a very small amount. The regular drink cup was also disappointing because it was filled with crushed ice and I could finish it in just two sips. They also did not provide a fork, spoon, or even a plate, so we only got a talam and lidi cucuk.

the most important comment i have to make:

please open an outlet in KL 😭🙏🏻

Name: Jomo Eatery Kuala Nerus
Review_Text:
⭐ JOMO Eatery Review (Harimanada Oyster Experience) ⭐
After a few missed chances, I finally got to try JOMO Eatery — and it was worth the wait.

Despite the place being packed, we were seated quickly and warmly welcomed. The staff were attentive, friendly, and helpful throughout our visit.

Yes, the pricing leans toward the premium side, but the portion sizes do justify it — especially the generous lamb shoulder and towering Korean burger.

🍴 Food Highlights:

Harimanada Oysters (10pcs) – The star of the night. Fat, juicy, and creamy with a briny sea burst. Topped with sea grapes, which adds a salty pop. Slightly salty by nature, but you may request a light rinse if preferred. Rare to find this quality in Terengganu — definitely recommended for oyster lovers.

Grilled Lamb Shoulder – Thick, juicy cuts marinated to mask gaminess. Paired with mashed potatoes (a tad salty) and vibrant veggies. Nicely presented.

Salted Egg Prawn Pasta – Sauce was balanced and flavorful. Tempura prawns were crispy but would be better with a lighter batter or soft-shell variety.

Crispy Korean Chicken Burger – Large portion. Flavor was decent, though the fried coating was thick and slightly salty. Spicy sauce may not be for everyone.

Creamy Garlic Mushroom Soup – Homey, rich, and satisfying.

Sides & Drinks – Well presented, though nothing stood out in particular.

📝 Verdict:

Atmosphere ✔️
Service ✔️
Presentation ✔️
Taste 👍 (with minor room for tuning)

🧾 Total: RM325.98 (incl. 6% SST & 6% service charge)

Would return again, especially for the oysters. A great spot for those looking to splurge on quality seafood in Terengganu.
Cleaned_Text: ⭐ jomo eatery review harimanada oyster experience ⭐ missed chances finally got try jomo eatery — worth wait despite place packed seated quickly warmly welcomed staff attentive friendly helpful throughout visit yes pricing leans toward premium side portion sizes justify — especially generous lamb shoulder towering korean burger 🍴 food highlights harimanada oysters pcs – star night fat juicy creamy briny sea burst topped sea grapes adds salty pop slightly salty nature may request light rinse preferred rare find quality terengganu — definitely recommended oyster lovers grilled lamb shoulder – thick juicy cuts marinated mask gaminess paired mashed potatoes tad salty vibrant veggies nicely presented salted egg prawn pasta – sauce balanced flavorful tempura prawns crispy would better lighter batter softshell variety crispy korean chicken burger – large portion flavor decent though fried coating thick slightly salty spicy sauce may everyone creamy garlic mushroom soup – homey rich satisfying sides drinks – well presented though nothing stood particular 📝 verdict atmosphere ✔️ service ✔️ presentation ✔️ taste 👍 minor room tuning 🧾 total rm incl sst service charge would return especially oysters great spot looking splurge quality seafood terengganu

Review_Text: I absolutely love this place 💗 !! The service was tiptop ! Came in with friends which was around 6 pax, they have one table for a group of friends. The ambience was nice and the food was nice. It's a bit on the pricey side as food was rm20+ per person and that's without drinks. We all ordered glasses of water but they gave us a 2 room temperature bottle which was a win for us! The pastries were free haha as it was a compensation for accidentally busting a shaker on us but we were fine but they gave us 2 desserts which I believe were the lemon tart and the Pavlova. Both are good but the lemon tart is suitable for someone who loves sour dessert. Don't get me talking about the food ! Even tho it's a bit pricey, the portion was big big !! We ordered the nasi lemak ayam berempah i think? (?) The chicken was big and the side dishes were so fulfilling. Then the salted egg chicken was cooked to perfection ! A big portion too. The pesto spaghetti doesn't taste too earthy haha which was good and it was a balance of spinach and milky. It's fulfilling too. Will definitely come again ! Thanks Jomo for the food! 👍🏻

Honestly, this one not really penyet. The chicken came as crispy fried chicken, not smashed at all. The chicken was okay la, well-cooked, but a bit dry for crispy chicken. Portion of tauhu was very little, and they even put long beans .. which is quite unusual for nasi penyet. But I really liked the soup. It’s not the usual plain bland soup; this one had strong chicken flavour with sweet spices, very unique. For me, the only mistake is the chicken… it felt more like nasi ayam crispy than ayam penyet.

Nasi Ayam Buttermilk (Signature)
Chicken cooked well, batter thick. But the taste was too heavy on the butter. No hint of curry or sweet flavour like some buttermilk chicken I’ve tried before. It was just salty and buttery all the way. Portion size was big though, so can be filling.

Tiramisu
This one not so impressive. The biscuit layer was mushy and a bit dry at the same time … macam over-soaked but not in a nice way. Coffee taste also hilang, almost not there. Cream was heavy and lacked the slight tang you usually get in tiramisu. Presentation was okay since it came in a cup, but flavour wise, can improve a lot.

Thats all

Name: The Nelang Cafe
Review_Text:
A Culinary Adventure at The Nelang Cafe

I recently savored the delights of The Nelang Cafe, and the experience was nothing short of delightful. The cozy ambiance, swift service, and standout dishes, particularly the Lamb Chop & Oglio Olio Seafood, left an indelible mark on my visit. Not to be overlooked is the RM4.90 Crispy Fried Chicken, which surpasses Colonel Sanders's KFC in size, taste, juiciness, and tenderness.

The fried chicken was a revelation—crispy on the outside, revealing a juicy and tender interior with every delectable bite.

In addition, I indulged in their specialty coffee paired with a delightful pastry. The exceptional flavors and appealing presentation added to the overall charm. The friendly and attentive staff heightened the welcoming atmosphere, and the cafe's unique decor showcased a meticulously curated space.

With reasonable prices and a pleasant vibe, The Nelang Cafe in Gong Badak is a must-visit for those seeking a charming spot to relish good coffee and delectable treats.

Name: The Nelang Cafe
Review_Text:
This is probably the longest review I've ever written!

My family and I visited Terengganu for the first time and asked someone nearby for restaurant or cafe recommendations. Out of the options, we decided to try Nelang Cafe as it was very close to our homestay. When we arrived, the cafe was quite crowded, and there wasn't enough space for our group (there were 12 of us). Just as we were about to leave for another cafe, a staff member (or possibly the owner) approached us and kindly found us a place to sit. Their customer service was excellent!

Once we settled, we ordered our food. Half of our group were vegetarians, and we were pleasantly surprised that they cooked vegetarian dishes upon request, which we truly appreciated. As for the rest of us, we ordered one of their family sets, and it was absolutely delicious. We're Indian, and we wanted to switch the "daging pad krapao" in the set to chicken instead, and they were more than happy to accommodate our request.

The price was reasonable—not too cheap, but not too expensive either. The restaurant setting was lovely, and we especially liked the spot where we sat. Overall, the food was great, the ambience was nice, and the service was even better!

Name: Hr Mee Champa Gong Badak
Review_Text:
If you are looking for a hearty bowl of noodles that soothes the soul and satisfies every craving, Mee Champa is definitely worth a visit, especially for those in the Gong Badak, Kuala Nerus area. The interior offers a cozy, rustic vibe with wooden ceilings, windows, and tables that add warmth and character to the space. The lighting is soft yet bright enough to clearly see your meal, and the overall cleanliness of the restaurant is commendable. It feels inviting and is perfect for both quick solo meals and casual group dining. Parking is surprisingly convenient, with a decent amount of space available right outside the restaurant, no need to circle the block or walk far, which is a big plus. Service is prompt and polite, with friendly staff who are accommodating and ready to offer recommendations, especially if it’s your first time. Food arrives quickly after ordering, adding to the smooth dining experience.

The bowl is generously filled with sliced beef, tripe, fresh herbs, and a beautifully clear, rich, and savory broth. The flavors are deep and comforting, with each sip of the broth delivering a satisfying warmth. The beef slices are tender and juicy, while the tripe adds a pleasant texture. The noodles are silky, springy, and absorb the flavors of the broth beautifully. Mee Champa serves a bowl that goes beyond just feeding your hunger, it feels like a hug in a bowl. Whether you are craving comfort food on a rainy day or simply want a flavorful meal paired with excellent service, this spot delivers on all fronts.
Cleaned_Text: looking hearty bowl noodles soothes soul satisfies every craving mee champa definitely worth visit especially gong badak kuala nerus area interior offers cozy rustic vibe wooden ceilings windows tables add warmth character space lighting soft yet bright enough clearly see meal overall cleanliness restaurant commendable feels inviting perfect quick solo meals casual group dining parking surprisingly convenient decent amount space available right outside restaurant need circle block walk far big plus service prompt polite friendly staff accommodating ready offer recommendations especially it’s first time food arrives quickly ordering adding smooth dining experience bowl generously filled sliced beef tripe fresh herbs beautifully clear rich savory broth flavors deep comforting sip broth delivering satisfying warmth beef slices tender juicy tripe adds pleasant texture noodles silky springy absorb flavors broth beautifully mee champa serves bowl goes beyond feeding hunger feels like hug bowl whether craving comfort food rainy day simply want flavorful meal paired excellent service spot delivers fronts

Name: Warung Nasi Dagang Aroma Hz
Review_Text:
Language

Download PDF

Watch

Edit

Nasi dagang (Jawi: ناسي داڬڠ‎, lit. 'trader's rice') is a Malaysian dish consisting of rice steamed in coconut milk, fish curry and extra ingredients such as pickled cucumber and carrots.[2]

It is a breakfast food in the states on the East Coast of Peninsular Malaysia (Terengganu, Kelantan, parts of Pahang and eastern Johor), southern Thai Malay provinces of Pattani, Yala and Narathiwat and across the Indonesian border in Natuna[3][4] and Anambas,[5] Riau Islands.

Nasi (rice)

edit

The combination of fenugreek seeds and coconut milk gives nasi dagang its flavour and fragrance. The rice may first be soaked in water for several hours to soften it. It is then mixed with thick coconut milk, sliced shallots, lemongrass and fenugreek seeds. The rice is steamed until cooked. It may also be steamed twice, where more coconut milk is added when it is half-cooked. Then the rice is steamed again until cooked. This method ensures a more creamy finish to the rice.

Kari ikan (fish curry)

edit

This accompanying dish is only specially prepared for nasi dagang and is sometimes locally called gulai darat.

This curry the fish is cooked in is not an Indian-style curry powder but a Malay-style curry, i.e., coconut milk mixed with traditional Malay spices such as lemon grass, galangal, chilli paste, and turmeric.

Tuna is the standard choice of fish but other fish can be used as well, such as tenggiri.[6] Chicken and prawns are also used sometimes; however, the gulai darat is prepared slightly differently.

Kelapa goreng (fried coconut)

edit

Kelapa Parut is freshly shaved, mixed with sliced shallots and fried until golden brown.

Hard-boiled eggs

edit

Hard boiled eggs are cut into four or eight slices.

Vegetable pickle

edit

The vegetable is pickled in rice vinegar and sugar. The vegetables commonly used are cucumber, chilli and carrots.

Sambal

edit

Chilli sambal can sometimes be included

Name: Kedai Makan Wan Sabil
Review_Text:
This is the place to tapau your breakfast.

They have tons of choices, be it nasi goreng, mee goreng or pulut ikan masin.

My suggestion is the pulut ikan masin which costs only Rm 1.50 (june 2024). The pulut is awesome, topped with fresh grated coconut and eaten with ikan masin talang. Over in KL, this dish could easily set you back Rm 4.00 or above.

Mee goreng cost you Rm 1.00 and nasi pataya Rm 3.50.

Mind you the portion is just nice for a person to enjoy multiple breakfast treats.

Love the place for its deliciousness of its dishes and ultimately the price of their dishes.

Great place 👍

Name: Asak Smokehouse
Review_Text:
At the time of posting, they offer burgers, spaghetti, grilled meat, and smoked beef.

We ordered spaghetti of carbonara variant and Tom yam carbonara variant.
Carbonara is a tad salty but if you look at the presentation and the taste I would say it is worth it. You can taste the milky texture of the sauce, great carbonara. Tom yam Carbonara is in a league of its own as it have that slight carbonara presence while the crab meat rich flavour presented itself strongly in it but it can be too much to finish it.
The smoked meat is nice, I couldn’t give much of an opinion other than what came with the meat itself, sauce is probably standard black pepper, tartar sauce and thai sauce. The mashed potato that comes with it is great, never thought egg mixed with it would be that good. Coleslaw is great, reminds me of Texas Chicken’s one.

Parking aplenty by the side of the road, I hope reviewers come to this place so they can thrive and become a brand of their own.

Name: Mcdonald'S Gong Badak Dt
Review_Text: Overall, Gong Badak's McDonald's is quite amazing in its own right, being the largest McDonald's restaurant in Terengganu. Its strategic location being close to UniSZA and UMT has improved the volume of visits, both for takeaways and dine in. Seatings are ample and well managed. However, due to the pandemic, customers aren't allowed to fully utilize the available sits to maintain physical distancing. The SOPs are carefully maintained at this establishment, there will always be a staff securing access control to the restaurant. Could've been better though if the traffic isn't so weird, during peak hours cars would line up to go for the drive-through but for those who want to dine in, they're not able to park their cars because the line prohibits them from accessing the parking lots. They have to line up too before being able to pull up into the parking lot.
