Name: Nyiur Pantai Sauh
Review_Text:
This café is honestly a dream spot. It’s right by the beach with a wide green lawn, coconut trees, and a cute little cottage-style café. Inside there are just a few small tables, plus some seating on the veranda, a small hut, and plenty of covered outdoor tables. You can even imagine bringing a camping chair to chill on the grass in the evening. The place feels super comfortable and cozy with amazing vibes.
The staff are warm and welcoming, which makes it even better. I had a latte — the coffee was exactly my taste, roasted just right and really good. They also serve food; I tried some kuih and roti jala, both tasty and affordable. Perfect spot for an evening coffee and chill session.
Hidden just a bit off the main road by the beach — seriously, such a beautiful place ✨☕🌴feels like i paid for the coffee with my soul, not my bank account 😂❤️

Name: Nyiur Pantai Sauh
Review_Text:
DEFINITELY A MUST GO PLACE IN TERENGGANU FOR A BEACH VIBE
Located at Pantai Sauh, just 20-30 min from Kuala Terengganu's city centre.. we came during the monsoon season though but the weather was pleasant and calming. The place is where u can find calmness, tranquility and peace. We chosed to picnic on the grass, while enjoying the sun and the comforting sea breeze..many coconut trees around, eventhough in the afternoon, the place was shaded and we really enjoyed the open feel of the area.
Ok enough about the environment. The place definitely has its own charm, decorated with vintage, rustic malay traditional style of window and wood craft with a touch of abstract painting and some artsy stuff from the beach..the cafe itself is open and breezy enough, even they dont need the turn on the fan..
We ordered freshly baked brownies ( 10/10), it was well balanced and made with quality chocolates and ingredients. We also ordered some other cakes and coffee, it was satisfying. And we also ordered watermelon juice ( the star of the day). The juice was the blended type, so it was really refreshing, we ended ordering 4 glass of the watermelon juice.
The owner was there, and he was really friendly and helpful in suggesting places that we should go around Terengganu.
P/S this place has totally made an impression to us with the calmness, the breeziness, the food and the vibes..
WILL DEFINITELY COME AGAIN!

Name: Ict Kak Ani Celup Tepung
Review_Text: ICT Kak Ani Celup Tepung is a popular food stall in Kuala Nerus District, Terengganu, Malaysia. It serves a variety of deep-fried seafood and vegetables coated with crispy batter. The menu includes fish, squid, shrimp, mushroom, spinach, and more. The food is served hot and fresh, with a spicy and tangy chili sauce on the side. The portion is generous and the price is reasonable. The stall is located near the beach, so you can enjoy the scenic view and the breeze while eating. The staff is friendly and attentive, and the service is fast. ICT Kak Ani Celup Tepung is a great place to enjoy a delicious and satisfying snack or meal. I highly recommend it to anyone who loves fried food and seafood.

Name: On The Way Kopitiam - K.Terengganu
Review_Text: The food here is absolutely delicious with a true authentic taste — every dish feels carefully prepared and full of flavor. The ambience strikes a nice balance between cozy and lively, creating a great spot whether you’re catching up with friends, enjoying a casual date, or just treating yourself to good food. Overall, it’s a place that combines quality, good vibes, and excellent service — definitely worth coming back to.

Name: On The Way Kopitiam - K.Terengganu
Review_Text:
Came here for dinner with my friends during our visit to Kuala Trengganu. Overall, the food was tasty and enjoyable—while not particularly exciting or surprising, it was satisfying. Portion sizes were just right, and the prices were reasonable.

One of the standout dishes was the laksam—a local specialty that I personally recommend. However, it's quite unique in taste, and not everyone may enjoy it; in fact, only about 3 in 5 of us really liked it. Still, it's worth trying if you're open to new flavors.

Name: Ayam 7 Goreng
Review_Text: I can start my review with the title of “Great food needs patience”. So I ordered carbonara with chicken grill spaghetti and my drinks was simple Teh O Iced. The food definitely great and delicious. The carbonara sauce were seasoned beautifully and the black pepper sauce were amazing. I was expecting a bigger portion with the price of RM17.00 per plate yet it comes with a normal portion which is ok. But maybe my expectations were higher since the restaurant was all over my tiktok FYP. So I’m looking forward to have bigger portions as advertised but yeah it’s just normal. With that, I ordered the food around 8:05 PM and need to wait for around 45 minutes. Ridiculously long time of waiting and to add on the restaurant wasn’t that full, yet my food took almost an hour to prepare. The staff wasn’t that happy or welcoming. They look tired, maybe due to overwork or understaff, I’m not sure why. They didn’t even bother to wipe my table and only pickup the plates and the cups (my table wasn’t that dirty but have some spills and rice) until I asked them to do so. So that my experience, I really have high expectations but sadly it is not. But don’t worry, still the food was great. Should try, if you’re not having “iman senipis kulit bawang”.

Name: Azmi Sate Jam Besar
Review_Text:
Many might not agree with the five star ratings, given that this place offers nothing but satay.

But they never advertise themselves as anything else. And as far as their satay goes, that's exactly what they deserve.

Historically the household satay name in Kuala Terengganu, they went on a hiatus, only to reappear many years later, at the current location. Gladly, the originality remains the same.

Sate kajang afficionado might have problem with the skinnier skewer size, but this is how satay here in Terengganu is. I just find the skinnier satay to be more bite size, and the meat more evenly grilled. Love the taste, and the peanut sauce / kuah satay os done just right.

Another reason for the five stars - they actually serve the drinks kurang manis, as ordered. Have been having issues here woth the local gauge of sugary sweetness.
Cleaned_Text: many might agree five star ratings given place offers nothing satay never advertise themselves anything else far satay goes thats exactly deserve historically household satay name kuala terengganu went hiatus reappear many years later current location gladly originality remains sate kajang afficionado might problem skinnier skewer size satay terengganu find skinnier satay bite size meat evenly grilled love taste peanut sauce kuah satay os done right another reason five stars actually serve drinks kurang manis ordered issues woth local gauge sugary sweetness

Name: Ayam Gepuk Pak Gembus Gong Badak
Review_Text:
I am very disappointed and frustrated with my experience at this outlet. The staff were extremely slow and inefficient. I clearly said I wanted take away, but they still processed my order as dine in. On top of that, I ordered a different set meal which should have been around RM14, but what appeared on the receipt was over RM20 with add-ons that I never requested.

What made it worse was that the staff never apologized. After waiting a long time, when I asked about my order, the staff casually told me they just changed it to take away — with no sense of responsibility at all.

To add to my frustration, the drink I had on the table spilled everywhere after the plastic bag tipped over. Feeling embarrassed and upset, I politely asked if I could get a replacement drink. Shockingly, the staff — especially Fatimah — rudely said no.

Name: Ak Steamboat
Review_Text:
Saya tak expect lebih pun sebenarnya untuk kedai ni. Adik yang ajak makan sini dengan harapan google rating dan komen-komen  sebelum ni dah di tambah baik.

Ni adalah review jujur dari saya tanpa dipengaruhi oleh sesiapa:
1. Service slow - Staff yang bertugas slow. Kami dtg dan terpinga² nak tanya siapa samada perlu bayar dulu atau duduk di meja dulu. Staff ade yang nampak kami sedang mencari² tapi tiada yang sudi membantu. Tiada yang memantau samada kelengkapan pinggan atau mangkuk mengcukupi menyebabkan kami terpaksa menunggu mangkuk kecil di tambah.
2. Kebersihan - sangat teruk. Pinggan, sudu & garfu, senduk sangat kotor (masih ade kesan makanan). Saya sampai perlu pergi basuh almost semua sudu garfu senduk dan lain² di sinki dan lap balik semua pinggan dan sudu dan sebagainya. Nak cakap customer ramai masa saya hadir, memang x ramai sangat pun. Minta maaf cakap, klau kebersihan tidak di jaga, saya tak boleh tolerate. Please improve on the hygiene. Ni adalah basic dalam perniagaan dan penyediaan makanan kpd pelanggan.
3. Kedai berbau - tak pasti bau apa tapi kurang menyenangkan. Cuma tidak mengganggu kami sangat sebab kami duduk bahagian depan kedai. Cuma bila nak ke station makanan tu, akan terhidu bau kurang menyenangkan.
4. Makanan - ok la. Not too bad. Perapan ok as other perapan dekat kedai lain juga.
5. Nota tambahan - just for information yang kedai akan minta deposit sebelum makan sebanyak RM20 (in case ada pembaziran makanan). Yang ni kami faham dan xkisah pun sebab ni salah satu cara mendidik agar tidak membazirkan makanan. Rate kami adalah RM29.90 utk seorang dewasa (ada promo masa ni).

Overall, ini adalah first dan last saya ke sini.
Jangan salah faham kerana rating rendah yang diberikan. Saya memang banyak pun makan² di restaurant biasa² dan high end, tapi jika kebersihan tidak di titik berat, saya tiada kompromi.
Cleaned_Text: tak expect lebih sebenarnya kedai adik ajak makan sini harapan google rating komenkomen dah tambah baik review jujur tanpa dipengaruhi oleh sesiapa service slow staff bertugas slow dtg terpinga² nak tanya siapa samada perlu bayar duduk meja staff ade nampak sedang mencari² tiada sudi membantu tiada memantau samada kelengkapan pinggan mangkuk mengcukupi menyebabkan terpaksa menunggu mangkuk kecil tambah kebersihan sangat teruk pinggan sudu garfu senduk sangat kotor ade kesan makanan sampai perlu pergi basuh almost sudu garfu senduk lain² sinki lap balik pinggan sudu sebagainya nak cakap customer ramai masa hadir memang x ramai sangat minta maaf cakap klau kebersihan jaga tak tolerate please improve hygiene basic perniagaan penyediaan makanan kpd pelanggan kedai berbau tak pasti bau apa kurang menyenangkan cuma mengganggu sangat sebab duduk bahagian depan kedai cuma nak station makanan tu terhidu bau kurang menyenangkan makanan ok la bad perapan ok perapan dekat kedai lain nota tambahan information kedai minta deposit makan sebanyak rm case pembaziran makanan faham xkisah sebab salah satu cara mendidik agar membazirkan makanan rate rm utk seorang dewasa promo masa overall first last sini jangan salah faham rating rendah diberikan memang makan² restaurant biasa² high end kebersihan titik berat tiada kompromi

Name: The Taaras Beach & Spa Resort
Review_Text:
We stayed in the Cliff Bay Suite during our 4D3N stay and the room was absolutely spectacular. The view was stunning and truly one of the highlights of the trip.

A huge shoutout to the resort staff, the service was exceptional. Everyone genuinely went above and beyond at their respective stations, and you could really feel the warmth and dedication in their service.

We also did diving at the resort, and my partner completed her Open Water certification here. Our lead diver, Aiman, was incredibly patient, calm, and experienced throughout the entire process. From start to finish, he made the experience feel safe and enjoyable.

The only downside for us was the restaurant food. Given the premium pricing, we were expecting a higher standard in terms of taste and quality.

Overall, it was a wonderful experience, and we would definitely return mainly because of the amazing, friendly staff who truly made our stay memorable.

Name: The Taaras Beach & Spa Resort
Review_Text: My vacation at The Taaras was truly amazing and exceeded all expectations. The snorkeling experience was one of the highlights — swimming among beautiful corals and schools of small, colorful fish felt like being in a natural aquarium. The beach itself was stunning, with clean, soft white sand that was incredibly relaxing to walk on and enjoy. The overall atmosphere was peaceful and refreshing, making it the perfect place to unwind and connect with nature. I genuinely enjoyed every moment of my stay and would highly recommend The Taaras to anyone looking for a memorable island getaway.

Name: Terengganu State Museum
Review_Text:
such a hidden gem in kuala terengganu! and it’s wayyyyyyyyy more better than the one in kelantan. the architecture is absolutely beautiful with traditional malay vibes, and the whole place is super spacious and peaceful, perfect for a chill walk and some aesthetic photos.

there are many interesting galleries about local history, textiles, islamic art and traditional village life, so it’s not just pretty but also very educational. the environment is clean, calm, and family friendly, and the staff are friendly too. the ticket price is very affordable, which is a big plus.

some parts could be a bit more interactive, but overall it’s definitely worth a visit if you’re in kuala terengganu, especially if you love culture, heritage, and aesthetic spots!

p/s : ticket price for adult is RM5, children RM2 (malaysian citizens)

Name: Terengganu State Museum
Review_Text:
There are two types of tickets:
- One for the main museum galleries
- One for the outdoor exhibits (traditional houses, boats, etc.)
Each ticket is RM5 for adults, while children and OKU (persons with disabilities) enter for free—very thoughtful!

During our visit, several areas were closed for maintenance, including the traditional boat exhibit. While it was a bit disappointing, we understand the importance of upkeep to preserve these cultural treasures. Hopefully more areas will reopen soon!

The museum complex is designed in the style of traditional Malay houses—it’s like walking through a life-sized kampung postcard. The buildings themselves are part of the learning experience.

The museum grounds are stunning—lush greenery, traditional architecture, and serene vibes. Perfect for post-wedding photoshoots, graduation portraits, or even just a peaceful stroll with family.

The open-air layout and spacious grounds make it ideal for families. Kids can explore freely between pavilions, peek into traditional house interiors, and enjoy the outdoor exhibits—even if interactive screens are limited.

Also, The staff were welcoming and helpful, adding to the overall pleasant experience.

There’s a small shop selling books, handicrafts, and cultural souvenirs—great for picking up a memento or educational gift.

Facilities for OKU are available, including ramps and elevators, making the museum inclusive and easy to navigate.

Open daily from 9 AM to 5 PM, except Fridays. It’s best to plan your visit early to enjoy the full experience before closing time.

Suggestion
Regular updates on which sections are open would be helpful for visitors planning their trip. Maintenance is necessary, but a little notice goes a long way!

Name: Terengganu State Museum
Review_Text: The museum is very big and nicely air-conditioned! I came around 1pm, and it was such a relief to step inside and feel cool right away. The exhibitions are arranged by floors—on the ground floor there’s a focus on batik and songket, but I found it a bit confusing that many of the photos were from Johor instead of Terengganu. The upper floors had more on Terengganu’s history, which was amazing to see. Personally, I think the clothing exhibition would be better placed upstairs, as it felt a little boring on the lower level. Also, there were many replicas instead of original artifacts, and some items didn’t have clear information about their function or date of origin. That said, taking photos outside the museum is really beautiful—the building and surroundings are stunning!
